books.title: The Gate of the Feral Gods
The Eye of the Bedlam Bride

evidence.note: Li Na is the first crawler to max base stats; system announces the 300 cap

donut.label: Enhanced Growth buff (+1 STR/INT, +2 CHA per level)

evidence.quote: all additional levels will automatically grant +1 to Strength and Intelligence. Charisma will gain +2 per level. No additional stat points will be received upon level-up.

carl.label: Agent Provocateur specialty (+1 INT per level-up)

evidence.quote: An additional +1 to intelligence upon level-up
All patch stat point benefits are now doubled.

timeline.label: Stage 2 Of 4. Still On The Gnomes.
Stage 3 Of 4. The Mad Dune Mage
Stage 4 Of 4. The Necropolis Of Anser

boxers.name: Boxers
boxers.description: Plain boxer shorts Carl was wearing when the world collapsed.

leather-jacket.name: Leather Jacket
leather-jacket.description: Heavy leather jacket grabbed on the way out into the freezing night.

pink-crocs.name: Pink Crocs
pink-crocs.description: His ex-girlfriend's too-small pink Crocs — the only footwear available.

bronze-pet-box.name: Bronze Pet Box
bronze-pet-box.description: Unopened pet-themed loot box awarded for the Crazy Cat Lady achievement.

legendary-pet-box.name: Legendary Pet Box
legendary-pet-box.description: Unopened top-tier pet loot box from the first-cat-in-dungeon achievement.

silver-adventurer-box.name: Silver Adventurer Box
silver-adventurer-box.description: Unopened adventurer loot box from the Early Adopter achievement.

bronze-adventurer-box.name: Bronze Adventurer Box
bronze-adventurer-box.description: Unopened adventurer loot box from the Empty pockets achievement.

gold-apparel-box.name: Gold Apparel Box
gold-apparel-box.description: Unopened clothing loot box from the no-pants achievement.

bronze-weapon-box.name: Bronze Weapon Box
bronze-weapon-box.description: Unopened weapon loot box from the Unarmed combat achievement.

healing-potion.name: Standard Healing Potion
healing-potion.effects: Restores at least 50% of health; does not cure poison or other ongoing health-drain conditions.
healing-potion.description: A small red glass bottle with a cork, about a third the size of a soda bottle.

scroll-of-heal-critter.name: Scroll of Heal Critter
scroll-of-heal-critter.description: A rolled-up scroll from the Bronze Pet Box; description not read in this chapter.

pet-biscuit.name: Pet Biscuit
pet-biscuit.description: Dry pet-food biscuits from the Bronze Pet Box.

toad-cudgel.name: Toad Cudgel
toad-cudgel.description: A crude wooden club shaped like a three-quarter-length baseball bat.

poker.name: Poker
poker.description: A wrought-iron fireplace poker with a screw-on point at the end.

mana-potion.name: Potion of Mana Restoration
mana-potion.description: A mana potion from a Bronze Adventurer Box.

poison-antidote.name: Poison Antidote
poison-antidote.description: Antidotes for poison from the Silver Adventurer Box.

crawler-biscuit.name: Crawler Biscuit
crawler-biscuit.description: Food biscuits for crawlers from the Silver Adventurer Box.

torch.name: Torch
torch.description: Torches from the Silver Adventurer Box.

goblin-dynamite.name: Stick of Dynamite
goblin-dynamite.description: Sticks of dynamite from the Silver Goblin Box.

lighter.name: Lighter
lighter.description: A lighter from the Silver Goblin Box.

goblin-pass.name: Goblin Pass
goblin-pass.effects: Did not enter inventory; instead branded itself as a goblin-skull mark onto Carl's left inner forearm and shows through his jacket.
goblin-pass.description: A goblin-skull-shaped pass symbol that permanently attaches itself to the recipient like a brand.

enchanted-nightgaunt-cloak-of-stoutness.name: Enchanted Nightgaunt Cloak of Stoutness
enchanted-nightgaunt-cloak-of-stoutness.effects: Grants resistance to poison and ice-based attacks and adds Anti-Piercing resistance to all worn armor; a Nightgaunt that sees the wearer may react badly.
enchanted-nightgaunt-cloak-of-stoutness.description: A black, hooded, nearly weightless cloak made from a demon-like creature's wing, with pointed ears on the hood; Mordecai gasped when it appeared.

enchanted-trollskin-shirt-of-pummeling.name: Enchanted Trollskin Shirt of Pummeling
enchanted-trollskin-shirt-of-pummeling.effects: Negates all melee-based damage debuffs (Stun, Knockback, Disarm, Out-of-Breath); does not provoke a negative reaction from trolls. Mordecai calls it a top under-armor tanking garment, normally Platinum/Legendary box loot, returning the wearer to full health in as little as two minutes.
enchanted-trollskin-shirt-of-pummeling.description: An enchanted shirt made of trollskin; Mordecai reacted strongly to its appearance.

enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-splatter-skunk.name: Enchanted Toe Ring of the Splatter Skunk
enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-splatter-skunk.effects: Worn on the right index toe; self-adjusting but uncomfortable. Carl felt his strength rise by 50%.
enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-splatter-skunk.description: An enchanted toe ring, to Carl's annoyance since he wanted shoes.

enhanced-pet-biscuit.name: Enhanced Pet Biscuit
enhanced-pet-biscuit.effects: Per Mordecai: over 100 possible random effects on a pet, roughly half good, the rest neutral or bad; worth a couple thousand gold.
enhanced-pet-biscuit.description: A tiny brown treat that looks like an ordinary pet biscuit but isn't; feeding it to a pet is a gamble.

tome-of-magic-missile.name: Tome of Magic Missile
tome-of-magic-missile.effects: Tomes permanently teach the spell they contain (unlike scrolls, which cast once).
tome-of-magic-missile.description: A spellbook that permanently teaches the Magic Missile spell.

enchanted-crown-of-the-sepsis-whore.name: Enchanted Crown of the Sepsis Whore
enchanted-crown-of-the-sepsis-whore.effects: A Fleeting, Unique item: it crumbles to dust if removed and a replacement generates elsewhere in the dungeon. All attacks (including magical) gain a 15% chance to inflict the Sepsis debuff. Wearing it permanently places the wearer in the royal line of succession of the Blood Sultanate on the ninth floor; royals must slay the Sultan and all other royal family members before descending to the tenth floor, and removing the crown does not remove this status.
enchanted-crown-of-the-sepsis-whore.description: A small cat-sized jeweled tiara with dark, smoky gems and a swirling purple center stone, from a Legendary Quadruped Box awarded for being the first cat to enter the dungeon.

poor-llama-skin.name: Poor Llama skin
poor-llama-skin.description: The skin looted from the Bad Llama corpse; Donut picked first after dealing 71% of the damage.

uncooked-llama-steak.name: Uncooked Llama steak
uncooked-llama-steak.description: One of the two llama steaks from the corpse, taken during Donut's first pick.

baggie-of-meth.name: Baggie of trailer park-grade meth
baggie-of-meth.description: Two baggies of meth from the Bad Llama corpse; added a Pharmaceuticals menu to Carl's inventory.

rat-pelt.name: Rat Pelt
rat-pelt.description: Multiple rat pelts collected over roughly ten skirmishes with rats and Scatterers; exact count not stated, and the text doesn't specify who received them.

scatterer-carapace.name: Scatterer Carapace
scatterer-carapace.description: A carapace dropped by a Scatterer cockroach; it added a Crafting Items menu to Carl's inventory.

bandages.name: Bandages
bandages.description: New type of loot box item that halts bleeding when used.

silver-ring.name: Silver Ring
silver-ring.description: A silver ring from the Silver Adventurer Box that boosts constitution.

gold-magic-item-box.name: Gold Magic Item Box
gold-magic-item-box.description: Awarded for being one of the first 100 crawlers to raise Magic Missile to level three.

talisman-of-the-slate-butterfly.name: Talisman of the Slate Butterfly
talisman-of-the-slate-butterfly.effects: Winged fairies are no longer automatically hostile toward the wearer. Jingles when moving.
talisman-of-the-slate-butterfly.description: A small silver butterfly collar charm honoring the tale of the Slate Butterfly, granting a fairy boon to those who commemorate it.

scatterer-hemolymph.name: Scatterer Hemolymph
scatterer-hemolymph.effects: Identified by the system as an alchemy material.
scatterer-hemolymph.description: Several vials of the white goo Scatterers leak when killed, dropped while clearing the roach nest; exact count not stated.

silver-goblin-box.name: Silver Goblin Box
silver-goblin-box.description: Loot box awarded for killing more than 10 mobs with an explosive.

gold-crowd-control-box.name: Gold Crowd Control Box
gold-crowd-control-box.description: Loot box awarded for killing more than 15 mobs with the same attack.

neighborhood-map.name: Neighborhood Map
neighborhood-map.effects: On touch, automatically populates about a square mile of the minimap, including mob dots and points of interest.
neighborhood-map.description: A scrap of paper dropped by the Hoarder; persistent boss loot that does not disappear when taken.

bronze-boss-box.name: Bronze Boss Box
bronze-boss-box.description: Boss box awarded to the killers of a Neighborhood Boss.

spell-book-torch.name: Spell Book (Torch)
spell-book-torch.effects: Teaches the Torch spell when applied.
spell-book-torch.description: A spell book from her Bronze Boss Box, which Donut applies to herself without reading it first.

tome-of-wisp-armor.name: Tome of Wisp Armor
tome-of-wisp-armor.effects: Teaches Wisp Armor: 5 mana, self-only, 5 minutes plus 1 minute per spell level with a 5 minute cooldown; negates 75% of incoming magic damage and grants temporary immunity to mind-control, but does nothing against physical attacks.
tome-of-wisp-armor.description: A valuable spell tome from the Bronze Boss Box; Carl holds onto it since he only has 3 mana.

satchel-of-gunpowder.name: Satchel of Gunpowder
satchel-of-gunpowder.description: A satchel of gunpowder from the Silver Goblin Box.

enchanted-spiked-kneepads-of-the-shade-g.name: Enchanted Spiked Kneepads of the Shade Gnoll Riot Forces
enchanted-spiked-kneepads-of-the-shade-g.effects: Cancels all Momentum-based attacks; thin eight-inch retractable spikes pop out magically when needed; self-adjusts to fit.
enchanted-spiked-kneepads-of-the-shade-g.description: Slip-on knee bracers made from Thorn Cadaver hide and spikes, from the Gold Crowd Control Box.

scroll-of-confusing-fog.name: Scroll of Confusing Fog
scroll-of-confusing-fog.effects: Fills the room with a mist that only mobs can see.
scroll-of-confusing-fog.description: Three scrolls from the Gold Crowd Control Box.

iron-skin-potion.name: Potion of Iron Skin
iron-skin-potion.effects: Raises physical armor by 100% for five minutes when drunk.
iron-skin-potion.description: Five potions from the Gold Crowd Control Box.

goblin-copper-chopper.name: Goblin Copper Chopper
goblin-copper-chopper.effects: Steam-powered two-wheeler with detachable one-wheel sidecar; runs on coal fed into a hopper, water supplied by a never-emptying permacube; two relief valves must be twisted to prevent overheating or overpressure explosions.
goblin-copper-chopper.description: A rickety human-sized goblin motorcycle built from welded copper scrap with skull seats, described as the unreliable and unsafe preferred assault transport of Goblin Bomb Bards. Classified as a Contraption.

coal.name: coal lump
coal.effects: Fuel for the chopper's steam engine.
coal.description: Lumps of coal taken from the goblin workshop's heap as part of the trade; stored as three inventory stacks of 999.

bag-of-dynamite-bombs-and-grenades.name: bag of dynamite, bombs, and grenades
bag-of-dynamite-bombs-and-grenades.effects: Assorted goblin explosives: dynamite sticks plus several types of small bombs and grenades.
bag-of-dynamite-bombs-and-grenades.description: A bag filled to the brim with goblin explosives, handed over by Rory to complete the trade; Carl stored it in inventory unexamined.

goblin-tools.name: goblin tools
goblin-tools.description: Tons of tools looted from the workshop, from shovels to wrenches to hammers; most too small to serve as weapons.

engineering-supplies-and-wick-coils.name: engineering supplies and wick coils
engineering-supplies-and-wick-coils.description: Assorted engineering supplies plus many coils of wick taken from the workshop.

goblin-explosives.name: goblin explosives
goblin-explosives.description: Multiple types of explosives looted from the goblin workshop.

alchemical-liquids.name: alchemical liquids
alchemical-liquids.description: About fifty gallons of various alchemical liquids taken from the workshop.

black-chopper-tire-discs.name: black chopper-tire discs
black-chopper-tire-discs.description: A pile of the black discs the goblins used for chopper tires.

goblin-workshop-tables.name: goblin workshop tables
goblin-workshop-tables.description: All the workshop tables Carl could lift went into his inventory; only the biggest were left behind.

black-powder.name: black powder
black-powder.description: Almost a ton of coarse, old-school black powder taken into inventory using discarded leather sacks; the remaining barrels were placed on a cart instead.

hobgoblin-pus.name: Hobgoblin Pus
hobgoblin-pus.effects: C4-like explosive detonated by a trigger; small amount but produces a big explosion
hobgoblin-pus.description: A blob of a plastic-explosive-like substance, likely the real prize of the workshop; only a small quantity but the description promised a big blast.

hobgoblin-detonator.name: Hobgoblin Detonator
hobgoblin-detonator.effects: Break off up to 10 tips per stick to detonate up to 10 bombs at once; 5-second countdown; roughly 10 kilometer range; single use
hobgoblin-detonator.description: Magical trigger mechanisms shaped like deodorant sticks; snap off a tip, stick it in an explosive, press the button to detonate.

goblin-box.name: Goblin Box
goblin-box.description: Several goblin boxes awarded from multiple explosive-based achievements.

gold-looter-box.name: gold Looter Box
gold-looter-box.description: Awarded for storing more than a ton of weight in inventory.

silver-beguiler-box.name: silver Beguiler Box
silver-beguiler-box.description: Reward box earned from charming the goblins.

assorted-potions-torches-and-biscuits.name: assorted potions, torches, and biscuits
assorted-potions-torches-and-biscuits.description: The usual pile of consumables from opening her boxes.

enchanted-fae-scale-quadruped-crupper-of.name: Enchanted Fae Scale Quadruped Crupper of the Fleet
enchanted-fae-scale-quadruped-crupper-of.effects: Light, flexible Fae Steel scale armor; weaker than Elven mail or good Orcish steel but the strongest alloy fairy folk can wear
enchanted-fae-scale-quadruped-crupper-of.description: Silver scale armor from her bronze Boss Box that drapes like a skirt over the back half of a quadruped, with a dedicated tail slot; her first piece of armor.

minion-army.name: Minion Army
minion-army.description: A spell tome from the Beguiler Box teaching a 50-spell-point spell that turns hostile enemies into allies who fight for you. Donut has only 24 spell points, and the system would not let her read a tome for a spell she cannot cast.

desperado-pass.name: Desperado Pass
desperado-pass.effects: Tattoo granting access to the Desperado Club; holding it negates the ability to obtain a Vanquisher pass; considered a disqualifying mark
desperado-pass.description: A gold-glowing tattoo from the first Asshole Box that branded itself onto her back over the right shoulder blade, similar to Carl's earlier goblin pass.

weapon-oil-weeping-wound.name: Weapon Oil: Weeping Wound
weapon-oil-weeping-wound.effects: Applied to edged weapons to make wounds bleed longer
weapon-oil-weeping-wound.description: Five potions of weapon oil from the second (silver) Asshole Box.

potions-goblin-dynamite-lighter-smoke-bo.name: potions, goblin dynamite, lighter, smoke bombs, and scrolls of Confusing Fog
potions-goblin-dynamite-lighter-smoke-bo.description: Box haul: more potions, more dynamite, another lighter, a couple of smoke bombs, and a couple more scrolls of Confusing Fog. Notably no pants or shoes.

ring-of-constitution-2.name: ring of constitution +2
ring-of-constitution-2.description: Another constitution ring, this one +2, from the bronze Boss Box.

skill-potion.name: Skill Potion
skill-potion.effects: Drinking it adds one level to the Determine Value skill
skill-potion.description: The single item inside the gold Looter Box; its snarky description mocks the drinker's past spending habits.

khaki-pants.name: khaki pants
khaki-pants.description: Bloodstained women's khaki pants tossed to Carl by Frank Q (as part of his ambush ruse); too small to wear, added to inventory anyway.

scat-thug-spear.name: Scat Thug spear
scat-thug-spear.description: Needle-like spears carried by the knee-high raccoon-headed Scat Thugs; useless to Carl, kept to sell later.

can-of-red-spray-paint.name: can of red spray paint
can-of-red-spray-paint.description: A can of red spray paint given by the eldercare group, used to deface and alter the directional signs pointing to their encampment.

troglodyte-club.name: troglodyte club
troglodyte-club.description: Crude clubs carried by the two Troglodyte Pygmies; the monsters dropped no loot, so Carl took their weapons.

half-full-bottle-of-johnny-walker-black.name: half-full bottle of Johnny Walker Black
half-full-bottle-of-johnny-walker-black.description: A partly consumed bottle of whisky that had been hidden inside the looted office cabinet.

spell-book-puddle-jumper.name: spell book
spell-book-puddle-jumper.description: A magical tome received from the Bronze Boss Box, later revealed to teach the Puddle Jumper spell.

bracelet.name: bracelet
bracelet.description: A bracelet that boosts dexterity, won from a Silver Adventurer Box awarded for finally dealing melee damage.

healing-scroll.name: Heal scroll
healing-scroll.effects: No countdown between reading each scroll; usable on other party members.
healing-scroll.description: Healing scrolls from a Silver Survivor's Box, awarded for ending a boss battle with under 5% health.

cowboy-hat.name: cowboy hat
cowboy-hat.description: A random cowboy hat from a loot box; Carl stored it rather than wearing it.

enchanted-shuriken-of-bloodlust.name: Enchanted Shuriken of Bloodlust

stats.bloodlust_damage_bonus: +8% per hit, compounding, per monster type

enchanted-shuriken-of-bloodlust.effects: Each hit on a monster raises all further damage against that monster type by a compounding 8%; the stars are very fragile.
enchanted-shuriken-of-bloodlust.description: A set of small, low-damage black throwing stars, ordinary looking but carrying a Bloodlust enchantment that stacks damage against a monster type.

determine-value-skill-tonic.name: Determine Value skill tonic
determine-value-skill-tonic.effects: Raises the Determine Value skill by one level when drunk.
determine-value-skill-tonic.description: Another skill potion for the Determine Value skill, found in the Gold Looter Box.

enchanted-war-gauntlet-of-the-exalted-gr.name: Enchanted War Gauntlet of the Exalted Grull
enchanted-war-gauntlet-of-the-exalted-gr.effects: A right-handed wrist bracer that becomes a spiked orcish-steel war gauntlet when the fist is clenched (or a hilted weapon held) for two seconds; does not negate the bare knuckle skill bonus; striking a worshipper of the war god Grull carries a 1.5% chance of transfiguring the target into the deity; warned not to wear during masturbation.
enchanted-war-gauntlet-of-the-exalted-gr.description: A transforming wrist bracer that manifests as a black, spiked war gauntlet, Carl's best loot after his shirt and cloak.

brush.name: brush
brush.effects: Being groomed with it activates a constitution buff.
brush.description: A hairbrush gifted to Donut by Sebastian, the Bopca proprietor of the Taco Bell safe room, after her third shower.

spike-strip.name: spike strip
spike-strip.effects: Each roll-over applies the shurikens' compounding 8% Bloodlust damage bonus to the boss; each strip survives only one or two stabs before breaking.
spike-strip.description: Several homemade strips built from pairs of Enchanted Shuriken of Bloodlust bolted between two pieces of wood, used to tag the rolling boss.

agatha-s-shopping-cart.name: Agatha's shopping cart
agatha-s-shopping-cart.description: Carl confiscates Agatha's heavy shopping cart into his inventory mid-battle to protect her; she demands it back.

borough-field-guide.name: Borough Field Guide
borough-field-guide.effects: Adds monster types to the borough's area of the map; hovering over a quadrant shows a tooltip such as 'Mobs Level 2-5. Axebeak'.
borough-field-guide.description: A guide dropped by the borough boss that annotates the local map with what monsters live where, so explorers know what is eating them. Carl loots his from the Dominatrix corpse; Donut already collected her own.

tuxedo-jacket.name: tuxedo jacket
tuxedo-jacket.description: A huge, blood-soaked, foul-smelling tuskling tuxedo jacket scavenged from the battlefield and stored, not worn.

ruby-necklace.name: ruby necklace
ruby-necklace.description: Part of a haul of unenchanted jewelry looted from the tusklings Carl killed; the inventory list marks the necklace as fairly valuable.

pig-harness.name: Pig Harness
pig-harness.description: A leather strap vest taken from the Tuskling Courtesan Carl killed; the system itself labels it a Pig Harness.

strap-on-dildo.name: strap-on dildo
strap-on-dildo.description: An enormous battlefield find that Imani dropped in disgust and Donut scooped into her inventory under the 'take everything' policy.

vial-of-brindle-grub-hemolymph.name: Vial of Brindle Grub Hemolymph
vial-of-brindle-grub-hemolymph.description: An alchemy material looted from a Brindle Grub corpse.

poor-dingo-pelt.name: Poor Dingo Pelt
poor-dingo-pelt.description: Pelts dropped by the two slain Danger Dingoes.

gold-coin.name: Gold Coin
gold-coin.description: First coins the pair has seen; dropped by a Danger Dingo and grabbed by Donut.

gold-piece.name: Gold Piece
gold-piece.description: Coins from the Bronze Asshole's Box.

drink-ticket-desperado-club.name: Drink Ticket – Desperado Club
drink-ticket-desperado-club.effects: Each red paper ticket can be redeemed at the Desperado Club for one free "drink" (quotation marks in original).
drink-ticket-desperado-club.description: Three paper vouchers for free drinks at the Desperado Club, from the Bronze Asshole's Box.

the-goo-inator-3000.name: The Goo-Inator 3000
the-goo-inator-3000.effects: A shaping tool usable only at a workbench; helps shape materials into other forms.
the-goo-inator-3000.description: A silver, oversized lollipop-shaped crafting tool with a button on the handle, from the Silver Mechanic's Box. Unusable until Carl can buy a personal space on the fourth floor.

cheat-code-potion.name: Cheat Code Potion
cheat-code-potion.effects: Permanently raises one randomly-chosen, recently-used combat or magic skill by three. Expires shortly after generation and cannot be hoarded.
cheat-code-potion.description: A short-shelf-life potion from the Silver Boss Box with a flashing 17-minute expiry timer.

enchanted-pedicure-kit-of-the-sylph.name: Enchanted Pedicure Kit of the Sylph
enchanted-pedicure-kit-of-the-sylph.effects: Nightly foot care (done in a Safe or Personal Space, must stay barefoot) also grants a 30-hour Unbreakable buff for the feet and a 30-hour buff making footfall-triggered traps sound an alarm with a 5-second delay.
enchanted-pedicure-kit-of-the-sylph.description: A zippered black folder holding 12 foot-care tools from the Platinum Shoe Box; maintaining the feet with it grants combat and trap-warning buffs.

spellbook-second-chance.name: Spellbook: Second Chance
spellbook-second-chance.effects: Teaches the Second Chance necromancy spell when read.
spellbook-second-chance.description: A tome delivered by the safe-room mailbox as Donut's floor-two Book of the Floor Club selection.

slingshot.name: Slingshot
slingshot.description: Slingshots looted from the three dead clurichauns, along with their dice and little bags of rock ammunition, pulled remotely into Donut's inventory.

rev-up-pamphlet.name: Rev-Up pamphlet
rev-up-pamphlet.description: A non-magical trifold brochure pitching the Rev-Up Moonshine Consultant pyramid scheme, looted from a clurichaun body.

rev-up-toilet-grade-moonshine.name: Rev-Up Toilet-Grade Moonshine
rev-up-toilet-grade-moonshine.effects: An accelerant: highly flammable 180-proof liquid with explosive fumes, inert until introduced to flame. Drinking a 1.5-ounce swig inflicts the Shit-Faced debuff, with a 50% chance of 30 hours of blindness, a 5% chance of instant death, and a 45% chance to permanently raise a random stat by two points.
rev-up-toilet-grade-moonshine.description: Large clay jugs of second-floor moonshine distilled from Brindle Grub slime trails, prized by drinking establishments on deeper floors. Twenty-five jugs collected from clurichaun corpses; Donut looted them and gave one to Carl.

carl-s-jug-o-boom.name: Carl's Jug O' Boom
carl-s-jug-o-boom.effects: An incendiary tossable: when lit and thrown it makes a small explosion followed by wide-area burning splash damage; flames burn 15 seconds per level of the thrower's Incendiary Device Handling skill. Inert until its torch is activated.
carl-s-jug-o-boom.description: A named oversized firebomb Carl invented from a moonshine jug topped up with goblin oil and corked with a torch; the system generated its own item entry crediting him as creator.

gold-pieces.name: gold pieces
gold-pieces.description: Each of the two dead laminak fairies dropped 25 gold pieces.

brochures.name: brochures
brochures.description: Each fairy carried five brochures in her inventory.

laminak-rev-up-consultant-elite-corpse-a.name: Laminak Rev-Up Consultant Elite Corpse (Alchemy Material)
laminak-rev-up-consultant-elite-corpse-a.effects: Tagged as an alchemy material; the fairies' essence is said to be valuable.
laminak-rev-up-consultant-elite-corpse-a.description: The intact corpses of the two killed laminak fairies, taken into Carl's inventory after Donut refused them.

laminak-fairy-clothes-and-wings.name: laminak fairy clothes and wings
laminak-fairy-clothes-and-wings.description: The fairies' leaf clothing and wings stayed behind on the ground when the corpses were pulled into inventory, and Carl collected them too.

alchemy-workstation.name: Alchemy workstation
alchemy-workstation.description: A workstation table taken from the filling room.

table.name: table
table.description: A second table taken from the filling room along with the alchemy workstation.

rev-up-smoothies-invigorating-banner.name: "Rev-Up Smoothies! Invigorating!" banner
rev-up-smoothies-invigorating-banner.description: A cotton-like cloth banner pulled off the wall of the filling room.

wooden-blender-box.name: wooden blender box
wooden-blender-box.description: Two dusty wooden boxes for a bicycle-powered blender: one holds twelve empty screw-lid glass containers plus a bladed blender lid, the other holds six glasses (its blade missing).

rev-up-immunity-smoothie-recipe.name: Rev-Up Immunity Smoothie Recipe
rev-up-immunity-smoothie-recipe.effects: Making the smoothie grants immunity to the Taint debuff and the Vigorous Measles.
rev-up-immunity-smoothie-recipe.description: A recipe sheet found in the second blender box; it requires only two ingredients and is intended game setup for fighting the boss safely.

bicycle-powered-blender-stationary-bicyc.name: bicycle-powered blender (stationary bicycle)
bicycle-powered-blender-stationary-bicyc.description: The unbolted one of the two stationary bicycles rigged to spin a blender platform; Carl pulled it into his inventory.

rev-up-immunity-smoothie.name: Rev-Up Immunity Smoothie
rev-up-immunity-smoothie.effects: Contains 10 doses. Each dose grants temporary immunity to all health-seeping conditions and debuffs, temporary protection against all communicable diseases, and inflicts the Buzzed debuff (which also adds the Shaky Cam and Truth Serum debuffs).
rev-up-immunity-smoothie.description: A sparkling pink drink blended from moonshine and a whole laminak fairy corpse on the bicycle-powered blender; foul-tasting but protective.

enchanted-fur-brush-of-the-ecclesiastic.name: Enchanted Fur Brush of the Ecclesiastic
enchanted-fur-brush-of-the-ecclesiastic.effects: If someone brushes Donut for ten minutes each night, she gains an extra 2 constitution for 30 hours; works like Carl's pedicure kit.
enchanted-fur-brush-of-the-ecclesiastic.description: A magic grooming brush from Donut's boss box, her only noteworthy loot from the fight.

gold-coins.name: gold coins
gold-coins.description: A couple hundred gold coins from the loot boxes; exact amount unstated, bringing the party hoard to just over 1,000 coins.

dynamite-smoke-bombs-and-lighters.name: dynamite, smoke bombs, and lighters
dynamite-smoke-bombs-and-lighters.description: More explosives supplies from a couple of goblin boxes; quantities unstated.

enchanted-bigboi-boxers.name: Enchanted BigBoi Boxers
enchanted-bigboi-boxers.effects: Wearer may cast a level 15 Protective Shell once every 30 hours; self-sizing enchantment made for size-altering creatures like the BigBoi.
enchanted-bigboi-boxers.description: White boxers covered in little red hearts, from Carl's boss box; Mordecai calls them a genuinely good prize.

platinum-lucky-bastard-box.name: Platinum Lucky Bastard Box
platinum-lucky-bastard-box.description: Loot box granted to each of Carl and Donut as the reward for the Like a Moth to the Flame achievement.

dungeon-gold-rush.name: Dungeon Gold Rush
dungeon-gold-rush.effects: Scratching one of its six spots applies a random effect to her next attack against a mob; one hour cooldown between scratches; cannot be used inside a saferoom.
dungeon-gold-rush.description: A red-and-green scratch-off ticket depicting a troll-like creature tossing gold coins. Roughly twenty possible symbols, mostly beneficial (e.g. mob drops 5,000 coins, doubled damage against the mob) but some harmful (e.g. mob splits in two, mob becomes invulnerable for 30 seconds).

fireball-or-custard.name: Fireball or Custard?
fireball-or-custard.effects: Scratching a spot mid-battle casts a spell at the closest red-tagged mob: 50/50 chance of a level-15 Fireball or a glob of healing Strawberry Custard; thirty minute cooldown between scratches.
fireball-or-custard.description: A red-and-pink scratch-off ticket with five spots, decorated with a cartoon dragon holding a spoon.

moab-mother-of-all-bombs.name: MOAB (Mother of All Bombs)
moab-mother-of-all-bombs.effects: Launches wheeled bombs backward from the moving chopper via a gravity-fed ramp; includes two side chambers with lever-operated drop doors.
moab-mother-of-all-bombs.description: A bomb-launching apparatus built over about five hours with Brandon and Chris: a ski-jump-style ramp made from spare chopper wheel wells, half-pipe guide channels, and shock-absorbing wheelchair casters, mounted on a newly built bike trailer and spray-painted 'Mother of All Bombs'.

baby-uno.name: Baby Uno
baby-uno.effects: Remote-detonated via a magical trigger held by Carl.
baby-uno.description: The largest wheeled bomb, kept in Donut's inventory for launching: three boom jugs, a clay jug of goblin oil, a small jar of gunpowder, and the last of Carl's hobgoblin pus, rolling on free weights used as wheels.

boom-jar-babies.name: boom jar babies
boom-jar-babies.effects: Ignite on impact or when hit with a low-powered magic missile.
boom-jar-babies.description: Several snowboard-sized, pinewood-derby-shaped wheeled bombs built around boom jars, stored in Donut's inventory; exact quantity not stated.

shredder-babies.name: shredder babies
shredder-babies.effects: Wick-lit shrapnel grenades; Donut can light the wicks mentally like torches thanks to her quadruped status.
shredder-babies.description: A few gunpowder-and-shrapnel wheeled bombs with long fuses, made specifically to rip open grub pupa sacs; exact quantity not stated.

oh-shit-babies.name: oh shit babies
oh-shit-babies.effects: Last-resort explosives deemed too dangerous for Donut to carry.
oh-shit-babies.description: Wheeled bombs each consisting of two boom jars and a full-sized gunpowder satchel wrapped in dynamite, kept in Carl's own inventory; exact quantity not stated.

magical-chain.name: magical chain
magical-chain.description: The 300-foot length of magical chain (originally Imani's train chain), the only thing in the hallway to survive the elemental's rampage; Carl grabbed it earlier and loaded it into a MOAB chamber with heavy weights at either end.

mana-toast.name: Mana Toast
mana-toast.description: A burned triangle of toast dispensed by a free toaster in a safe room; it restores mana and nothing else.

silver-mechanic-s-box.name: Silver Mechanic's Box
silver-mechanic-s-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Grease Monkey achievement.

gold-sapper-s-box.name: Gold Sapper's Box
gold-sapper-s-box.description: Loot box awarded by the 'You call that a trap?' achievement.

gorgon-marital-aid.name: Gorgon Marital Aid
gorgon-marital-aid.effects: Hardening and de-hardening tool; workbench-only; fuses joints or adds plasticity to rigid materials without weakening them
gorgon-marital-aid.description: A spatula-shaped tool, supposedly popular with intergalactic porn stars, used to alter material rigidity at a workbench.

sapper-s-table.name: Sapper's Table
sapper-s-table.effects: Explosives or traps crafted at it don't lose stability and can't go off prematurely
sapper-s-table.description: A crafting table like Carl's engineering and alchemy tables, dedicated to safe explosive and trap construction.

proximity-trigger.name: Proximity Trigger
proximity-trigger.effects: Attachable to any non-static trap via a Sapper's Table; enables activation conditions like countdowns and mob-type triggers
proximity-trigger.description: Dime-sized black boxes with attached wire; highly valuable trap components that ranked near the top of Carl's inventory by value.

bates-zip-up-tactical-boots.name: Bates zip-up tactical boots
bates-zip-up-tactical-boots.description: A brand-new pair of tactical boots in Carl's size, identical to his old service boots; a troll gift from the Maestro, since Carl's buffs and skills require bare feet. Carl keeps them boxed and does not wear them.

socks.name: Socks
socks.description: Socks included in the boot box, part of the Maestro's troll gift.

spike-module.name: Spike Module
spike-module.effects: A ready-to-go trap requiring no tinkering; can be upgraded or reconfigured at a Sapper's Table
spike-module.description: A pre-built trap module from Donut's Gold Sapper's Box; the sapper's table needed to modify it won't be available until the fourth floor.

alarm-module.name: Alarm Module
alarm-module.effects: A ready-to-go trap requiring no tinkering; can be upgraded or reconfigured at a Sapper's Table
alarm-module.description: A pre-built trap module from Donut's Gold Sapper's Box; the only new items among her opened boxes, the rest being duplicates.

spiked-helmet.name: Spiked Helmet
spiked-helmet.description: A few kobold spiked helmets scavenged from the burned corpses after the boss fight; quantity not stated.

gerbil-cage.name: Gerbil Cage
gerbil-cage.description: The small metal-reinforced shoebox-sized carrier that held the gerbil boss, undamaged by the explosion.

master-pen-key.name: Master Pen Key
master-pen-key.effects: Opens the individual fighter and bait-animal cages of the kobold fighting pits; magically attuned to kobolds, so a non-kobold can use it only once before it breaks.
master-pen-key.description: A cage key looted from the kobold who pressed the button to open the dingo pens.

hardened-wood-chair.name: Hardened Wood Chair
hardened-wood-chair.description: Several dozen hardened wood spectator chairs ringing the fighting arena, all looted into inventory.

male-mongoliensis.name: Male Mongoliensis
male-mongoliensis.effects: Level 1 pet-class mob, not yet bonded to a crawler; attacks any mob it sees and fights to the death.
male-mongoliensis.description: A seven-inch pink-feathered dinosaur-chicken chosen from the pet reward room; stubborn and vicious, fully grown at level 15 though few reach it. Donut names him Mongo.

spell-book-of-heal-critter.name: Spell Book of Heal Critter
spell-book-of-heal-critter.description: A spell book for the animal-healing spell, received from her boss-fight loot box.

potion-of-determine-value.name: Potion of Determine Value
potion-of-determine-value.effects: When drunk, adds new inventory sort options including a History tab; does not show monetary values of individual items.
potion-of-determine-value.description: Another Determine Value potion, this time from a boss box rather than a Looter Box.

silver-earth-box.name: Silver Earth Box
silver-earth-box.description: Loot box awarded as an incentive for choosing an Earth class.

clown-meat.name: Clown Meat
clown-meat.description: Meat looted from the corpse of Terror the Clown.

big-top-ticket.name: Big Top Ticket
big-top-ticket.description: A circus admission ticket looted from Terror the Clown.

gold.name: Gold
gold.description: Coins looted from Terror the Clown.

oversized-butcher-knife.name: Oversized Butcher Knife
oversized-butcher-knife.description: Two giant butcher knives the stilt clown had wielded.

red-wig.name: Red Wig
red-wig.description: The dead clown's curly red wig.

little-hat.name: Little Hat
little-hat.description: The dead clown's small flowered hat.

scented-candle-sea-breeze.name: Scented Candle (Sea Breeze)
scented-candle-sea-breeze.description: An unopened Amazon-boxed candle labeled Sea Breeze that smells nothing like the sea.

cat-tree.name: Cat Tree
cat-tree.description: A large multi-platform cat tree with scratching posts and a hammock, fancier than her old one.

earth-hobby-potion.name: Earth Hobby Potion
earth-hobby-potion.effects: Drinking grants a random level-3 Earth-based hobby skill the drinker does not already have; cannot be sold or transferred.
earth-hobby-potion.description: A potion mandated by Syndicate law for crawlers who chose a domestic class.

magical-black-pet-collar.name: Magical Black Pet Collar
magical-black-pet-collar.effects: Places Mongo's orange dot on the map wherever he is.
magical-black-pet-collar.description: A black collar for Mongo from Donut's gold pet box.

clockwork-triplicate-spellbook.name: Clockwork Triplicate Spellbook
clockwork-triplicate-spellbook.description: Donut's floor spellbook, retrieved from the saferoom mailbox via the book of the floor club.

cactus-plant.name: Cactus Plant
cactus-plant.description: A three-inch potted cactus with yellow spikes from Home Depot, price tag still reading $3.99.

louis-l-amour-paperback-set-the-sackett-.name: Louis L'Amour Paperback Set (The Sackett Family Saga)
louis-l-amour-paperback-set-the-sackett-.description: A shrink-wrapped set of 18 western paperbacks from one series, recalling Carl's father's bookshelf.

lemur-skin.name: Lemur Skin
lemur-skin.description: A skin carried by the sentinel lemur Donut killed.

throwing-knife.name: Throwing Knife
throwing-knife.description: Six hand-sized black-handled throwing knives in a small bandolier, taken from the dead lemur.

2-strength-ring.name: +2 Strength Ring
2-strength-ring.description: Two rings pulled from the fingers of the dead crawler Nica Bautista.

enchanted-anklet-of-the-fallen-oak.name: Enchanted Anklet of the Fallen Oak
enchanted-anklet-of-the-fallen-oak.effects: Boosts the Double Tap skill, which gives a chance for critical damage when the same attack hits twice in a row.
enchanted-anklet-of-the-fallen-oak.description: A wooden anklet with three clicking seed-pod beads, pried from a dead tree-creature crawler; Carl gave it to Donut for the constitution buff.

mold-lion-heart.name: Mold Lion Heart
mold-lion-heart.description: The heart Carl punched out of a Mold Lion's chest; stored in his inventory.

loose-herbs-potion-ingredients.name: Loose herbs (potion ingredients)
loose-herbs-potion-ingredients.description: Several potion-making ingredients, mostly loose herbs, that Mordecai took from the pub kitchen and passed to Carl to hold in his inventory.

fireball-or-custard-scratcher-lottery-ti.name: Fireball or Custard scratcher lottery ticket

map-upgrade.name: Map upgrade
map-upgrade.effects: Reveals all dots in the neighborhood on the minimap
map-upgrade.description: Standard neighborhood-boss loot from Heather's remains that lights up the surrounding area with mob dots.

ice-cream-cone-of-parasitic-worms.name: Ice cream cone of parasitic worms
ice-cream-cone-of-parasitic-worms.effects: Eating it infests the eater with the vine's parasitic worms
ice-cream-cone-of-parasitic-worms.description: A petrified cone filled with writhing bone-white worms, handed over by Apollon the strongman with Grimaldi's compliments.

street-urchin-spikes.name: Street urchin spikes
street-urchin-spikes.description: The long black needles shed during the street urchin attack; Carl picks them up and stores them in his inventory. Quantity unspecified.

quest-loot-box.name: Quest loot box
quest-loot-box.description: A loot box awarded to Donut for the completed circus quest; type not specified in this chapter.

gold-fan-box.name: Gold Fan Box
gold-fan-box.effects: Locked for thirty hours while fans vote on its contents.
gold-fan-box.description: Carl's first Fan Box, granted by the One Quadrillion! achievement; its contents are chosen by viewer vote.

gold-quest-box.name: Gold Quest Box
gold-quest-box.description: Quest completion box for the circus quest, referenced in the Johnny Quest! achievement text.

skill-potion-acute-ears.name: Skill potion (Acute Ears)
skill-potion-acute-ears.effects: Grants level 3 in the Acute Ears skill when drunk.
skill-potion-acute-ears.description: A skill potion from the Platinum Quest Box that teaches the Acute Ears skill at level three.

enchanted-fang-caps-of-the-expectorating.name: Enchanted Fang Caps of the Expectorating Tizheruk
enchanted-fang-caps-of-the-expectorating.effects: Fleeting item (vanishes if removed). Each bite has a 25% chance to inflict level-five Poisoned and a 10% chance to inflict level-five Paralyzed. Sold in pairs; transferable to a pet, and Mongo's kind can wear up to six pairs.
enchanted-fang-caps-of-the-expectorating.description: A pair of self-installed metallic tooth caps that turn two incisors into vicious ripping fangs; they resemble loose vampire teeth.

smoke-bombs.name: Smoke bombs
smoke-bombs.description: Multiple smoke bombs from the box rewards. Quantity unspecified.

hobgoblin-dynamite.name: Hobgoblin dynamite
hobgoblin-dynamite.effects: White sticks with a larger yield and much better stability than red goblin dynamite.
hobgoblin-dynamite.description: A whole box of hobgoblin dynamite from the bronze boss box earned for the fight with Heather.

trap-supplies.name: Trap Supplies
trap-supplies.description: Assorted odds and ends labeled Trap Supplies; Mordecai warns not to use them until a sapper's table is set up.

100-000-gold-coin-casino-chip.name: 100,000 Gold Coin Casino Chip
100-000-gold-coin-casino-chip.effects: Comp chip; cannot be sold, transferred, or redeemed for gold. Must be played at the High Roller's Roulette Table or the Wheel of Fortune game at the Desperado Club Casino.
100-000-gold-coin-casino-chip.description: A heavy black casino chip with pink and purple edge splotches and the Desperado Club dagger logo, found in Carl's Lucky Bastard box instead of the expected lottery ticket.

scroll-of-upgrade.name: Scroll of Upgrade
scroll-of-upgrade.effects: Upgrades one random currently-equipped item; short shelf life with a 20-minute timer once picked up.
scroll-of-upgrade.description: A single-use scroll from the Platinum Quest Box that randomly improves one equipped item, by a small or large amount.

assorted-non-magical-loot-extra-weapons-.name: Assorted non-magical loot (extra weapons, first-floor spears, random items)

alchemical-supplies.name: Alchemical supplies
alchemical-supplies.description: 10,000 gold worth of alchemical supplies bought at the town's potion stores at Mordecai's direction.

magical-pet-carrier.name: Magical pet carrier
magical-pet-carrier.effects: Zaps Mongo inside so he can sleep and regain strength; he is released with a button press.
magical-pet-carrier.description: A Rubik's-Cube-sized brown box etched with a fire hydrant symbol, bought for 15,000 gold so Mongo can properly rest at night, since his self-imposed guard duty was hurting his stats.

ink-pouches.name: Ink pouches
ink-pouches.description: A few ink pouches dropped as loot by brain boiler mobs; Mordecai was excited about them. Text does not specify who pocketed them.

bottle-of-bourbon.name: Bottle of bourbon
bottle-of-bourbon.description: An unlabeled bottle of cheap earth bourbon ordered at the club for around 50 gold; Carl drinks from it during the visit.

pharmacist-s-starter-kit.name: Pharmacist's starter kit
pharmacist-s-starter-kit.description: A large chest packed with chemicals and herbs for potion-making, including two vials of bujold sap substituted for brin root; Mordecai intends it for his fourth-floor alchemy library.

hair-of-the-dog-potion.name: Hair of the Dog potion
hair-of-the-dog-potion.description: An anti-alcohol potion included in the trade with Quint to sober Donut up from her Dirty Shirley.

blitz-stick.name: Blitz Stick
blitz-stick.effects: Eating one temporarily raises Intelligence by 5; smoking one gives a 15% chance to permanently raise Intelligence by 1. Possible side effects; Mordecai warns they are magically addictive hallucinogens.
blitz-stick.description: Three licorice-like drug sticks Quint threw in on the house; Carl stores them without intending to use them.

hob-lobber-fused.name: Hob-lobber (fused)
hob-lobber-fused.effects: Round fused bomb; yield is one-eighth of a hobgoblin dynamite stick or half a goblin stick. Impact resistant and extra stable; light the fuse and throw. Slightly smaller than a baseball but dense and heavy — too big for Carl's slingshot.
hob-lobber-fused.description: A case of 25 fused round bombs from Pustule's booth, part of the 1,000-gold negotiated lot with the goblin dynamite.

herbs-and-vials-alchemy-supplies.name: Herbs and vials (alchemy supplies)
herbs-and-vials-alchemy-supplies.description: A pile of herbs and vials Mordecai bought from the Silk Road alchemist with Carl's 500 gold and handed over to Carl to carry.

meat-hooks.name: Meat Hooks
meat-hooks.effects: Scroll of a spell that lures all nearby carnivorous pet-class monsters away from their owners toward a burning stench.
meat-hooks.description: A spell scroll taken from Vicente's corpse; the same spell he used to lure Mongo into the alley.

201st-security-group-military-uniform-sh.name: 201st Security Group Military Uniform Shirt
201st-security-group-military-uniform-sh.description: A black button-up military-style shirt with a '201st Security Group - Magical Ops' shoulder patch and a 'Vicente' nameplate, pulled off the dead city elf.

gate-pass.name: Gate Pass
gate-pass.effects: Keeps town guards from hassling the holder while carrying corpses or doing low-key criminal activity; only valid in the issuing township.
gate-pass.description: A pass signed by Magistrate Featherfall stating the holder acts on his orders, found in GumGum's inventory.

mysterious-letter.name: Mysterious Letter
mysterious-letter.description: A blood-stained folded letter from GumGum's inventory written in an unreadable script; Mordecai identifies the writing as necroscript, likely a scroll penned by an undead magic user.

framed-picture-of-bea.name: Framed Picture of Bea
framed-picture-of-bea.description: A small framed photo of Carl's ex-girlfriend Bea taken from her Instagram, delivered via Donut's gold-tier fan box; Donut later displays it beside her cat tree.

xistera.name: Xistera
xistera.effects: A jai-alai throwing scoop that hurls projectiles hard and fast; self-adjusts to projectile size, and a pull-ring retracts it into a bracer over the forearm so the gauntlet still works. Not magical, but advanced technology.
xistera.description: A banana-shaped wicker-looking scoop of light metallic material that straps to the arm with buckles, received from Carl's gold-tier fan box. It replaces his clurichaun slingshot as his ranged option.

oranges.name: Oranges
oranges.description: Five oranges provided by Fitz the barkeep for Carl to test-throw with the xistera.

quest-clues-prostitute-corpse.name: Quest Clues (prostitute corpse)
quest-clues-prostitute-corpse.description: The body of the murdered prostitute found by the inn that morning, stored in Carl's inventory after showing the Gate Pass to a guard; the system labeled it 'Quest Clues'.

rope-30-foot-length.name: Rope (30-foot length)
rope-30-foot-length.description: A thirty-foot rope bought for five gold, used to descend from the elevated town-hall entrance back to the street.

hob-lobbers-impact-detonated.name: Hob-lobbers (impact-detonated)
hob-lobbers-impact-detonated.description: Three boxes of hobgoblin bombs that detonate on impact, bought at the Silk Road.

hobgoblin-smoke-curtain.name: Hobgoblin Smoke Curtain
hobgoblin-smoke-curtain.effects: Round fused bombs producing much heavier smoke than goblin smoke bombs
hobgoblin-smoke-curtain.description: A ten pack of fused smoke bombs from Pustule, said to outperform standard goblin smoke bombs.

bronze-assassin-s-box.name: Bronze Assassin's Box
bronze-assassin-s-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Assassin! achievement.

platinum-tyrant-s-box.name: Platinum Tyrant's Box
platinum-tyrant-s-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Ultimate Extreme Power! achievement, alongside control of the settlement.

silver-quest-box.name: Silver Quest Box
silver-quest-box.description: Loot box awarded by the CockBlock! achievement, taken from another crawler's quest reward.

small-animal-cage.name: Small animal cage
small-animal-cage.description: A moldy, disused small rodent cage from the magistrate's quarters that interested Mongo.

kimaris-cased-plush-figure.name: Kimaris (cased plush figure)
kimaris-cased-plush-figure.effects: Glass case survived a point-blank explosion unbroken, suggesting it is enchanted
kimaris-cased-plush-figure.description: One of Miss Quill's collectible glass cases pulled from the rubble, holding a plush armored man on a black horse tagged Kimaris.

handmade-ladder.name: Handmade ladder
handmade-ladder.description: A rickety ladder Carl assembled in about an hour from scrap wood and metal weight bars in his inventory.

carl-s-doomsday-scenario.name: Carl's Doomsday Scenario
carl-s-doomsday-scenario.effects: Unstable custom explosive; will detonate almost instantly if removed from inventory and can no longer be stabilized.
carl-s-doomsday-scenario.description: A city-leveling bomb formed by combining a massively overloaded soul crystal with a Sheol Glass Reaper Case; Carl pulled the exploding case into his inventory to contain it.

assorted-armor-pieces.name: assorted armor pieces
assorted-armor-pieces.effects: Each piece gives small stat point boosts
assorted-armor-pieces.description: Random armor pieces grabbed from the deactivated swordsmen guards after the floor collapsed.

colossal-broadsword.name: colossal broadsword
colossal-broadsword.description: A huge broadsword taken from the deactivated swordsmen guards' equipment.

train-conductor-s-souvenir-hat.name: Train Conductor's Souvenir Hat
train-conductor-s-souvenir-hat.description: A cheap blue-and-white toddler-style souvenir hat embroidered with 'I rode the Iron Tangle'.

bones.name: bones
bones.description: A few bones taken from the pile in the janitors' rail car.

how-may-i-hurt-you-button.name: "How may I hurt you?" button
how-may-i-hurt-you-button.description: A pinback button taken from a dead Jikininki janitor's suit.

platinum-fan-box.name: Platinum Fan Box
platinum-fan-box.effects: Prize decided by fan vote; becomes available in 30 hours.
platinum-fan-box.description: Loot box awarded for the 500-trillion-follower achievement; contents to be voted on by viewers.

platinum-it-s-not-my-fault-you-fish-head.name: Platinum It's Not My Fault You Fish-Headed Assholes Don't Properly Program Your Quests Box
platinum-it-s-not-my-fault-you-fish-head.description: Spite loot box issued by the AI to crawlers hurt by the court veto.

assorted-adventurer-s-box-loot-potions-b.name: assorted adventurer's box loot (potions, bandages, clothing, unenchanted weapons)
assorted-adventurer-s-box-loot-potions-b.description: Bronze and silver adventurer's boxes yield miscellaneous potions, bandages, random clothing, and unenchanted weapons earmarked for sale, plus a couple hundred gold coins.

quiet-slippers.name: Quiet Slippers
quiet-slippers.description: A pair of stealth slippers from the Bronze Assassin's box; Carl cannot wear footwear so they are unusable to him.

antidote-potions.name: antidote potions
antidote-potions.description: A batch of antidote potions from the Bronze Assassin's box, redundant while Carl wears his Nightgaunt Cloak.

bang-bro-spell-tome.name: Bang Bro spell tome
bang-bro-spell-tome.effects: Teaches the spell Bang Bro when read.
bang-bro-spell-tome.description: Magical tome from the Bronze Boss box earned for killing Quill.

confusing-fog-scroll.name: Confusing Fog scroll
confusing-fog-scroll.effects: Creates a disorienting fog; Carl prefers these to smoke bombs.
confusing-fog-scroll.description: Three scrolls from one of the silver quest boxes.

dynamite-sticks.name: dynamite sticks
dynamite-sticks.description: Additional sticks of dynamite from various random loot boxes; quantity not stated.

enchanted-necklace-of-the-haute-bourgeoi.name: Enchanted Necklace of the Haute Bourgeoisie
enchanted-necklace-of-the-haute-bourgeoi.effects: Each encrusted gem represents a settlement the bearer controls, paying a recurring tax stipend and granting bonuses; currently holds one Poor Sapphire gem for the Medium Skyfowl Settlement from the third floor. Upgrading requires conquering a Large settlement.
enchanted-necklace-of-the-haute-bourgeoi.description: A silver-colored chain of leadership from the Platinum Tyrant's Box marking Carl as custodian of a settlement.

personal-space-and-upgrade-coupons.name: personal space and upgrade coupons
personal-space-and-upgrade-coupons.effects: One coupon for a free level-three Personal Space, two for tier-one environmental upgrades, two for tier-one crafting tables, and two for table upgrades; each capped at 250,000 gold value and not combinable.
personal-space-and-upgrade-coupons.description: Seven paper coupons from the AI's Platinum spite box.

random-scrolls-and-miscellaneous-sale-it.name: random scrolls and miscellaneous sale items
random-scrolls-and-miscellaneous-sale-it.description: A batch of random scrolls and useless items to sell from Donut's boss and quest boxes.

enchanted-tiara-of-mana-genita.name: Enchanted Tiara of Mana Genita
enchanted-tiara-of-mana-genita.effects: Improves ability to detect mobs on the map and removes automatic hostility from worshippers of the goddess Mana Genita.
enchanted-tiara-of-mana-genita.description: A crystal tiara with white jewels replacing the one Donut lost; weaker than her old tiara but still beneficial.

hole-spellbook.name: Hole spellbook
hole-spellbook.effects: Teaches the spell Hole when read.
hole-spellbook.description: A spellbook Donut was owed, retrieved from the saferoom mailbox.

bronze-crafter-s-box.name: Bronze Crafter's Box
bronze-crafter-s-box.description: Loot box from the Martha Stewart! crafting achievement, opened immediately.

unenchanted-pliers.name: unenchanted pliers
unenchanted-pliers.description: A nice pair of ordinary pliers from the Bronze Crafter's Box.

tape-measure.name: tape measure
tape-measure.description: A standard tape measure from the Bronze Crafter's Box.

bucket-of-pink-glitter.name: bucket of pink glitter
bucket-of-pink-glitter.description: An entire bucket of pink glitter from the Bronze Crafter's Box; some spills immediately.

large-padded-mallet.name: large padded mallet
large-padded-mallet.description: Bought from Limp Richard's general store; listed at 250 gold, Donut negotiated the price down to 150 gold.

science-fiction-paperback-books.name: science fiction paperback books
science-fiction-paperback-books.description: Five books received in trade: three Andre Norton titles including Breed to Come, plus The Lathe of Heaven by Ursula K. Le Guin and The Forever War by Joe Haldeman.

minor-mob-drops-shock-chomper-fangs-cave.name: minor mob drops (Shock Chomper fangs, Cave Mudge clubs, gold coins)
minor-mob-drops-shock-chomper-fangs-cave.description: A few corpses pulled onto the platform during the sniping session dropped sharp fangs and about 10 gold each from Shock Chompers, and clubs plus a couple of coins from Bonkers; none valuable, quantities unspecified.

cave-mudge-liver.name: Cave Mudge liver
cave-mudge-liver.description: A Bonker's dropped liver, listed as an alchemical supply, added to Carl's inventory.

pollyslog-arms.name: Pollyslog arms
pollyslog-arms.effects: Severed monster arms that secrete acid; taken as crafting/alchemy material.
pollyslog-arms.description: Two acid-secreting arms cut from a pollyslog by the train doors.

prize-suitcase-case-number-one.name: Prize suitcase (case number one)
prize-suitcase-case-number-one.effects: Reward Room prize case; must be opened before it can be added to inventory.
prize-suitcase-case-number-one.description: A black roller bag chosen as Carl's Yellow Line Reward Room prize.

prize-suitcase-case-number-eight.name: Prize suitcase (case number eight)
prize-suitcase-case-number-eight.effects: Reward Room prize case; must be opened before it can be added to inventory.
prize-suitcase-case-number-eight.description: A large yellow flowered suitcase chosen as Donut's Yellow Line Reward Room prize.

invisibility-potion.name: Invisibility Potion
invisibility-potion.description: A potion found in Carl's prize suitcase; the system description jokes that its function is obvious.

used-vial.name: Used vial
used-vial.description: An emptied potion vial dropped by the cornet, with a trace of honey-like golden liquid inside.

wire-cage-materials-cart.name: Wire Cage Materials Cart
wire-cage-materials-cart.description: A brand-new wheeled wire materials cart found in the robot warehouse room and taken into inventory.

scrap-metal-from-dwarven-automatons.name: Scrap metal from dwarven automatons
scrap-metal-from-dwarven-automatons.effects: Crafting material for the engineering workbench.
scrap-metal-from-dwarven-automatons.description: Almost 15 tons of scrap metal, cogs, and wheels salvaged from two dynamited Dwarven Industrial Light-Duty Automatons.

dwarven-battery-damaged.name: Dwarven battery (damaged)
dwarven-battery-damaged.effects: Both batteries listed as damaged in the menu.
dwarven-battery-damaged.description: Two damaged dwarven batteries pulled from the wrecked automatons.

mordecai-s-special-brew.name: Mordecai's Special Brew
mordecai-s-special-brew.effects: Combines a Gold Standard Healing Potion and Troll's Second Wind for near-immortality via constant healing and regeneration for 30 seconds; does not stop instant-death effects; adds about 10 hours to potion sickness; only two can ever be taken.
mordecai-s-special-brew.description: A custom emergency potion brewed by Mordecai on the level-three alchemy table.

superb-constitution-buff-potion.name: Superb Constitution Buff Potion
superb-constitution-buff-potion.effects: Permanently raises Constitution by a random amount from one to four; only two may ever be drunk in the dungeon.
superb-constitution-buff-potion.description: Two stat potions Mordecai brewed specifically for Donut.

fragmenting-potion-ball.name: Fragmenting Potion Ball
fragmenting-potion-ball.effects: A full potion vial can be transferred into the ball, which can then be thrown at enemies to apply the potion on impact; breaks if thrown too hard.
fragmenting-potion-ball.description: About twenty rubbery avocado-like throwing balls Mordecai crafted, meant to pair with Carl's xistera and attack potions.

determine-value-skill-potion.name: Determine Value skill potion
determine-value-skill-potion.description: A skill potion awarded from a Gold Looter box.

enchanted-mongoliensis-saddle.name: Enchanted Mongoliensis Saddle
enchanted-mongoliensis-saddle.effects: Adjusted to fit cat species; magically affixes to a Mongoliensis as a mount; rider also gains Anti-Piercing Resistance.
enchanted-mongoliensis-saddle.description: A garish blue and red tasseled saddle from Donut's Platinum fan box, letting her ride Mongo.

the-dungeon-anarchist-s-cookbook.name: The Dungeon Anarchist's Cookbook
the-dungeon-anarchist-s-cookbook.effects: Officially described as a humorous chicken-and-goblin recipe book.
the-dungeon-anarchist-s-cookbook.description: A thick, anarchy-symbol-marked book Carl chose as prize six of nine from his Platinum fan box Prize Carousel.

alcohol-cure-potion.name: Alcohol cure potion
alcohol-cure-potion.description: A potion that removes the effects of alcohol.

enchanted-daggers.name: Enchanted daggers
enchanted-daggers.effects: Each dagger carries a different enchantment; the one used on Donut had a 100% damage bonus when stabbed into a target's back
enchanted-daggers.description: A dozen daggers with varied enchantments looted from the dead crawler Ji-Hoon.

explosives-and-case-of-smoke-curtains.name: Explosives and case of smoke curtains
explosives-and-case-of-smoke-curtains.description: A few explosives including an additional case of smoke curtains, plus items from Mordecai's shopping list, bought at the Silk Road.

curved-metal-rods-with-wired-hob-lobber-.name: Curved metal rods with wired hob-lobber fuses
curved-metal-rods-with-wired-hob-lobber-.effects: Fuse pops when it bridges an electrified rail, letting Carl test whether track sections are live
curved-metal-rods-with-wired-hob-lobber-.description: Several test probes Carl built at his benches by wiring hob-lobber fuses to curved metal rods.

jelly-bomb.name: Jelly Bomb
jelly-bomb.effects: Impact-triggered landmine; a depressed spike smashes an impact-detonated hob-lobber, exploding the whole mine with shrapnel
jelly-bomb.description: Tire-shaped contact mines Carl built from a hob-lobber-stuffed metal tube plus a hobgoblin dynamite stick, with scored metal for shrapnel; the system named the design Jelly Bomb.

gold-engineering-box.name: Gold Engineering Box
gold-engineering-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Locomotive Breath achievement.

ochre-line-engineer-s-key.name: Ochre Line Engineer's Key
ochre-line-engineer-s-key.effects: Should open the engine car of other ochre line trains; keys are color-coded per line
ochre-line-engineer-s-key.description: The engineer's key looted from Gore-Gore, central to the plan of boarding a moving engine car.

rev-up-magic-hair-restoration.name: Rev-Up Magic Hair Restoration
rev-up-magic-hair-restoration.description: A bottle of hair-restoration product looted from the balding mantaur engineer.

neighborhood-map-ochre-line.name: Neighborhood map (Ochre Line)
neighborhood-map-ochre-line.effects: On acceptance it populates the interface with the entire Ochre line from station 11 to 435, including all stops and live positions of every train and its occupants' dots
neighborhood-map-ochre-line.description: A map item from Gore-Gore's corpse that reveals the full ochre train line rather than just the local area.

healing-scrolls.name: Healing scrolls
healing-scrolls.description: A bunch of healing scrolls from the boss box for the Gore-Gore kill; Donut called the box a bust.

poison-dart-trap-kit.name: Poison dart trap kit
poison-dart-trap-kit.description: A trap kit from the Gore-Gore boss box.

war-mage-dismember-s-severed-head.name: War mage Dismember's severed head
war-mage-dismember-s-severed-head.description: The head of the war mage killed with the Hole spell trick, taken into inventory before leaving the train.

armorer-s-table.name: Armorer's table
armorer-s-table.effects: Its Armorer's Workshop tab converts shaped metal into true equippable wearables (e.g. items titled as helmets or backpacks) that resize to fit
armorer-s-table.description: A crafting table obtained with a free table coupon, chosen so Katia can equip Carl-made armor and add it to her mass.

ugly-ass-backpack-with-a-completely-usel.name: Ugly Ass Backpack With a Completely Useless Design that Only an Idiot Would Wear
ugly-ass-backpack-with-a-completely-usel.effects: Open-top container recognized by the system as an equippable backpack; contents count as carried mass, and it can be inventoried with contents intact
ugly-ass-backpack-with-a-completely-usel.description: A basket-like metal backpack with dingo-hide straps Carl built at the armorer's table so Katia can equip over a ton of extra metal mass.

ugly-ass-backpack-loaded-with-metal-pole.name: Ugly Ass Backpack (loaded with metal poles, reeds, and metal balls)

alarm-traps-trap-supplies-and-healing-sc.name: Alarm traps, trap supplies, and healing scrolls
alarm-traps-trap-supplies-and-healing-sc.description: Silver quest box contents: nothing but healing scrolls and trap kits; Carl got two more alarm traps plus random trap supplies.

bronze-benefactor-box.name: Bronze Benefactor Box
bronze-benefactor-box.description: A benefactor box sent by Carl's new sponsor, the Valtay Corporation; contents of these are said to beat regular platinum boxes.

valtay-corporation-neural-enhancer-544-v.name: Valtay Corporation Neural Enhancer #544. Variant 32.c
valtay-corporation-neural-enhancer-544-v.effects: Swallowing it permanently alters the brain, installing the interface upgrade 'Identify and Analyze Subspace Portals'; cannot be unequipped or undone
valtay-corporation-neural-enhancer-544-v.description: A blue-and-yellow capsule from the Valtay benefactor box, compatible with Carl's current wetware (Syndicate Crawl Version 47.002.Human); its value listed just below the Kimaris figurine.

sheol-brick.name: Sheol Brick
sheol-brick.effects: Fuel that burns far hotter and far longer than coal, lasting a week or more.
sheol-brick.description: A hardened brick formed from a nonviable Lesser Demon infant, used to stoke steam boilers.

steam-engineer-s-key.name: Steam Engineer's Key
steam-engineer-s-key.effects: Grants access to Iron Tangle employee-only areas and actions on all 400 steam-type trains of the Tangle.
steam-engineer-s-key.description: A large bronze key dropped by the Nightmare Express's dead crew.

neighborhood-boss-box.name: Neighborhood Boss Box
neighborhood-boss-box.description: Boss boxes awarded for killing three neighborhood bosses aboard the train.

platinum-big-daddy-box.name: Platinum Big Daddy Box
platinum-big-daddy-box.description: Loot box granted by the Three Cheers for Slaughter achievement.

route-map.name: Route Map
route-map.description: Iron Tangle route map looted from a neighborhood boss corpse on the tracks.

prism-industries-capacitating-and-focusi.name: Prism Industries Capacitating and Focusing Goggles. Special High-Fashion Edition. "The Princess Donut."
prism-industries-capacitating-and-focusi.effects: Shields eyes from environmental hazards and blinding magic attacks; enhances dark vision; adds heat vision and other visual modes; catches and focuses eye-originating energy spells, letting Magic Missile split into up to four beams or store charge between shots.
prism-industries-capacitating-and-focusi.description: One-of-a-kind sunglasses commissioned and signed by Princess D'Nadia of the Prism Kingdom as a gift for Donut, delivered in her first benefactor box.

toothbrush.name: Toothbrush
toothbrush.description: A regular, unenchanted toothbrush from the Platinum Big Daddy Box.

seize-the-day-toothpaste.name: Seize the Day Toothpaste
seize-the-day-toothpaste.effects: Brushing in a safe room grants a 30-hour buff: triple damage against all boss monsters, or quadruple damage if the boss is a Province, Country, or Floor Boss. Contains five applications.
seize-the-day-toothpaste.description: Travel-sized tube of enchanted cherry toothpaste from the Platinum Big Daddy Box, red with a black skull and 'Carpe Diem' printed on it.

banger-sphere.name: Banger Sphere
banger-sphere.description: Baseball-sized metal spheres crafted at his engineering table from dwarven robot scrap, used as xistera ammunition; he made a few hundred (exact count not stated). The system auto-named only this size 'Banger Spheres'.

babababoon-hats.name: Babababoon hats
babababoon-hats.description: Took all the hats (including baseball caps) from the dead babababoons; count not stated.

babababoon-corpses.name: Babababoon corpses
babababoon-corpses.description: Collected several of the actual babababoon corpses into inventory.

pvp-coupon.name: PVP Coupon
pvp-coupon.effects: If the holder kills the crawler named on the back (Crawler #4,122, Carl), it awards a Gold Savage Box, Gold Weapons Box, Gold Apparel Box, a Platinum Adventurer Box (redeemable max 3 times), and +1 Player Level (redeemable max 3 times)
pvp-coupon.description: A black ticket embossed with a gold leaf skull that rewards betrayal: killing the crawler whose name is printed on its reverse. This one names Carl.

hob-lobber.name: hob-lobber
hob-lobber.description: Restock of hob-lobbers from the boxes (quantity not stated).

pink-feather-boa.name: Pink Feather Boa
pink-feather-boa.description: A pink feather boa Donut came back wearing after visiting the Desperado Club strip clubs.

ring-of-divine-suffering.name: Enchanted Night Wyrm's Ring of Divine Suffering
ring-of-divine-suffering.effects: Grants the wearer the Marked for Death skill: mark a full-health crawler, and when that crawler dies by any cause you gain a permanent +1 to their highest stat (benefit grows +1 per three marks killed), but while a mark is active you cannot heal. Cooldowns and rules vary by floor; current floor cooldown 5 hours, marks take 30 seconds to form, current mark benefit +1 stat point.
ring-of-divine-suffering.description: A green glass ring with a red jewel, originally from a legendary loot box, given to Carl by Frank Q as a form of revenge. Described as a tool for crawler killers that can grow its user exponentially stronger on deeper floors but can also destroy them.

bowl-of-snickers-bars.name: Bowl of Snickers Bars
bowl-of-snickers-bars.description: A bowl of full-sized Snickers bars taken from the deluxe production trailer's refreshment counter to test that Carl's inventory still worked there.

self-powered-refrigerating-shelf.name: Self-Powered Refrigerating Shelf
self-powered-refrigerating-shelf.effects: Keeps items cold without any power connection
self-powered-refrigerating-shelf.description: Wall-mounted cooling shelves from the trailer kitchen, taken because several of Carl's trap, potion, and bomb recipes require frozen or cold components.

makeup-table.name: Makeup Table
makeup-table.description: A real makeup table with a light-up mirror from the celebrity trailer, taken for the personal space main room.

couch.name: Couch
couch.description: A couch stolen from the deluxe production trailer on the way out.

bowl-of-cat-treats.name: Bowl of Cat Treats
bowl-of-cat-treats.description: A bowl of cat treats grabbed off the trailer counter for good measure.

dirty-little-phased-bolt.name: Dirty Little Phased Bolt
dirty-little-phased-bolt.effects: Invisible Phased ammunition that hides its entry wound; casts Temporary Amnesia and Suffering Bleed, draining the victim to 10% health (a second bolt drains to 1%); deaths are attributed to another source with no kill credit or player-killer skull. At least one bolt is broken and unusable until repaired.
dirty-little-phased-bolt.description: Invisible crossbow bolts marketed to assassins, pulled by Carl from Katia's body after Hekla secretly shot her.

hat.name: hat
hat.effects: Lets the wearer pass through the Iron Tangle's portals to teleport to a trainyard; Donut estimates the pile could sell for over three million gold.
hat.description: Over 700 hats donated by fellow crawlers at station 101/75 so Carl can distribute them to people trapped at the abyss.

colored-line-key.name: colored-line key
colored-line-key.effects: Also works for the trainyard portals, like the hats.
colored-line-key.description: Fifteen colored subway line keys handed over by fellow crawlers along with the hats.

broken-spears-and-armor-pieces.name: broken spears and armor pieces
broken-spears-and-armor-pieces.description: Remnants scavenged from the devoured gnoll corpses littering station 75's cavern; Carl notes little of it is useful and the bodies were already looted.

standard-adventurer-box.name: standard adventurer box
standard-adventurer-box.description: Carl and Donut each receive only a handful of standard adventurer boxes despite the day's chaos; exact count and contents not stated.

repair-bench.name: Repair Bench
repair-bench.description: A workbench left behind in the looted hobgoblin repair shop at station 75; whoever raided the shop overlooked it.

tables-shelves-and-empty-hob-lobber-case.name: tables, shelves, and empty hob-lobber cases
tables-shelves-and-empty-hob-lobber-case.description: Plain tables, tool shelves, and empty wooden hob-lobber crates taken from the repair shop.

metal-spear-shelves-and-wooden-armor-man.name: metal spear shelves and wooden armor mannequins
metal-spear-shelves-and-wooden-armor-man.description: Metal shelving for spears plus forty wooden armor mannequins taken from the otherwise-emptied gnoll armory (qty 40 refers to the mannequins).

dwarven-battery.name: dwarven battery
dwarven-battery.effects: Brick-sized dwarven battery at 2% power, pulled from an auxiliary repair cart.
dwarven-battery.description: A nearly-drained dwarven battery from an Interdiction Auxiliary Railway Repair Cart's battery compartment.

dwarven-battery-fabricator.name: dwarven battery fabricator
dwarven-battery-fabricator.effects: Came with fifty dwarven batteries: five fully charged, five at 50%, most of the rest at 10-15%. Each battery takes four mana potions and five minutes to fully charge; Carl charged two more for twelve usable batteries total.
dwarven-battery-fabricator.description: Fabricator for dwarven batteries obtained from a moleman shopkeeper in a trade, recounted here; Carl suspects its placement in the first store was no coincidence.

growler-gary-s-severed-arms.name: Growler Gary's severed arms
growler-gary-s-severed-arms.effects: Gnoll hands are needed on the interdiction cart's paired sensor plates to start it.
growler-gary-s-severed-arms.description: Two gnoll arms bitten off at the elbow by Mongo after killing the regenerating bartender Growler Gary; Carl stores them to use as cart keys.

dwarven-batteries.name: dwarven batteries
dwarven-batteries.description: Carl pulls the batteries from all the auxiliary repair carts after driving them out of the way; exact count not stated, though he'd estimated 40-plus if every cart had one.

growler-gary-s-alcohol-bottles.name: Growler Gary's alcohol bottles
growler-gary-s-alcohol-bottles.description: Carl's group takes the rest of the drunk bartender's remaining liquor stash from under the bar.

growler-gary-s-left-hands.name: Growler Gary's left hands
growler-gary-s-left-hands.effects: Only left hands work on the second key console; two left hands are needed per rapid-response cart, and they must stay in place or the cart shuts off.
growler-gary-s-left-hands.description: Fourteen left hands collected by repeatedly killing the regenerating gnoll bartender, enough for six carts plus a spare pair for a seventh.

the-left-fang-of-the-green-sultan.name: The Left Fang of the Green Sultan
the-left-fang-of-the-green-sultan.effects: Enchanted saber that is part of a two-weapon set; it provides no buffs or powers unless paired with its twin.
the-left-fang-of-the-green-sultan.description: One of Eva's two sabers, dropped in the gore of the vermillion engine car when Hekla was raised; Carl retrieves it into his inventory.

engineer-hat.name: Engineer Hat
engineer-hat.effects: Allows the wearer to pass through Iron Tangle employee portals.
engineer-hat.description: Bautista and a long line of rescued crawlers each lay their train hats at Donut's feet in tribute; she gathers them all into her inventory.

stuffed-grulke-infantry-figure.name: Stuffed Grulke Infantry Figure
stuffed-grulke-infantry-figure.effects: Pulling the tag summons a real Grulke infantry soldier (takes about 10 seconds); common figures fight for roughly fifteen to thirty seconds. Single use; the tag is gone afterward.
stuffed-grulke-infantry-figure.description: A collectible Jaxbrin Amusements beanie figure of a brown frog in a spiked helmet, tag still attached; the system notes it is the most common variation of the Grulke line and nearly worthless to collectors. Given to Carl by Bautista.

enchanted-auto-buckler-of-the-peach-pit.name: Enchanted Auto Buckler of the Peach Pit
enchanted-auto-buckler-of-the-peach-pit.effects: Forms a metallic buckler on command (hotlist or wrist shake). Shatter enchantment: attacks landing on the shield have a 1.5% chance to disarm the opponent; if disarmed, 90% chance the weapon drops, 5% chance it breaks, 5% chance it transfers to the wielder's inventory.
enchanted-auto-buckler-of-the-peach-pit.description: A wrist bracer that projects a small shield. Katia hands it to Carl after her new riot shield made it redundant.

sheol-rocks.name: sheol rocks
sheol-rocks.effects: Burning rocks from Fire Brandy's engine fire; Mordecai considers them extremely valuable.
sheol-rocks.description: Carl collected several hundred still-burning sheol rocks plus a few dozen unlit ones from the demon engineer Fire Brandy before the floor's end.

map-of-the-stars.name: Map of the Stars
map-of-the-stars.effects: Adds boss locations and descriptions to a large area of the map.
map-of-the-stars.description: The city-boss drop left in the dead Mimic Rex's remains; the city-boss counterpart to the neighborhood map and borough field guide.

fan-box.name: Fan Box
fan-box.effects: Contains the traditional Naga wedding gift: the holdings of the defeated (Vinata's estate).
fan-box.description: A fan box awarded alongside the abyss-kill experience; contents not shown in this chapter.

gold-venison-box.name: Gold Venison Box
gold-venison-box.effects: Loot box stated to contain only money.
gold-venison-box.description: A gold-tier loot box awarded by the Quarry Sees Another Spring achievement.

spellbook-of-the-floor-astral-paw.name: Spellbook of the Floor (Astral Paw)
spellbook-of-the-floor-astral-paw.effects: Teaches the spell Astral Paw when read.
spellbook-of-the-floor-astral-paw.description: Donut's spellbook-of-the-floor Desperado Club prize, collected from the mailbox.

silver-fan-box.name: Silver Fan Box
silver-fan-box.effects: Contents to be chosen by viewers; opens the next day.
silver-fan-box.description: Fan box awarded for having the most switchovers during a battle.

platinum-that-wasn-t-too-smart-was-it-bo.name: Platinum That Wasn't Too Smart, Was It? Box
platinum-that-wasn-t-too-smart-was-it-bo.description: Platinum box awarded for attacking the Province Boss.

scroll-of-water-breathing.name: Scroll of Water Breathing
scroll-of-water-breathing.effects: Grants underwater breathing for a duration of intelligence stat times three, in seconds.
scroll-of-water-breathing.description: A common water-breathing scroll; the system jokes it offers no protection from undead barracudas.
