wall-of-fire-spellbook.name: Wall of Fire spellbook
wall-of-fire-spellbook.effects: Teaches the 15-mana Wall of Fire spell.
wall-of-fire-spellbook.description: Magic book from Donut's final platinum box.

surefire.name: Surefire
surefire.effects: Crafting component that adds the Guided status to any projectile, powered or not; extra targeting options when added to traps; does not add range.
surefire.description: A case of 25 bottle-cap-sized crafting items from the Gold Sniper's Box, usable on many crafting tables.

enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-leprous-bandit.name: Enchanted Toe Ring of the Leprous Bandit
enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-leprous-bandit.effects: Grants Sticky Feet: once every six hours walk on walls/ceilings for Dexterity x 2 seconds with bare feet. Grants Super Spreader: once per hour pass any active debuff to a chosen target without removing it from yourself.
enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-leprous-bandit.description: A unique toe ring named for a folk-hero thief who saved his plague-quarantined planet; carries two benefits, making it nearly legendary tier in value.

extinction-sigil-tattoo-wall-monitor-rac.name: Extinction Sigil Tattoo (Wall Monitor Race)
extinction-sigil-tattoo-wall-monitor-rac.effects: Removes automatic hostility from natural enemies of lizard-class creatures; lizard-class enemies deal 20% more damage to Carl and Wall Monitors deal 150% more; only concealable with a cover-up sleeve.
extinction-sigil-tattoo-wall-monitor-rac.description: A small lizard-foot tattoo from the Platinum Asshole's Box that auto-applied below Carl's left eye, his third tattoo.

pawna-s-tears.name: Pawna's Tears
pawna-s-tears.effects: Adds plus five to any one spell or skill of the drinker's choosing; can be saved indefinitely.
pawna-s-tears.description: An ornate smoking potion from the Legendary Deity's Box, tied to Pawna, goddess of peace and Grull's sister; the most valuable loot Carl has received so far.

thorny-devil-liver.name: Thorny Devil Liver
thorny-devil-liver.description: Organ dropped by the slain Thorny Devil.

thorny-devil-teeth.name: Thorny Devil Teeth
thorny-devil-teeth.description: Several moderately valuable teeth dropped by the slain Thorny Devil; exact count not stated.

feral-goose-corpse.name: Feral Goose corpse
feral-goose-corpse.description: Carl stores the dead level 45 boss goose in his inventory, which now has a Mob Morgue tab for worthless monster bodies.

altitude-based-deployment-device-broken.name: Altitude-Based Deployment Device (broken)
altitude-based-deployment-device-broken.effects: Broken egg-shaped deployment shell with an adjustable dial-based opening mechanism.
altitude-based-deployment-device-broken.description: The rusted gnomish drop-egg that failed to open; Carl takes the whole shell to study its mechanism.

the-royal-chariot.name: The Royal Chariot
the-royal-chariot.effects: Silent-engined tracked vehicle that crosses sand and snow quickly; seats three; breaks into two pieces so Carl can store it in his inventory.
the-royal-chariot.description: A crude tracked ATV-snowmobile hybrid (system label: Tracked All-Terrain Suicide Machine, a Contraption) built by Carl and Katia from the salvaged train-cart engine; named by Donut. The system mocks its missing roll cage and bad center of gravity.

dromedarian-bazooka-tube.name: Dromedarian Bazooka Tube
dromedarian-bazooka-tube.description: Two launcher tubes bought from the dromedarians by Donut and Mordecai; the camels refused to sell guided rockets.

rocket-propelled-missile.name: Rocket-Propelled Missile
rocket-propelled-missile.effects: Straight, line-of-sight (unguided) missiles for the bazooka tubes.
rocket-propelled-missile.description: Ten unguided rockets purchased from the dromedarians along with the launcher tubes.

town-hall-office-chair-and-desk-contents.name: town hall office chair and desk contents
town-hall-office-chair-and-desk-contents.description: Miscellaneous minor loot rifled from an unused office desk, plus the chair.

head-of-svern-changeling-principal.name: head of Svern (Changeling Principal)
head-of-svern-changeling-principal.effects: Mordecai says he can make a potion from an untransformed changeling head.
head-of-svern-changeling-principal.description: The glowing, round head of a slain level 49 Changeling Principal guard, taken as an alchemy ingredient.

bowl-with-three-shriveled-mushrooms.name: bowl with three shriveled mushrooms
bowl-with-three-shriveled-mushrooms.description: A small bowl holding a trio of shriveled black mushrooms, apparently psychedelic rather than edible.

basement-table-and-chair.name: basement table and chair
basement-table-and-chair.description: Small table and chair looted from the catacomb chamber under city hall.

stuffed-grulke-infantry-figure-beanbag-s.name: stuffed Grulke infantry figure (beanbag summon toy)

bronze-benefactor-box-valtay-corporation.name: Bronze Benefactor Box (Valtay Corporation)
bronze-benefactor-box-valtay-corporation.description: Benefactor box from Carl's original sponsor, the Valtay Corporation.

silver-benefactor-box-the-open-intellect.name: Silver Benefactor Box (The Open Intellect Pacifist Action Network, Intergalactic NFC)
silver-benefactor-box-the-open-intellect.description: Benefactor box from Carl's new pacifist-network sponsor.

valtay-corporation-neural-enhancer-275-v.name: Valtay Corporation Neural Enhancer #275. Variant 35.j
valtay-corporation-neural-enhancer-275-v.effects: Pill that permanently alters the brain and adds a current elevation and airspeed readout to the interface; cannot be undone once taken.
valtay-corporation-neural-enhancer-275-v.description: A sponsor pill from the Valtay, compatible with Carl's Syndicate Crawl Version 47.002b.Human wetware, installing a speed/altitude interface upgrade.

toraline-root-vegetable.name: Toraline Root Vegetable
toraline-root-vegetable.effects: Alchemy Material; Mordecai says similar tubers appear in specialty healing salves for scaled creatures.
toraline-root-vegetable.description: A foul-tasting tuber that only grows in lava-covered dirt; listed as Very Rare but low monetary value.

modified-two-stage-missile.name: modified two-stage missile
modified-two-stage-missile.effects: Longer-range anti-air missiles built with Mordecai's help on Carl's level-four sapper's table.
modified-two-stage-missile.description: Four modified missiles intended to reach the gnomes' flying Wasteland, pending a range test.

bronze-benefactor-box-princess-d-nadia.name: Bronze Benefactor Box (Princess D'Nadia)
bronze-benefactor-box-princess-d-nadia.description: Another benefactor box from Princess D'Nadia, bronze tier this time.

gnomish-farseer-telescope.name: Gnomish farseer telescope
gnomish-farseer-telescope.effects: Powerful magical telescope with a mounting clamp; shows mob descriptions when zoomed in.
gnomish-farseer-telescope.description: A large, heavy gnomish telescope stolen from young Skarn's rooftop; the promised thirty gold payment was never left.

dwarfish-aluminum.name: Dwarfish Aluminum
dwarfish-aluminum.description: Almost a ton of light but strong dwarfish metal salvaged from the chicken hawk wreckage. Exact holder ambiguous; Katia took the buzzsaw separately.

field-guide.name: Field Guide
field-guide.effects: Reveals the level and description of all mobs in the entire bowl area.
field-guide.description: A guide taken from the borough boss corpse that identifies local monsters, such as the exploding Night Frights.

surefires.name: Surefires
surefires.effects: Used to give missiles a guided targeting upgrade.
surefires.description: A case of 25 surefires from the silver boss box earned for killing Ruckus the chicken hawk.

jellyfish-salve.name: Jellyfish Salve
jellyfish-salve.effects: Removes the pain portion of the Kill Me Now It Hurts debuff; does NOT remove the one-minute healing block from a Pain Amplifier Jelly sting. 5 applications.
jellyfish-salve.description: A lotion bottle that substitutes for the folk remedy of having someone urinate on a jellyfish sting.

enchanted-roll-of-never-ending-duct-tape.name: Enchanted Roll of Never-ending Duct Tape
enchanted-roll-of-never-ending-duct-tape.effects: 50-meter roll of extra-durable fabric tape that regenerates at 1 meter per hour back up to 50 meters.
enchanted-roll-of-never-ending-duct-tape.description: A fan-awarded enchanted duct tape roll from the silver fan box earned at the end of the previous floor; Carl immediately uses it to bind the fake Wynne.

anti-fall-potion-mordecai-s-brew-unnamed.name: Anti-fall potion (Mordecai's brew, unnamed in this chapter)
anti-fall-potion-mordecai-s-brew-unnamed.effects: A fall-survival potion; Mordecai brewed three copies so Donut could join the raid.
anti-fall-potion-mordecai-s-brew-unnamed.description: One of three copies of an unpleasant fall-survival potion Mordecai squeezed out of his supplies before the balloon operation.

fake-gnomish-pocket-watch.name: Fake gnomish pocket watch
fake-gnomish-pocket-watch.effects: A prop facsimile of Henrik's watch; would not pass close inspection.
fake-gnomish-pocket-watch.description: Carl shaped a copy of the gnomish signal watch from memory at his bench in about thirty seconds, on a hunch.

platinum-quest-box.name: Platinum Quest Box
platinum-quest-box.description: Reward for completing the quest 'Squeeze out the Juice Box' by saving Hump Town from the bombardment. Each crawler receives one (Donut opens hers next chapter).

benefactor-box-veriluxx-realpet-companio.name: Benefactor Box (Veriluxx RealPet Companions)
benefactor-box-veriluxx-realpet-companio.description: Donut's sponsor/benefactor box arrives after the battle; not yet opened.

little-red-gnome-hats.name: Little red gnome hats
little-red-gnome-hats.description: Donut goes around collecting the conical red hats from fallen dirigible gnome corpses; quantity unspecified.

intact-dirigible-gnome-corpses.name: Intact dirigible gnome corpses
intact-dirigible-gnome-corpses.description: Carl picks up a few intact gnome bodies to add to his growing corpse collection ('graveyard').

assorted-gnome-loot-tools-scrap-metal-co.name: Assorted gnome loot (tools, scrap metal, cogs, springs, gold coins, unenchanted armor bits)
assorted-gnome-loot-tools-scrap-metal-co.description: Miscellaneous salvage looted from fallen gnome corpses; mostly broken metal plus the odd gold coin.

gnomish-sniper-rifle.name: Gnomish sniper rifle
gnomish-sniper-rifle.description: The only unbroken wide-bore sniper rifle among three dead gnomish snipers; Donut loots it along with their red hats.

bandolier-with-5-live-ammo-balls.name: Bandolier with 5 Live Ammo Balls
bandolier-with-5-live-ammo-balls.effects: Each Live Ammo Ball is a clamshell sphere holding a living creature in a stasis field; it opens on impact and can be fired from a gnomish Tickle Stick, lobbed like a grenade, or fired in clusters from AA batteries. Storable in inventory like pet carriers. Every ball here contains a Level 11 Frenzied Gerbil.
bandolier-with-5-live-ammo-balls.description: An unbroken sniper's bandolier of five live-ammo grenade balls; Donut looted it and dropped it at Carl's feet.

half-splat-potion.name: Half Splat Potion
half-splat-potion.effects: Prevents death from falling damage, but leaves the drinker at 5% health on landing (per Mordecai).
half-splat-potion.description: Three potions from the Silver Skydiver's Box; Mordecai advises keeping them hotlisted until a real Featherfall is available.

coffee-shop-author-kit.name: Coffee Shop Author Kit
coffee-shop-author-kit.effects: Adds a second (for Carl, third) tab to the scratchpad that syncs with the magical paper. Text written in either place appears in both. A properly copied spell on the sheet can be presented at a market kiosk to list unlimited scrolls for sale, or used with a printing press to make scrolls or tomes. The ink is not bottomless.
coffee-shop-author-kit.description: A magical sheet of paper, self-writing quill pen, and capped jar of black ink from the Gold Makeup Sex is the Best Sex box; a scroll-production tool normally used by scholar and arcanist classes.

platinum-sharpening-stone.name: Platinum Sharpening Stone

stats.damage: +(2 x current level)% to all attacks
stats.stat_buffs: +1 to all current stat buffs (does not add new buffs)

platinum-sharpening-stone.effects: Applied to any spiked offensive weapon to grant the listed buffs; has a short shelf life and may slightly change the weapon's appearance.
platinum-sharpening-stone.description: A little black rock from the Platinum Quest Box, an enhancement item for existing weapons.

veriluxx-realpet-dungeon-crawl-special-e.name: Veriluxx RealPet Dungeon Crawl Special Edition Exclusive 'Princess Donut the Queen Anne Chonk'
veriluxx-realpet-dungeon-crawl-special-e.effects: An interactive, allegedly nearly indestructible licensed robot toy replica of Donut (about three-quarters size) with an onboard learning AI persona — partly and erroneously programmed with Garfield material.
veriluxx-realpet-dungeon-crawl-special-e.description: The product sample from Donut's Veriluxx benefactor box: a merchandising robot cat toy of Donut herself, thrilling her as her first merch.

fang-caps-pair.name: Fang Caps (pair)
fang-caps-pair.effects: Two additional caps fitted onto Mongo's teeth (Carl installs them), adding 15% movement speed and 15% strength to the level-29 dinosaur.
fang-caps-pair.description: From Donut's Platinum Quest Box, matching her previous platinum quest box's contents.

skill-potion-animal-wrangling.name: Skill Potion (Animal Wrangling)
skill-potion-animal-wrangling.effects: Adds three points to the Animal Wrangling skill.
skill-potion-animal-wrangling.description: A skill potion from Donut's Platinum Quest Box.

celestial-grenade.name: Celestial Grenade
celestial-grenade.effects: Summons the wielder's worshipped god for 60 seconds and grants a 60-second Divine Intervention aura; if the wielder worships no deity, it summons a random god with no buff.
celestial-grenade.description: A spiked ball weapon created by the nuns of Enyo to forcibly summon their goddess into a village during aggressive conversion campaigns. Extremely rare and expensive.

robot-donut-doll.name: Robot Donut doll
robot-donut-doll.effects: Animated talking robot replica of Donut; self-destructs with a countdown explosion if sufficiently broken.
robot-donut-doll.description: Replacement prototype robot dolls of Donut sent by her toy-company sponsor; the latest was claimed to be more durable.

gnomish-drop-bear-airplane-renamed-night.name: Gnomish Drop Bear airplane (renamed Nightmare II)
gnomish-drop-bear-airplane-renamed-night.effects: Two-engine gasoline biplane with rear gun and an emergency recovery balloon that lifts it to the top of the bubble.
gnomish-drop-bear-airplane-renamed-night.description: An abandoned, ramshackle gnomish biplane originally named Death Trap, secured by Louis, Firas, and Langley; Carl spray-painted it Nightmare II and used it to reach the flying house.

letter-from-the-glass-wizard.name: Letter from the Glass Wizard
letter-from-the-glass-wizard.description: A letter from Ghazi to Kane proposing a truce: stop the bombing or Ghazi, who holds the winding box and has cracked its secret, will cast the world into the void.

mysterious-watch.name: Mysterious Watch
mysterious-watch.effects: Final piece of the Gate of the Feral Gods artifact set.
mysterious-watch.description: One of the pocket-watch pieces of the Gate of the Feral Gods artifact, looted from Commandant Kane's corpse.

soul-crystal-f-quality.name: Soul Crystal (F-Quality)
soul-crystal-f-quality.effects: Powers the magical balloon keeping the house afloat; a clamshell housing controls power flow; killing things recharges it; breaking it is dangerous.
soul-crystal-f-quality.description: A small purple gem on a necklace that fuels the flying house's balloon. Bonnie handed it over per her father's instructions to give it to an adult.

assorted-household-loot-from-the-flying-.name: Assorted household loot from the flying house
assorted-household-loot-from-the-flying-.description: Carl looted the reconstituted Texas house: books, toys, furniture, two laptops, three televisions, gaming consoles, the girl's pink bed and nightstand, dolls and action figures, two mountain bikes, a plastic kayak, a full workbench of tools, holiday decorations, and the disconnected main electrical panel. No guns were included by the system.

robot-donut-doll-new-prototype.name: Robot Donut doll (new prototype)
robot-donut-doll-new-prototype.effects: Sturdier prototype about three times heavier and slightly bigger than the last; survived Mongo's attacks; self-destructs in five seconds if its head panel is opened.
robot-donut-doll-new-prototype.description: The latest Veriluxx robot replica of Donut, delivered via benefactor box so the pair can demo it on an upcoming sponsor show.

social-media-board.name: Social media board
social-media-board.effects: Environmental upgrade displaying a running, AI-moderated feed of social media comments; its purchase means Zev is no longer their social media manager.
social-media-board.description: The interface upgrade Carl had Mordecai buy for Donut; Loita confirms it has been purchased.

pink-bed-nightstand-and-framed-family-ph.name: Pink bed, nightstand, and framed family photo

hobgoblin-disco-ball.name: Hobgoblin Disco Ball
hobgoblin-disco-ball.effects: Unstable magically-infused deflagrating tossable; emits thick multi-colored smoke that reacts to light and sound, concealing all movement inside; unlike a normal smoke curtain, the user is blinded too
hobgoblin-disco-ball.description: A Hobgoblin smoke curtain infused with an invisibility potion, producing a party-like storm of rainbow smoke that bounces to the beat.

loita-s-rebreather.name: Loita's rebreather
loita-s-rebreather.effects: Small collar-sized breathing device usable only by a knee-high creature with gills
loita-s-rebreather.description: A rebreather apparatus looted from the dead admin Loita's body; occasionally sprays water.

decapitated-glass-head-depicting-lika-fr.name: Decapitated glass head depicting Lika from Troubadour's Bounty
decapitated-glass-head-depicting-lika-fr.effects: System description only notes that it is sticky
decapitated-glass-head-depicting-lika-fr.description: The mostly intact glass head of the shattered Lika statue (a crystalized personal companion doll), chipped at the chin, taken from the fountain room floor.

fulgurite.name: Fulgurite
fulgurite.description: A few of the larger intact glass pieces from the shattered statue; the inventory identifies the glass as fulgurite (quantity not stated).

leather-satchel.name: Leather satchel
leather-satchel.effects: Contains the Letter from the Council and the Mysterious Box
leather-satchel.description: A partially scorched leather bag Carl took from the mage Ghazi after punching him out during the sand-ooze escape.

mysterious-winding-box.name: Mysterious Winding Box
mysterious-winding-box.effects: Hums with arcane power; has space for two watches and invites the user to activate it with both watches inside
mysterious-winding-box.description: A heavy old wooden case with a glass window, two knobs, and two round watch cutouts, resembling a humidor or music box; taken from Ghazi's satchel and stored in inventory.

letter-from-the-council.name: Letter from the Council
letter-from-the-council.description: A letter from the Larracos college council explaining that Ghazi summoned the banished deity Psamathe, who now possesses the ghost Queen Quetzalcoatlus, and ordering him to banish the necropolis with the winding box; includes an angry postscript from Tish.

burn-gel.name: Burn Gel
burn-gel.effects: Flammable toothpaste-thick gel made from moonshine and a paste; burns hotter, longer, and brighter than plain fire with little smoke.
burn-gel.description: A bag of homemade incendiary gel Carl prepared at his crafting table from a cookbook recipe, loaded into a funnel like a piping syringe.

banger-sphere-impact-detonated-alarm-tra.name: Banger Sphere (impact-detonated alarm trap)
banger-sphere-impact-detonated-alarm-tra.effects: Hollow metal sphere containing an impact-detonated alarm trap that blasts a chosen song; each labeled for a purpose.
banger-sphere-impact-detonated-alarm-tra.description: Distraction devices Carl built at his sapper's table in under three minutes, with songs picked from memory.

water-breathing-scroll.name: Water Breathing Scroll
water-breathing-scroll.effects: Allows breathing underwater for intelligence x3 seconds.
water-breathing-scroll.description: Ten additional water breathing scrolls from the Bronze I'm Wet Box.

belt-of-buoyancy.name: Belt of Buoyancy
belt-of-buoyancy.effects: Keeps the wearer from sinking; only functions in water.
belt-of-buoyancy.description: A simple belt that wraps around Donut's stomach, an extra item from her silver I'm Wet Box. She was not pleased.

skill-potion-dodge.name: Skill Potion (Dodge)
skill-potion-dodge.effects: Raises the Dodge skill by one level.
skill-potion-dodge.description: A skill potion from Donut's silver Pacifist's Box that broke her Dodge skill past a training plateau.

potion-of-bloodlust.name: Potion of Bloodlust
potion-of-bloodlust.effects: For (Constitution) seconds: doubles strength and movement speed, halves dexterity and constitution, and inflicts the 'Where the Fuck Am I? Who the Fuck Are You?' debuff (berserk amnesia). Each crawler, NPC, or mob killed while active adds another 25% movement speed. Mordecai warned against ever using it.
potion-of-bloodlust.description: A berserking potion from Carl's silver Pacifist's Box; the last user Mordecai saw killed half their own party.

mad-dune-mage-s-note.name: Mad Dune Mage's note
mad-dune-mage-s-note.effects: Multi-page letter explaining how the winding box and paired watches open gates, a list of dial-in destinations around Larracos, and diagrams of gate scenarios; final page missing.
mad-dune-mage-s-note.description: The note Katia looted from the Mad Dune Mage (Ghazi) and handed to Carl, detailing the feral gods' gate mechanics and warnings about the Nothing.

raspberry-gelato-bowl.name: Raspberry gelato (bowl)
raspberry-gelato-bowl.description: A bowl of raspberry gelato from the cart in The Death Stalker tavern; Donut haggled the pazuzu vendor down from two gold to one.

blue-barrel-filled-with-sea-water.name: Blue barrel (filled with sea water)
blue-barrel-filled-with-sea-water.description: A floating barrel Carl grabbed inside the flooded Akula; it entered his inventory still full of sea water.

assorted-submarine-debris-including-bugb.name: Assorted submarine debris (including bugbear metal parts)
assorted-submarine-debris-including-bugb.effects: Mostly worthless scrap, but some pieces are made of very light bugbear metal.
assorted-submarine-debris-including-bugb.description: Carl scooped floating mechanical parts and robot pieces into his inventory while moving through the Akula.

neighborhood-map-the-bugbear-s-delight.name: Neighborhood Map (The Bugbear's Delight)
neighborhood-map-the-bugbear-s-delight.effects: Instantly populated the entire Akula on Carl's map, showing red mob dots, corpse Xs, and which sections are flooded or dry.
neighborhood-map-the-bugbear-s-delight.description: The unlooted neighborhood boss map taken from the corpse of The Bugbear's Delight, a hidden control box under the boss chamber grates that had been killed by system deactivation.

blown-heavy-duty-fuse.name: Blown Heavy-Duty Fuse
blown-heavy-duty-fuse.description: A burned-out fuse looted from the control panel of The Bugbear's Delight alongside the neighborhood map.

torn-book-page.name: Torn Book Page
torn-book-page.effects: Explains how to defeat a Wailing Shrieker ghost.
torn-book-page.description: A page ripped from a cleric's journal. It describes the Wailing Shrieker ghost, how such ghosts are created to guard tombs, and how to defeat one: high-level exorcism, destroying the lover's corpse, banishment spells, or electric shock; flesh-giving spells can neutralize her.

good-healing-potion.name: Good Healing Potion
good-healing-potion.description: A new, higher tier of healing potion starting to appear in Carl's loot; Mordecai says it signals good progression. Quantity not stated.

potion-of-dinosaur-repellent.name: Potion of Dinosaur Repellent
potion-of-dinosaur-repellent.effects: If a pack of dinosaurs attacks the party, they eat the drinker last; lasts 30 hours.
potion-of-dinosaur-repellent.description: An ominous consumable that makes attacking dinosaurs save the drinker for last. Mordecai reacted with an 'uh-oh'.

stock-certificate.name: Stock Certificate
stock-certificate.effects: Each certificate is one share of common stock in a publicly-traded company based in the home system of one of the seven lawsuit plaintiffs; all traded via the I.R.F. exchange and averaging about 10 credits per share.
stock-certificate.description: Seven physically varied stock certificates (paper, an inscribed egg, printed glass). The first is one share of Sigmund Textiles Foundry in the Gun-ya system of the Skull Empire, last recorded at 10.422 credits per share. Mordecai notes crawlers should not be able to hold anything with credit value.

photograph.name: Photograph
photograph.effects: Non-magical 8x10 photos identifying the mothers of the seven Syndicate Court plaintiffs, effectively revealing which factions sued for the gate.
photograph.description: Seven ordinary photographs, each portraying the mother of a lawsuit plaintiff, e.g. Queen Consort Ugloo of the Skull Empire (mother of Prince Stalwart) and Epitome Noflex of the Dream. Mordecai warns Carl to keep them in his inventory.

ring-of-water-breathing.name: Ring of Water Breathing
ring-of-water-breathing.effects: Wearer can breathe underwater as well as on land; per Mordecai it grows gills and makes the skin slimy, so only wear it in water.
ring-of-water-breathing.description: A glowing, sparkling blue ring from the Gold I'm Wet Box that grants amphibious breathing.

drakea-s-enchanted-kerchief-of-disorder.name: Drakea's Enchanted Kerchief of Disorder
drakea-s-enchanted-kerchief-of-disorder.effects: Can be worn on the neck, hair, or face. Grants +5 to the Detect Traps skill, a level-15 Tripper spell castable once every five hours, and the Remote Detonator benefit (magically remote-detonate any explosive the wearer designates, with extra trigger options via a Sapper's table).
drakea-s-enchanted-kerchief-of-disorder.description: A black paisley bandana from Carl's first gold boss box, named for Drakea, the author of the 22nd edition of the Dungeon Anarchist's Cookbook.

enchanted-anarchist-s-battle-rattle.name: Enchanted Anarchist's Battle Rattle
enchanted-anarchist-s-battle-rattle.effects: A patch jacket with unlimited upgrade slots: the +1 to all base stats grows by +1 per compatible patch added, doubled if an eligible Back Patch is attached. Also grants Desperado Club access (already held), Naughty Boys Employment Agency access (already held), and +50% range and accuracy for all thrown explosives.
enchanted-anarchist-s-battle-rattle.description: A black sleeveless canvas jacket with a subtle purple glow from the Platinum Spicy Box, replacing Carl's ruined leather jacket as his signature protection item.

upgrade-patch-small.name: Upgrade Patch. Small.
upgrade-patch-small.effects: When affixed to an eligible garment: +5% Strength and immunity from cloud-based attacks. Patches are fleeting: removing one destroys it.
upgrade-patch-small.description: A half-dollar-sized screen-printed patch depicting planet Earth (under Borant regency), meant to be sewn onto the patch jacket.

sewing-kit.name: Sewing Kit
sewing-kit.description: A small, unenchanted sewing kit with white and red thread spools, for attaching patches to the jacket.

gate-of-the-feral-gods.name: Gate of the Feral Gods
gate-of-the-feral-gods.description: Collected from the dungeon mailbox after being delivered via Liaison action.

potion-ball-of-mordecai-s-special-brew.name: potion ball of Mordecai's Special Brew
potion-ball-of-mordecai-s-special-brew.effects: Heals the target instantly and makes it near-invulnerable for thirty seconds; confirmed to work on Orthrus.
potion-ball-of-mordecai-s-special-brew.description: A throwable ball made by combining Mordecai's special brew potion with another item Mordecai had provided, via the inventory system.

quan-ch-s-severed-arm.name: Quan Ch's severed arm
quan-ch-s-severed-arm.description: Quan's entire left arm, ripped off at the shoulder during the fight; it still has three rings on its fingers.

plus-two-ring-of-strength.name: plus two ring of strength
plus-two-ring-of-strength.description: A simple strength ring of the kind commonly found on the first two floors, pulled off Quan's severed arm.

rockard-s-ring-of-sniping.name: Rockard's Ring of Sniping
rockard-s-ring-of-sniping.effects: Grants the Ripe benefit (all creatures below 50% health are marked on the map) and the Loaded benefit (all non-hidden creatures wearing magical gear are marked on the map).
rockard-s-ring-of-sniping.description: An amber-stoned ring named for an infamous orc crawler notorious for stealing kills and loot at the last moment; taken from Quan's severed arm.

sappy-gold-ring.name: Sappy gold ring
sappy-gold-ring.description: An unenchanted gold band from Quan's index finger, engraved inside with 'For Daddy'; the system calls it worthless. Carl keeps it (still on the severed finger) in his inventory.

tome-of-twinkle-toes.name: tome of Twinkle Toes
tome-of-twinkle-toes.effects: Teaches a cheap spell that makes Mongo or any other minion run very fast, lasting one second per point of the caster's intelligence.
tome-of-twinkle-toes.description: Spellbook Donut received from her platinum quest box; she was excited about it.

enchanted-handcuffs.name: Enchanted Handcuffs
enchanted-handcuffs.effects: Locks a person's wrists together; requires at least 200 strength to break; no key — locked and unlocked magically by Carl, with an optional safe word or phrase.
enchanted-handcuffs.description: A pair of magically reinforced novelty handcuffs encased in red fuzzy velvet, from Carl's first Silver Savage Box.

tome-of-ping.name: tome of Ping
tome-of-ping.effects: Teaches the Ping spell; the book glows and adds the spell to Carl's list when opened.
tome-of-ping.description: A warm, black leather hardback with a skull on the cover, from Carl's platinum quest box.

twin-sun-tattoos.name: Twin Sun Tattoos
twin-sun-tattoos.description: Tribal-style suns tattooed on the back of each hand, glowing faintly orange - the mark of Emberus worship. The rays differ subtly: curled on the right, triangular on the left.

frogger-arcade-machine.name: Frogger arcade machine
frogger-arcade-machine.description: A stand-up Frogger video game machine from Carl's fan box, similar to the one his father owned; Carl rigged a dwarven battery to power it and used it to unwind with the team.

left-fang-of-the-green-sultan.name: Left Fang of the Green Sultan

gate-pieces.name: gate pieces
gate-pieces.description: The three pieces of the portal gate artifact, handed back to Carl by Elle so his party can carry out phase four of the plan.

emergency-platinum-benefactor-box.name: Emergency Platinum Benefactor Box
emergency-platinum-benefactor-box.effects: Sponsor-sent emergency loot box; Mordecai says it may be the most expensive box in the game's history.
emergency-platinum-benefactor-box.description: An emergency platinum box sent by Carl's sponsor, The Open Intellect Pacifist Action Network, Intergalactic NFC, as the feral demon approaches.

enchanted-xistera-extension-slot.name: Enchanted Xistera Extension Slot
enchanted-xistera-extension-slot.effects: Attaches to the end of a xistera and lets the wielder throw a head; custom-fitted for decapitated love doll heads. A thrown withering spirit head travels vastly farther, and the head magically returns once the attachment is removed from the xistera.
enchanted-xistera-extension-slot.description: A glowing curved wicker attachment made specifically for Crawler Carl by The Open Intellect Pacifist Action Network, designed for hurling Samantha's head.

phase-lava-rock-potion.name: Phase Lava Rock Potion
phase-lava-rock-potion.effects: Lets the drinker pass through lava and lava rock for one minute.
phase-lava-rock-potion.description: A potion Mordecai brewed from the sponsor-box yam so Carl's hand can phase through Chris's lava-rock body.

maggie-my-s-inventory-contents-including.name: Maggie My's inventory contents (including many hats)
maggie-my-s-inventory-contents-including.effects: Looted from Maggie's corpse when Mongo vomited it up; Carl tells Donut to give it all to Chris, but Donut objects to giving up the hats and the handover is not shown.
maggie-my-s-inventory-contents-including.description: The full inventory of the dead crawler Maggie My, described as a lot of stuff including a collection of hats.

platinum-venison-box.name: Platinum Venison Box
platinum-venison-box.description: A platinum-tier loot box awarded upon reaching the sixth floor; contents not opened this chapter.

placed-trap-alarm.name: Placed Trap (alarm)
placed-trap-alarm.effects: A crawler-targeting trap that sounds a loud alarm with no delay.
placed-trap-alarm.description: An alarm trap set by Hunter Ontario near the Zockau stairwell; Carl disarmed it and pulled it into his inventory.

alarm-trap-tripped.name: Alarm trap (tripped)
alarm-trap-tripped.effects: A music-blasting alarm trap; cannot be disarmed once triggered but can be stored to silence it.
alarm-trap-tripped.description: The street alarm trap blaring 'Kickstart My Heart'; Carl physically picked it up into his inventory to cut it off.

gold-junkie-s-box.name: Gold Junkie's Box
gold-junkie-s-box.description: Loot box awarded for first use of an artifact of Divine Suffering; not yet opened.

gold-hunter-killer-box.name: Gold Hunter-Killer Box
gold-hunter-killer-box.description: Loot boxes awarded for permanently killing hunters; not yet opened.

platinum-asshole-s-box.name: Platinum Asshole's Box
platinum-asshole-s-box.description: Loot box awarded for the mass casualty explosion in Zockau; not yet opened.

hunting-trophy.name: Hunting Trophy
hunting-trophy.effects: Redeemable like an arcade prize ticket at a prize counter at the Butcher's Masquerade party near the end of the floor; cheapest prize costs one hand; reverts to a mere appendage if transferred or sold.
hunting-trophy.description: Severed hands/claws of the hunters Carl permanently killed (including the mantis Xindy and draconian Chin'dua), serving as exchange currency at the Butcher's Masquerade.

potion-spell-upgrade-unnamed.name: potion (spell upgrade, unnamed)
potion-spell-upgrade-unnamed.effects: Had to be drunk immediately; upgraded the Fear spell.
potion-spell-upgrade-unnamed.description: Strawberry-tasting potion from the Platinum Asshole's Box that had to be consumed on the spot.

the-night-wyrm-s-nasty-little-web-of-suf.name: The Night Wyrm's Nasty Little Web of Suffering Tattoo
the-night-wyrm-s-nasty-little-web-of-suf.description: A spiderweb tattoo on the left elbow granting access to the Guild of Suffering, a cult of the Night Wyrm demigod. His third tattoo in a week.

nitro-sludge.name: Nitro Sludge
nitro-sludge.effects: Inert ramen-like blocks that release enormous oxygen when vaporized at high temperature, amplifying explosions; each block equivalent to several hundred pounds of ammonium nitrate.
nitro-sludge.description: By-product blocks of a shield potion Mordecai had been working on, left on Carl's sapper's table a few days earlier on the previous floor. Quantity described only as 'a few blocks'.

just-wait-until-your-daddy-gets-home-whe.name: Just Wait Until Your Daddy Gets Home - Wheeled Bomb
just-wait-until-your-daddy-gets-home-whe.effects: High Yield Thermobaric Explosive that also imbues Fear within the blast radius; Fortified status. Simulator predicts it flattens everything within a third of a mile and severely injures or kills for a half mile beyond.
just-wait-until-your-daddy-gets-home-whe.description: A five-foot egg-shaped bomb Carl built from a broken live ammo ball casing filled with explosives and Nitro Sludge blocks, welded shut and mounted on a pull cart for Bomo.

enchanted-collar-charm-of-the-effete-bou.name: Enchanted Collar Charm of the Effete Bourgeoisie
enchanted-collar-charm-of-the-effete-bou.effects: Mid-tier chain of leadership: each encrusted jewel represents an owned settlement (currently one Plain Turquoise gem for Point Mongo, Sixth Floor). Holding a settlement's jewel when the floor collapses grants a permanent tax stipend every ten days. Snitch (level 10) summons five town guards instantly and works up to one kilometer outside an owned settlement's limits. Upgrading requires conquering an Extra-Large settlement.
enchanted-collar-charm-of-the-effete-bou.description: A pendant marking the bearer as a settlement-owning elite, gained through Donut's conquest of Point Mongo. She must wear it to command the town guards.

gold-trapmaster-box.name: Gold Trapmaster Box
gold-trapmaster-box.description: Loot box from the Insurgent achievement.

fail-safes.name: Fail Safes
fail-safes.effects: Trap add-on mods that notify Carl if a trap is ever disabled or set off.
fail-safes.description: A pack of twenty trap add-ons from the Gold Trapmaster Box.

dinosaur-repellent-potion.name: dinosaur repellent potion
dinosaur-repellent-potion.effects: Effect vaguely described; lasts a full thirty hours.
dinosaur-repellent-potion.description: Ten potions received since the end of the previous floor; source not stated in this chapter.

enchanted-black-leather-boots.name: enchanted black leather boots
enchanted-black-leather-boots.effects: Protects the wearer from magical bolts.
enchanted-black-leather-boots.description: Black leather boots looted from the hunter Bravvo, enchanted to guard against magical bolt attacks.

good-health-potion.name: Good Health Potion
good-health-potion.description: Health potions looted from the hunter Bravvo's slot-based inventory.

good-mana-refill.name: Good Mana Refill
good-mana-refill.effects: Restores 200 mana points gradually over two minutes rather than instantly.
good-mana-refill.description: A slower-acting mana potion; Carl kept two of the thirty looted from Bravvo.

magical-longsword.name: magical longsword
magical-longsword.description: Bravvo's enchanted longsword that boosts constitution; Carl put it in the sell pile.

hunter-bravvo-s-clothes.name: hunter Bravvo's clothes
hunter-bravvo-s-clothes.description: The dead hunter's remaining, non-magical clothing, all taken by Carl.

corpse-of-hunter-bravvo.name: corpse of hunter Bravvo
corpse-of-hunter-bravvo.description: Carl took the hunter's naked corpse into his inventory after looting it.

potion-of-levitation.name: Potion of Levitation
potion-of-levitation.effects: Lets the drinker rise into the air for a duration of intelligence times two in seconds; weaker than a flying potion.
potion-of-levitation.description: A set of nine levitation potions, the best prize from Bravvo's loot; he had been using one to scout Point Mongo from the air.

arrow-of-enthusiastic-double-gonorrhea.name: Arrow of Enthusiastic Double Gonorrhea
arrow-of-enthusiastic-double-gonorrhea.effects: Poison-tipped arrow inflicting a non-lethal disease that repeatedly sets the victim's genitals on fire and heals them; only cured by gelding oneself or passing it to someone else.
arrow-of-enthusiastic-double-gonorrhea.description: An ordinary-looking arrow with a horrifically cursed poison tip, looted from the Dream hunter Arwick's inventory via Carl's blast-chest safe-looting method.

hand-drawn-hunter-territory-map.name: hand-drawn hunter territory map
hand-drawn-hunter-territory-map.description: A crude map drawn on a Canadian diner kids' menu dividing the hunting grounds among hunter factions (the Hive, the Dream, Skull Empire, the Crafters, Nebular Sin Patrol and others) and marking claimed crawler targets; Carl and Donut are claimed by both the Hive and the Skull Empire.

crawlcon-special-guest-badge.name: CrawlCon Special Guest badge
crawlcon-special-guest-badge.effects: Enrolls Carl as a virtual special guest at CrawlCon with three scheduled events; a 22-hour countdown timer appears on his interface.
crawlcon-special-guest-badge.description: A plastic badge on a Titan Entertainment lanyard granting a free fan booth at the galaxy-wide CrawlCon convention. Carl is scheduled for an art-contest judging panel, a discussion panel, and an autograph and keepsake session, all attended virtually.

black-bracelet.name: black bracelet
black-bracelet.effects: Grants +10 Constitution along with an additional unnamed effect; Carl plans to give it to Donut.
black-bracelet.description: A black bracelet looted from the unconscious Dream elf Akland's arm.

hunter-trophy.name: hunter trophy
hunter-trophy.description: Two hunter trophies gained; Carl got kill credit for both Dream elves, including the one Samantha's missile killed.

enchanted-obsidian-bracelet-of-the-raggl.name: Enchanted Obsidian Bracelet of the Raggle Rouser
enchanted-obsidian-bracelet-of-the-raggl.effects: Wearer may cast a level-10 Fireball spell once every twenty minutes. Item has been upgraded three times; 50% chance to permanently break if upgraded again.
enchanted-obsidian-bracelet-of-the-raggl.description: A light black bangle of obsidian glass, rock formed only under extreme lava conditions; the description warns disrespecting the item could turn the wearer into the same.

unenchanted-bow.name: unenchanted bow
unenchanted-bow.description: Looted from the corpse of the hunter killed by Samantha's seeking missile.

regular-arrows.name: regular arrows
regular-arrows.description: A bunch of ordinary arrows looted from the dead hunter; exact quantity not stated.

photograph-of-louis.name: photograph of Louis
photograph-of-louis.description: An actual photograph of crawler Louis, apparently taken from his Facebook page, with a filter adding dog features.

enchanted-venomous-elven-rock-chucker.name: Enchanted Venomous Elven Rock Chucker
enchanted-venomous-elven-rock-chucker.effects: Hurls up to 300 kilos and infuses anything thrown with Poison Cloud; 90-second cooldown between uses. Taking it creates a new Siege Equipment inventory tab.
enchanted-venomous-elven-rock-chucker.description: A surprisingly small, light trebuchet-like contraption the Dream elves assemble in the field from magically grown poisonous bamboo. Its launch plates appear to be disguised high technology rather than magic.

boulders.name: boulders
boulders.description: A few heavy boulders from nearby ancient ruins, taken as ammunition for the rock chucker; exact quantity not stated.

scroll-of-lightning.name: scroll of Lightning
scroll-of-lightning.description: Three Lightning scrolls looted from The Talent, who died zapping himself with his own scroll near the all-tree.

credit-chit.name: credit chit
credit-chit.description: An unlisted extra item that appeared when Donut used Loot All on The Talent's corpse; it resembles a credit card and has no description. Carl tells her to hold it until Mordecai can examine it.

neighborhood-field-guide-map.name: Neighborhood Field Guide Map
neighborhood-field-guide-map.effects: Shows which river sections are occupied by naiads and which are safe to cross.
neighborhood-field-guide-map.description: A local area map looted from the dead borough boss, showing naiad-held stretches of the river.

summoning-trap.name: Summoning Trap
summoning-trap.effects: When triggered, summons two random town guards or minions from the current floor after a two-second delay; misfires if the setter controls no town or minions; specific targets programmable at a Sapper's Table; single use.
summoning-trap.description: A recycled one-use trap that teleports two guards or minions to its location; useless to Carl without minions, so Donut would have to set it.

silent-alarm-and-glue-traps.name: Silent alarm and glue traps
silent-alarm-and-glue-traps.effects: Glue traps prevent the victim from moving away for a full minute.
silent-alarm-and-glue-traps.description: Assorted disarmed traps collected from the riverbank slope; quantities not stated.

pink-feather.name: Pink Feather
pink-feather.description: A small, dirty, flattened pink feather retrieved by Samantha from the far shore; the system dismisses it as garbage. Evidence of the dinosaur Big Tina's boa.

assorted-armor-pieces-and-enchanted-dagg.name: Assorted armor pieces and enchanted daggers and swords
assorted-armor-pieces-and-enchanted-dagg.description: Magical gear looted from the dead ursine on the south shore; nothing exceptional, but collectively worth over a thousand gold.

disarmed-traps-including-slippery-slope-.name: Disarmed traps including Slippery Slope trap
disarmed-traps-including-slippery-slope-.effects: Slippery Slope would have made the victim slip and fall into the water.
disarmed-traps-including-slippery-slope-.description: A few remaining traps found and disarmed at the edges of the south shore; all placed into inventory.

thorny-dervish-bodies-flowers-and-other-.name: Thorny dervish bodies, flowers, and other alchemy items for Mordecai
thorny-dervish-bodies-flowers-and-other-.description: Corpses and flowers of thorny dervish plant mobs plus several other items on Mordecai's wanted list, gathered while grinding south toward the dryad village.

automaton-table.name: Automaton Table
automaton-table.effects: A level 5 crafting table for building automatons.
automaton-table.description: A level-5 automaton crafting table left in the empty studio for Carl to loot, arranged as the security commander's personal favor for the art contest vote.

dr-ratchet-s-guide-to-building-automaton.name: Dr. Ratchet's Guide to Building Automatons for Fun and Profit
dr-ratchet-s-guide-to-building-automaton.description: An autographed paperback guide to automaton construction by the dwarf Dr. Ratchet, found on the automaton table and taken into inventory.

bowl-of-fruit.name: bowl of fruit
bowl-of-fruit.description: A bowl of fruit provided in the green room; Carl took the fruit and the bowl into his inventory.

just-wait-until-your-daddy-gets-home.name: Just Wait Until Your Daddy Gets Home

bronze-condom.name: Bronze condom
bronze-condom.description: An unenchanted wrapped condom with bronze packaging, sketched with an older woman on one side and a sheep on the other.

vocal-coach-training-room.name: Vocal Coach training room
vocal-coach-training-room.effects: A special saferoom training room with holographic vocal coach Lover Illiana for training Donut's voice; may give her voice-cast illusions substance.
vocal-coach-training-room.description: Prize from a platinum Did-You-Really-Just-Cast-Fireball-in-a-Room-This-Small box: a closet-sized voice training booth.

hunter-killer-box.name: Hunter Killer Box
hunter-killer-box.description: Loot boxes for hunter kills, including credit for hunters killed by the enraged mantis and one box from Future Hunter the orc.

invisibility-potions.name: invisibility potions
invisibility-potions.description: A few invisibility potions from the Silver Lucky Bastard box; exact count not stated.

cloak-with-hammer-based-attack-skills.name: cloak with hammer-based attack skills
cloak-with-hammer-based-attack-skills.effects: Adds a few hammer-based attack skills; Carl cannot wear it.
cloak-with-hammer-based-attack-skills.description: A cloak from the Silver Lucky Bastard box, useless to Carl.

enemy-of-the-church-tattoo-diwata.name: Enemy of the Church Tattoo (Diwata)
enemy-of-the-church-tattoo-diwata.effects: All Diwata adherents outside a Club Vanquisher attack Carl on sight; he may not worship Diwata or her allies; the tattoo cannot be hidden with a cover-up sleeve and is removable only by killing Diwata or losing the arm. Warned never to let Diwata see him.
enemy-of-the-church-tattoo-diwata.description: A large flaming-ball-of-snakes tattoo from the Gold Apostate Box that slapped itself onto Carl's left upper arm, marking him an enemy of the goddess Diwata.

upgrade-patch-medium.name: Upgrade Patch. Medium.
upgrade-patch-medium.effects: Finely made (upgrades enhanced); imbues its upgrades when affixed to an eligible garment; each patch on the vest also adds +1 to all stats. Removal destroys the patch.
upgrade-patch-medium.description: A medium white rectangular patch from the Gold Spicy Box, a bomb embroidered in Spanish blackwork from Spider Reaper Minion silk thread.

head-of-cici-the-rottweiler.name: head of Cici the Rottweiler
head-of-cici-the-rottweiler.description: The severed head of Lucia Mar's giant Rottweiler, killed when Donut snapped off her Hole spell; Donut looted it into her inventory as a trophy.

gold-benefactor-box.name: Gold Benefactor Box
gold-benefactor-box.description: A gold benefactor box awarded by Carl's new sponsor, the Plenty, at the CrawlCon press event. The announcer states Carl will open it upon his return to the dungeon.

amethyst-sliver.name: amethyst sliver
amethyst-sliver.description: A small sliver of amethyst from Carl's Gold Tyrant Box (earned by killing a mayor), even smaller than the poor sapphire already on his necklace. It grants +3 Dexterity and nothing else.

silver-benefactor-box.name: Silver Benefactor Box
silver-benefactor-box.description: A silver benefactor box from the Apothecary, received via system notification right after Carl opened the Plenty's gold box.

enchanted-silver-bracelet-of-the-ab-sola.name: Enchanted Silver Bracelet of the Ab-solar
enchanted-silver-bracelet-of-the-ab-sola.effects: Protection against Vampirism, protection against Lycanthropy, and +25% magical damage against all sapient undead.
enchanted-silver-bracelet-of-the-ab-sola.description: A sparkling silver bracelet set with purple gems shaped like garlic bulbs, from the Apothecary's gold benefactor box. Its rambling description mocks fictional child heroes before listing the anti-undead protections.

charm-animal-potion-recipe.name: Charm Animal potion recipe
charm-animal-potion-recipe.effects: Recipe for a Charm Animal potion that must be brewed for a specific animal and requires a liter of that animal's blood per potion.
charm-animal-potion-recipe.description: A potion recipe from the Apothecary's silver benefactor box; Mordecai says he has chased this recipe for fifty cycles and they already have the rare thorn sap tips it needs.

drawing-of-mongo.name: drawing of Mongo
drawing-of-mongo.description: A drawing of Mongo included with the Charm Animal recipe in the Apothecary's silver benefactor box, signaling that Mongo's blood is the special ingredient.

holy-gooper.name: Holy Gooper
holy-gooper.effects: Anti-vampire grenade: blessed Emberus shrine water mixed with a fine healing potion and an air-activated coagulant in a potion ball; acts like napalm on vampires, especially combined with Donut's maxed Torch spell.
holy-gooper.description: Two potion balls Mordecai crafts and dips in the Emberus shrine's red water before handing to Carl; the system labels them Holy Goopers.

flask-of-mongo-s-blood-1-liter.name: flask of Mongo's blood (1 liter)
flask-of-mongo-s-blood-1-liter.effects: Special ingredient required to brew the Charm Animal potion keyed to Mongo; one liter needed per potion.
flask-of-mongo-s-blood-1-liter.description: A large glass flask of Mongo's blood that Donut collects (cutting and healing him three times) while Carl hides from the hostile dryad guards; she hands it to Mordecai for brewing.

charm-animal-potion.name: Charm Animal potion
charm-animal-potion.effects: A green potion brewed for a specific animal from the Apothecary's recipe using Mongo's blood.
charm-animal-potion.description: The completed Charm Animal potion Mordecai finishes brewing, collected by Carl at the guildhall in Prepotente Number Seven.

spellbook-of-get-out-of-jail.name: Spellbook of Get Out of Jail
spellbook-of-get-out-of-jail.effects: Teaches the Get Out of Jail spell, which can free a frozen/paralyzed target
spellbook-of-get-out-of-jail.description: A magical tome Miriam Dom created by unlearning her own spell; reading it teaches the spell needed to wake Prepotente.

orange-traffic-cone-hat.name: orange traffic-cone hat
orange-traffic-cone-hat.description: A small orange cone-shaped hat Miriam had taken off a gnome-like creature; she offered one to Donut before dying.

scroll-of-size-up.name: Scroll of Size Up
scroll-of-size-up.description: Two scrolls taken from the pile of items Miriam left behind; she had over forty of them.

uncle-morty-s-insta-lawn-kill-and-undead.name: Uncle Morty's Insta Lawn-Kill and Undead Repellant, Extra-Strength Edition
uncle-morty-s-insta-lawn-kill-and-undead.effects: Anti-Odious-Creeper herbicide with anti-undead boosts; each jug can kill two to three Creepers but is very flammable and must be absorbed before igniting
uncle-morty-s-insta-lawn-kill-and-undead.description: Moonshine jugs of bubbling yellow weedkiller Mordecai brewed from an odious bloom herbicide plus undead-killing additives; Carl takes two of the four jugs.

silver-siege-master-box.name: Silver Siege Master Box
silver-siege-master-box.description: Loot box awarded to both Carl and Donut for the B'sieging! achievement.

nipple-rings-magical.name: Nipple Rings (magical)
nipple-rings-magical.effects: Three remaining magical nipple rings pulled from the dead hunter; all four of Iota's rings are magical.
nipple-rings-magical.description: Body-piercing rings looted from the hunter Iota's corpse; their specific properties are not identified in this chapter.

healing-potion-regular.name: Healing Potion (regular)
healing-potion-regular.description: Standard healing potions looted from Iota's gear.

blitz-sticks.name: Blitz Sticks
blitz-sticks.description: Blitz sticks taken from Iota's inventory.

assorted-alcohol-bottles.name: Assorted alcohol bottles
assorted-alcohol-bottles.description: Over forty different types of alcohol from Iota's inventory, including several Earth brands; Carl takes all of it. Exact bottle count not stated.

satchel-bombs.name: Satchel Bombs
satchel-bombs.effects: Throwable bags detonated on command, each with the power of three sticks of hobgoblin dynamite, enough to level a small town.
satchel-bombs.description: Small leather-handled bag bombs from the boss box earned for killing an Odious Creeper. Quantity described only as a bunch.

pawna-s-cries.name: Pawna's Cries
pawna-s-cries.effects: Adds +3 to any one spell or skill of the drinker's choosing; limited shelf life, meaning it must be used before the floor ends.
pawna-s-cries.description: A valuable potion from Carl's benefactor box, a weaker version of the Pawna's Tears he already owns. It arrived with an unusual attached note reading only to save the toraline.

plus-one-token.name: Plus-One Token
plus-one-token.effects: Can be given to another crawler to grant them entry to the Butcher's Masquerade; becomes invalid if the giver falls out of the top 50 or dies before the party.
plus-one-token.description: System announcement grants every top-50 crawler a plus-one token for the party; Carl and Donut each receive one.

high-elf-castle-diagram.name: High Elf castle diagram
high-elf-castle-diagram.description: A surprisingly detailed map of the High Elf castle provided by Edgar the tortoise, showing ballrooms, staging areas, throne room, subsurface levels, a dungeon, and a secret entrance.

stuffed-slate-butterfly-figure.name: Stuffed Slate Butterfly Figure
stuffed-slate-butterfly-figure.effects: When the tag is pulled, harmless slate butterflies fly to the closest light source and drain its energy away.
stuffed-slate-butterfly-figure.description: Common Jaxbrin collectible beanie figures from Bautista's sponsor sample box; he offers Carl up to five of them.

i-take-it-all-back-potion.name: I Take it All Back - Potion
i-take-it-all-back-potion.effects: Removes any effects of Scolopendra's Transmutation spell.
i-take-it-all-back-potion.description: A black, bubbling potion from Prepotente's benefactor box that was intended for Donut; he delivers it to her. A gift tag instructs it be given to Princess Donut, used when the time is right, and NOT given to Tina.

dreadnought-corpse-haxor-the-destroyer.name: Dreadnought corpse (Haxor the Destroyer)
dreadnought-corpse-haxor-the-destroyer.description: After killing the level 50 unaffiliated Dreadnought hunter-merchant in the club bathroom, Carl pulls the entire body into his inventory to hide the evidence.

spider-stalker-main-driveshafts-case.name: Spider Stalker main driveshafts (case)
spider-stalker-main-driveshafts-case.description: A case of the rare, expensive main driveshafts needed to build Spider Stalker automatons, secured on the way out of the Desperado Club.

automaton-seeker-spiders.name: Automaton seeker spiders
automaton-seeker-spiders.effects: Rat-sized exploding spider robots programmable with targets; twenty are set to seek hunters (kills credited to Donut, who activates them), plus two programmed to follow Samantha and explode five seconds after target acquisition.
automaton-seeker-spiders.description: Explosive spider automatons Carl built with his Automaton table, deployed for the coordinated town raid against the hunters.

diwata-flower-petals.name: Diwata flower petals
diwata-flower-petals.description: A few palm-tree-sized petals Elle harvested from the giant flower bush left by Diwata's manifestation, collected at Mordecai's request; Donut takes them into her inventory.

enchanted-hammer-of-fast-forward.name: Enchanted Hammer of Fast Forward
enchanted-hammer-of-fast-forward.effects: Enchanted but has no inherent magical properties; enchantment lets such items affect things like ghosts. A hidden mark shaped like the number seven is impressed on the wooden handle.
enchanted-hammer-of-fast-forward.description: A small ball-peen hammer that fits in one hand, described by the system as magical yet doing nothing magical. Sent in a silver Apothecary benefactor box to Imani with a note to give it to Donut; Carl hands it over before the party.

enchanted-stick-of-cascadia-s-screams.name: Enchanted Stick of Cascadia's Screams
enchanted-stick-of-cascadia-s-screams.effects: Not a wand, but per its description it can do something truly magical when used correctly. A hidden mark shaped like the number seven is at one end.
enchanted-stick-of-cascadia-s-screams.description: A thin metal rod roughly a quarter-inch thick and ten inches long, seemingly aluminum. Delivered via crawler Dmitri Popov in an Apothecary benefactor box with instructions to give it to Donut.

recharge-wand-scroll.name: recharge wand scroll
recharge-wand-scroll.effects: A scroll for recharging a wand.
recharge-wand-scroll.description: One of the Apothecary benefactor-box items relayed through other crawlers for Donut; Carl considers its purpose obvious.

tiara-new.name: tiara (new)
tiara-new.description: A new tiara from the Apothecary's benefactor boxes intended for Donut before the Butcher's Masquerade.

sheet-music.name: sheet music
sheet-music.description: A sheet of music from the Apothecary's benefactor boxes intended for Donut's talent-show performance.

shrink-wand.name: shrink wand
shrink-wand.effects: A wand that shrinks targets, like the one Zhang once had; holds ten charges.
shrink-wand.description: Bought at the masquerade prize counter for 20 hunter hands; delivered as a sealed prize box that can only be opened in a saferoom.

eighth-floor-book-of-lore.name: Eighth Floor Book of Lore
eighth-floor-book-of-lore.effects: A Future Floor Boon with a random starting location; its full title reveals itself upon purchase. Limited to one buyer.
eighth-floor-book-of-lore.description: A 30-hand future-floor boon for the eighth floor, bought at the prize counter; arrives as a prize box openable only in a saferoom.

sheet-music-song-of-skedaddle.name: sheet music: song of Skedaddle
sheet-music-song-of-skedaddle.effects: Bard-only sheet music enabling an escape-type song; reusable, and the song joins the bard's repertoire if sung perfectly. Chaco says it is a lesser version of Wings of the Pyxie.
sheet-music-song-of-skedaddle.description: A 10-hand sheet of escape-song music bought for Donut's use; delivered as a prize box requiring a saferoom to open.

feather-fall-potions.name: Feather Fall potions
feather-fall-potions.effects: Fall-protection potions, bought to cover the party's shortage after Eva stole the staged potions.
feather-fall-potions.description: A case of 24 Feather Fall potions from the prize counter; arrives as a prize box openable only in a saferoom.

healing-potions-case.name: healing potions (case)
healing-potions-case.effects: A case of good healing potions; the one-hand prize tier is described as a 25-pack.
healing-potions-case.description: A case of healing potions bought at the prize counter to refill supplies; delivered as a prize box requiring a saferoom to open.

size-up-potion.name: Size-Up Potion
size-up-potion.description: Carl's first naturally looted Size-Up potion, meaning more will drop in the future and he won't need to trade Prepotente for them.

princess-lunette-incomplete-flesh-golem.name: Princess Lunette (Incomplete Flesh Golem)
princess-lunette-incomplete-flesh-golem.effects: Inert, weightless golem body with no map dot; per Signet's wish, meant to be completed by a Pulpmancer as a body for Samantha
princess-lunette-incomplete-flesh-golem.description: The lifeless flesh-golem replica of Signet's mother, left behind when Signet sacrificed herself and entrusted to Carl.

beanie.name: beanie
beanie.effects: Grants the spell I Need My Personal Space, a knockback that works like a miniature Protective Shell
beanie.description: A beanie Katia had just received from the Apothecary in a benefactor box; she hands it to Carl so he can clear a path through the mantis nymphs.

assorted-trap-items.name: assorted trap items
assorted-trap-items.description: Multiple trap items looted from the hunter Zabit's corpse, too many to sort mid-battle; his drop shield and case of invisibility potions were not among the loot, presumed still in a prize box.

photograph-of-the-burrower-mantis-queen.name: photograph of the Burrower mantis queen

the-enchanted-stick-of-cascadia-s-scream.name: The Enchanted Stick of Cascadia's Screams

squad-flags.name: squad flags
squad-flags.effects: Planting a flag in an entity below 5% health converts it into a T'Ghee totem added to the squad leader's inventory; lost flags are not replaced.
squad-flags.description: Set of twenty squad flags bearing an auto-generated squad logo, issued to the squad leader at the end of the floor announcement.

mute-spellbook.name: Mute spellbook
mute-spellbook.description: Book-of-the-floor delivery waiting in the saferoom mailbox; a spell that stops monsters from casting.

legendary-hunter-champion-box.name: Legendary Hunter Champion Box
legendary-hunter-champion-box.description: Loot box awarded by The Scourge achievement.

platinum-apostate-box.name: Platinum Apostate Box
platinum-apostate-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Marked for Life achievement.

celestial-predator-box.name: Celestial Predator Box
celestial-predator-box.description: Celestial-tier loot box awarded by the Apex Predator achievement.

platinum-orgy-guy-box.name: Platinum Orgy Guy Box
platinum-orgy-guy-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Ménage à Quatro achievement.

platinum-here-s-some-good-shit-because-y.name: Platinum Here's-Some-Good-Shit-Because-You're-Gonna-Need-It-Box
platinum-here-s-some-good-shit-because-y.description: Loot box awarded by the Fuck This Floor achievement.

gold-pacifist-s-box.name: Gold Pacifist's Box
gold-pacifist-s-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Pacifist achievement.

gold-scab-box.name: Gold Scab Box
gold-scab-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Collective Bargaining achievement.

healing-and-mana-potions.name: healing and mana potions
healing-and-mana-potions.description: Good healing and mana potions from a dozen bronze and gold adventurer boxes, along with dozens of junk clothing items and weapons.

potion-of-phase-through-wall.name: Potion of Phase Through Wall
potion-of-phase-through-wall.effects: Lets the drinker pass through wood, stone, or metal walls; dangerous to use, emergency-only per Mordecai.
potion-of-phase-through-wall.description: A few phase potions from the adventurer boxes.

town-upgrade-coupon.name: Town Upgrade coupon
town-upgrade-coupon.effects: Must be used at an owned town; Mordecai says it will be needed for their castle. Carl and Donut each already have a lower-tier one from the Tina quest floors earlier.
town-upgrade-coupon.description: Coupon for a free Town Upgrade from the Tina quest box, saved for the next floor.

love-vampire-skill-potion.name: Love Vampire skill potion
love-vampire-skill-potion.description: Skill upgrade potion for Donut's Love Vampire skill, from the Gold Pacifist's Box.

rigorous-spellbook.name: Rigorous spellbook
rigorous-spellbook.description: Spellbook (plus a few unnamed items) from the Masquerade prize counter box purchased from Chaco; a team buff that reflects the effects of debuffs and afflictions back at attackers.

cosmic-buff-potion.name: Cosmic Buff potion
cosmic-buff-potion.effects: Permanently raises a random stat by 10 points; not subject to the normal buff-potion limit.
cosmic-buff-potion.description: Pink, frothy potion from the Platinum Here's-Some-Good-Shit box.

mercenary-coupon.name: Mercenary coupon
mercenary-coupon.effects: Entitles the bearer to 100,000 gold worth of mercenary services at any participating Meat Shields location.
mercenary-coupon.description: Coupon for a free mercenary purchase, from the Platinum orgy box.

flawless-jeweler-s-gem.name: Flawless Jeweler's Gem
flawless-jeweler-s-gem.effects: Used to upgrade Donut's tiara; must be applied by a jeweler (one is available in the Desperado Club).
flawless-jeweler-s-gem.description: Large yellow-orange citrine-like jewel from the Legendary boss box.

enchanted-nipple-ring-of-the-superior-fi.name: Enchanted Nipple Ring of the Superior Fire Demon's Hand Maiden
enchanted-nipple-ring-of-the-superior-fi.effects: Allows removal of cursed items; imperviousness to natural fire and lava attacks; resistance to fire-based spells; no health loss inside Sheol; may cast Bitch, What? once a day (a 20-second self-only berserk with fire ignition and invulnerability to blunt damage, 30-hour cooldown). Equippable only by a female quadruped; must be manually affixed the first time.
enchanted-nipple-ring-of-the-superior-fi.description: Glowing red nipple ring from the Legendary Beguiler's Box, originally a protective item used by hellspawn familiars who chaperone Sheol's unstable superior fire demons.

tome-of-legionnaires-of-the-damned.name: Tome of Legionnaires of the Damned
tome-of-legionnaires-of-the-damned.effects: Summoning spell: reanimates recently-unequipped weapons or armor with wailing spirits as temporary minions. Cost 25 mana; duration (spell level*2 + intelligence level) seconds; one-minute cooldown; single active instance; used items usually disintegrate afterward.
tome-of-legionnaires-of-the-damned.description: A thick, chained, wriggling leather tome from the De-Sleeving Box that teaches a powerful undead-summoning spell.

enchanted-prison-tattoo-kit.name: Enchanted Prison Tattoo Kit
enchanted-prison-tattoo-kit.effects: Lets an artist tattoo a willing or unwilling target; tattoo artistry can imbue magical buffs and upgrades depending on the artist's talent.
enchanted-prison-tattoo-kit.description: Stick-and-poke tattoo kit (needle, ink, sticks, abrasive) from the Gold Quest Box, the same gear Edgar the tortoise used on Tsarina Signet.

peace-symbol-upgrade-patch.name: peace symbol upgrade patch
peace-symbol-upgrade-patch.effects: Neutral mobs are less likely to fall into aggro; fleeting item, destroyed if removed after affixing.
peace-symbol-upgrade-patch.description: Small round black-and-white peace symbol patch for Carl's jacket, from the Gold Pacifist's Box.

find-traps-skill-potion.name: Find Traps skill potion
find-traps-skill-potion.description: Skill potion from the Gold Scab Box that disappeared into inventory before Carl could examine it; later identified as +3 to Find Traps.

enchanted-nipple-ring-of-the-defiler.name: Enchanted Nipple Ring of the Defiler
enchanted-nipple-ring-of-the-defiler.effects: Instantly reveals the religious affiliation of nearby mobs and crawlers; allows destroying church shrines without deity retribution; grants Black Nimbus once an hour (strips a single target of their deity's blessing and buffs, bars them from churches for 30 + [caster's Intelligence+Constitution] - [target's Intelligence/2] seconds, higher levels may force a smite). Only equippable by a crawler Marked for Death by a god; must be manually affixed the first time.
enchanted-nipple-ring-of-the-defiler.description: Silver nipple ring with a hanging eyeball, from the Platinum Apostate Box.

enchanted-right-back-atcha-personal-shie.name: Enchanted Right Back Atcha Personal Shield
enchanted-right-back-atcha-personal-shie.effects: Auto-deploying small buckler that can catch physical attacks (success based on dexterity) and magical bolt attacks (success based on intelligence) and throw them back at the attacker; attacks must be caught with the buckler.
enchanted-right-back-atcha-personal-shie.description: Glowing black leather forearm bracer with buckles, from the Legendary boss box for killing Imogen.

crafting-table-upgrade-coupons.name: crafting table upgrade coupons
crafting-table-upgrade-coupons.description: Two crafting table upgrade coupons from the Legendary Scourge box.

doctor-ratchet-s-automaton-build-it-kit.name: Doctor Ratchet's Automaton Build-It Kit
doctor-ratchet-s-automaton-build-it-kit.effects: Contains parts to build over fifteen automatons, from personal servants to flame-throwing sentinel droids; includes parts Carl needs for recipes in the stolen instruction book.
doctor-ratchet-s-automaton-build-it-kit.description: Heavy steampunk chest of automaton components from the Legendary Scourge box.

the-scavenger-s-daughter.name: The Scavenger's Daughter
the-scavenger-s-daughter.effects: Back upgrade patch; benefits hidden until affixed to an eligible garment and worn. Divine item: gains varying abilities, buffs, or debuffs near certain deities and demons. Fleeting item, destroyed if removed. Only one back patch may be affixed at a time.
the-scavenger-s-daughter.description: Full-sized black back patch embroidered with a white pyramid of skulls and transparent fiberoptic-like stitching, the sole content of the Celestial Predator Box.

ghost-tourist-s-belongings-iphone-wallet.name: ghost tourist's belongings (iPhone, wallet, clothes)
ghost-tourist-s-belongings-iphone-wallet.description: After a ghost's kick strips his clothes off, Carl pockets the working rental iPhone (no service), a wallet with credit cards, Euro banknotes, and a few American five dollar bills, plus the pants, shoe, sock, and underwear; all replay loot turns to dust when the floor collapses.

paper-map-of-havana.name: paper map of Havana
paper-map-of-havana.description: English-language tourist map of downtown Havana with transit instructions, plucked from a ghost's hands and used for navigation.

red-beret.name: red beret
red-beret.description: Glowing magical red beret looted (along with the rest of the corpse's loot) from The Experience, the Che Guevara-headed dog monster Donut killed; its stats made Donut gasp but were not shown.

t-ghee-card-combo.name: T'Ghee Card (Combo)
t-ghee-card-combo.effects: Consumable utility card: temporarily combines two monster cards into a single creature; summoning length equals the shorter of the two summoning phases, and both monsters are tagged out for the rest of combat afterward. Destroyed on use.
t-ghee-card-combo.description: Postcard-sized card found in The Experience's inventory; Carl could not take it because cards may only be held by the squad leader, so it goes to Donut.

geraldo-monk-seal-picket-sentry-totem-ca.name: Geraldo. Monk Seal Picket Sentry (Totem Card)
geraldo-monk-seal-picket-sentry-totem-ca.effects: Level 70 monk seal totem automatically activated as the first member of the party's six-slot squad.
geraldo-monk-seal-picket-sentry-totem-ca.description: A totem card captured by flagging a second monk seal on the beach, adding Geraldo as the team's first squad member.

time-extend.name: Time Extend
time-extend.effects: Utility card that doubles the time a summoned totem lasts; reusable, not a one-time consumable.
time-extend.description: Two common utility cards collected after clearing the duende swarm.

stout.name: Stout
stout.effects: Utility card that adds 25% to a mob's health for the duration of combat; reusable, not a one-time consumable.
stout.description: A common utility card collected after clearing the duende swarm.

spirit-box.name: Spirit Box
spirit-box.effects: Set trap that roots a corporeal target in place for sixty seconds, lowers spirit resistance, and summons a random non-corporeal entity to attack or possess the victim.
spirit-box.description: A spirit-summoning trap Carl disarms on the cemetery road and takes into his inventory.

expired-tube-of-mace.name: Expired tube of mace
expired-tube-of-mace.effects: No magical properties; a joke prize keychain mace tube.
expired-tube-of-mace.description: An old, expired keychain self-defense mace tube with no game effects.

hobbled.name: Hobbled
hobbled.effects: Consumable snare card that cuts an opponent totem's summoning time by 90%; the reduction persists through the combat even on resummon.
hobbled.description: The party's first snare card, depicting a pair of legs tied together.

the-bard-s-golden-throat.name: The Bard's Golden Throat
the-bard-s-golden-throat.effects: Auto-tunes singing within one and a half steps of the correct note, assists forming bard-based songs and spells, and amplifies all speech and song at twice normal volume, adjustable up to five times.
the-bard-s-golden-throat.description: An enchanted golden, amethyst-encrusted headset microphone voted on by Donut's fans; a working version of the prop she used at the masquerade.

massage-coupon.name: Massage coupon
massage-coupon.effects: Entitles Carl plus one companion to a free one-hour massage at the Penis Parade or Penis Palace spa in the Desperado Club.
massage-coupon.description: A newspaper-clipping style coupon voted on by fans, good for a spa massage at a Desperado Club strip club.

flee-card.name: Flee Card
flee-card.description: A pair of consumable Flee cards given by Katia from her team's surplus.

blue-stuff.name: Blue Stuff
blue-stuff.effects: Utility card that increases a totem's supply of mana points.
blue-stuff.description: A utility battle card Carl had not seen before, taken from Katia's pile.

greased-lightning.name: Greased Lightning
greased-lightning.effects: Utility card that increases a mob's speed by 25%.
greased-lightning.description: A utility battle card taken from Katia's pile; Carl notes it would make Geraldo extremely fast.

troll-smoke-mantle.name: Troll Smoke Mantle
troll-smoke-mantle.effects: A step up from Pustule's smoke curtains; combining four with a stun grenade widens the stun's range and extends it up to thirty seconds.
troll-smoke-mantle.description: A case of twelve smoke mantles bought for 30,000 gold from a Desperado Club market booth via Li Na's team.

anti-undead-stun-bomb.name: Anti-undead stun bomb
anti-undead-stun-bomb.effects: A modified stun grenade combined with four smoke mantles; mass-paralyzes non-corporeal undead in a wide area for up to thirty seconds.
anti-undead-stun-bomb.description: A beachball-sized stun bomb Carl built with Mordecai; he also built six smaller smoke missiles and six single-target anti-undead missiles into Bomo's new armor.

skylar-spinach-two-thirds-of-an-enraged-.name: Skylar Spinach. Two-Thirds of an Enraged Ghommid Splitter
skylar-spinach-two-thirds-of-an-enraged-.effects: T'Ghee totem card of a non-corporeal, affliction-dealing splitter mob from Cuba; splits into two pieces on a fatal blow until the primary dies twice, gaining Constitution each iteration. Notable attacks: Iced, Dodge, Terrifier, Splitter, plus 5 additional skills and spells. Auto-activated into the squad because slots were empty.
skylar-spinach-two-thirds-of-an-enraged-.description: A rare totem card obtained by flagging the tough donkey-headed ghommid splitter; captured at two-thirds strength since Carl had already killed it once.

asojano-totem-card.name: Asojano totem card
asojano-totem-card.effects: The level 130 city boss Asojano, Lord of Smallpox, captured as a battle card by flagging him at the moment of death.
asojano-totem-card.description: Asojano puffs away and turns into a card when Carl flags him; no card text is shown in this chapter.

the-thief.name: The Thief
the-thief.effects: A non-consumable mystic card that lets the holder steal a card from someone else.
the-thief.description: A mystic-type card with the black border of mythical rarity, gifted by the goddess Yemaya in thanks for the shrine repair; it completes the party's collection of all five card kinds.

snare-card.name: Snare card
snare-card.description: One more snare-type card added to their card collection while grinding.

hole-in-the-bag.name: Hole in the Bag
hole-in-the-bag.effects: Drains a totem's mana; unlike their Hobble card, it is not consumable.
hole-in-the-bag.description: A card that saps mana from an opposing totem and can be reused.

force-discard.name: Force Discard
force-discard.effects: Picks a random card from the opponent's hand and sends it to the discard pile.
force-discard.description: A rare mystic card for the totem card game.

t-ghee-card-heyzoos-uzi-jesus.name: T'Ghee Card: HeyZoos. Uzi Jesus.
t-ghee-card-heyzoos-uzi-jesus.effects: Undead, ranged, healer totem mob from Ecuador. Notable attacks: Blood into Lead, Resurrect Totem, Bullet Hell, plus 20 additional skills and spells. Collecting it auto-placed the totem into the squad since slots were open; it cannot be removed or traded until the squad is full except by tearing the card.
t-ghee-card-heyzoos-uzi-jesus.description: A unique totem card depicting an anime-style Jesus firing an uzi. Florin gave it away because his charisma was too low to control the totem, which once shot him during a summons.

bottle-of-hand-sanitizer.name: Bottle of hand sanitizer
bottle-of-hand-sanitizer.description: Thrown in with the We Want Meat! achievement reward.

certified-pole-dancer-pinback-button.name: Certified pole dancer pinback button
certified-pole-dancer-pinback-button.description: A little pinback button reading 'Certified pole dancer,' given by the dwarf patron Rosemarie after Carl limped out of the strip club cubicle. Katia mentions she got one too.

blank-silver-tiara.name: Blank silver tiara
blank-silver-tiara.description: A simple, silver-colored blank tiara bought from the gnome jeweler at the Desperado Club as a mount for the citrine gem.

enchanted-tiara-of-the-inebriated-dragon.name: Enchanted Tiara of the Inebriated Dragonfly
enchanted-tiara-of-the-inebriated-dragon.effects: When equipped: 50% reduction to potion cooldown, +10 intelligence, protection from poisoning (already obtained), negates the dexterity debuff when inebriated, and grants a level 10 Hover skill.
