enchanted-tiara-of-the-inebriated-dragon.description: A citrine-encrusted tiara themed on a children's story about a drunk dragonfly locked out by his wife. Created by installing Donut's Flawless Jeweler's Gem into a blank silver tiara.

raul-totem-card.name: Raul totem card
raul-totem-card.description: A common totem card of Raul, a level 70 Red Maniseros Land Crab and Sun-Kissed Cloud Rank Warrior captured by flag. Carl caught the card and handed it to Donut; it is the fifth of their six squad slots.

liquid-therapy-potion.name: Liquid Therapy potion
liquid-therapy-potion.effects: Taken once a day, it protects until the drinker sleeps against the Bedlam Bride's Unending Insanity and Lights Out spells plus a host of other fear-based debuffs and afflictions.
liquid-therapy-potion.description: A protection potion Mordecai brewed from expensive ingredients; several were made, enough for the entire guild.

damnation.name: Damnation
damnation.effects: Snare card: when an enemy totem it is cast on times out, that totem card is discarded whether the totem died or not.
damnation.description: A snare card dropped by the Legendary monster La Ciguapa after Mongo killed her; useful against enemy totems too powerful to kill outright.

glow-up.name: Glow-Up
glow-up.effects: Single-use consumable special effect card castable on any active card or totem; permanently rewrites the target card to reflect its current buffed state (e.g. doubled summon time becomes permanent).
glow-up.description: A mythic consumable card, not on the list of known card types, dropped by La Ciguapa; could make an already-strong totem card far more powerful if stacked with buffs first.

demon-snare.name: Demon Snare
demon-snare.description: Traps taken from the trapped secret corridor behind the Desperado Club shops while Carl disarmed the passage for the Astrid ambush.

fairy-wings.name: fairy wings
fairy-wings.description: The wings Carl rips from the fairy Astrid to break her spell freezing Katia; fairies store their magic in their wings. Carl stores them in his inventory.

jola.name: Jola
jola.effects: Totem card added to the active deck
jola.description: A totem card of a mythic creature from Iceland, given to Donut from Katia's excess totem cards before the club trip.

combined-totem-card-new-creature.name: combined totem card (new creature)
combined-totem-card-new-creature.effects: New totem created by combining two existing totems and buffing them with utility cards
combined-totem-card-new-creature.description: The product of the Combo card merge; described only as a jerk who likes fighting.

sailor-hat.name: sailor hat
sailor-hat.effects: Lowers the draw countdown on her cards by two seconds.
sailor-hat.description: White old-school sailor's hat taken from the corpse of the defeated deckmaster Robert the Human.

token-coin-numbered-1.name: token (coin numbered 1)
token-coin-numbered-1.effects: One of nine tokens required before fighting the key boss
token-coin-numbered-1.description: A poker-chip-sized coin dropped by Robert the Human; nine tokens must be collected to unlock the final key fight.

token-coin-numbered-2.name: token (coin numbered 2)
token-coin-numbered-2.effects: Second of nine tokens needed for the key fight
token-coin-numbered-2.description: Poker-chip-sized coin etched with the number two, dropped by the Babababoon deckmaster.

tokens-coins-numbered-3-4-and-5.name: tokens (coins numbered 3, 4, and 5)
tokens-coins-numbered-3-4-and-5.effects: Tokens three through five of the nine needed for the key fight
tokens-coins-numbered-3-4-and-5.description: Coins collected from deckmaster opponents three through five (a level 50 turtle creature and two guarded deckmasters), each five levels higher than the last.

precision-utility-cards.name: Precision utility cards
precision-utility-cards.effects: Each boosts a totem's chance to land attacks; multiple copies stack on one totem while counting as a single buff
precision-utility-cards.description: Accuracy-buff utility cards looted from the defeated Reef Explorer's bloated deck.

assorted-non-totem-cards-from-the-reef-e.name: assorted non-totem cards from the Reef Explorer's deck
assorted-non-totem-cards-from-the-reef-e.effects: Adds the defeated deck's non-totem cards to the squad's collection
assorted-non-totem-cards-from-the-reef-e.description: Donut grabs all remaining non-totem cards from the roughly 40-50 card drop, discarding the totems (a Swordfish Interloper and a rare squid creature).

token-coin-numbered-6.name: token (coin numbered 6)
token-coin-numbered-6.effects: Sixth of nine tokens needed for the key fight
token-coin-numbered-6.description: Coin collected from the defeated level 65 Reef Explorer deckmaster.

scroll-of-build-trench.name: Scroll of Build Trench
scroll-of-build-trench.effects: Places a permanent trench 2.3 meters deep and 2 meters wide; length scales with user's intelligence plus strength; scrolls can be combined to add sleeping quarters and other front-line accessories
scroll-of-build-trench.description: A set of WWI-themed trench-digging scrolls found inside the Silver Spam Box.

the-filthy-little-crawler-s-book-of-vood.name: The Filthy Little Crawler's Book of Voodoo
the-filthy-little-crawler-s-book-of-vood.effects: Cannot be traded, destroyed, or discarded; when near another crawler who owns the same book, their name is permanently added; owners can exchange private messages by writing under a name, or broadcast by writing under their own entry
the-filthy-little-crawler-s-book-of-vood.description: A thick blank leather book from the Gold Prepotente Box, styled after old convention voodoo message boards; most crawlers in the dungeon received one.

lamp-snare-cards-consumable.name: Lamp snare cards (consumable)
lamp-snare-cards-consumable.effects: Paralyzes a totem for ten seconds and makes it glow brightly
lamp-snare-cards-consumable.description: Consumable snare cards looted from the defeated Visitor's 30-card drop; Carl calls them a great prize.

remaining-non-totem-cards-from-the-visit.name: remaining non-totem cards from the Visitor's deck
remaining-non-totem-cards-from-the-visit.effects: Adds the defeated deck's non-totem cards to the squad's collection
remaining-non-totem-cards-from-the-visit.description: The squad takes all the cards they can from the 30-card drop, leaving the 18 totems behind (a legendary level-110 Demon Queen and Quench Moths).

token-seventh-coin.name: token (seventh coin)
token-seventh-coin.effects: Seventh of nine tokens needed for the key fight
token-seventh-coin.description: Token collected from the defeated Visitor, Deckmaster Seven of Ten.

corpse-of-tom-turkey-deckmaster.name: corpse of Tom (turkey deckmaster)
corpse-of-tom-turkey-deckmaster.effects: Holds an unclaimed token needed for the key fight
corpse-of-tom-turkey-deckmaster.description: Carl scoops the mangled boss turkey corpse into his inventory before it can slide off the collapsing cliff; it cannot be looted mid-combat.

corpse-of-sharp-elbows-ogre-deckmaster.name: corpse of Sharp-Elbows (ogre deckmaster)
corpse-of-sharp-elbows-ogre-deckmaster.effects: Holds an unclaimed token needed for the key fight
corpse-of-sharp-elbows-ogre-deckmaster.description: Carl pulls the decapitated ogre necromancer's body into his inventory while dangling over the cliff edge, reopening his puncture wounds.

cards-from-sharp-elbows-deck.name: cards from Sharp-Elbows' deck
cards-from-sharp-elbows-deck.effects: Adds the dead ogre deckmaster's cards, including some of the squad's old favorites, to Donut's collection
cards-from-sharp-elbows-deck.description: While Carl was unconscious, Donut grabbed all of Sharp-Elbows' cards floating at the cliff edge and reworked the deck.

necromancer-staff-card-worm-fulcrum.name: necromancer staff card (Worm Fulcrum)
necromancer-staff-card-worm-fulcrum.effects: Non-consumable special card that acts like a totem without using a totem slot; takes a card slot while active. Donut can imbue it with one of her spells before a fight and cast that spell once; its appearance changes based on the held spell
necromancer-staff-card-worm-fulcrum.description: The magical staff card Sharp-Elbows used to raise the zombie turkeys, now added to Donut's deck as a new totem-like card. She tested it by imbuing Clockwork Triplicate and used it on Geraldo, successfully making copies.

golden-combo.name: Golden Combo
golden-combo.effects: Combines cards like the earlier combo card, but is not consumable and can be reused indefinitely
golden-combo.description: A unique, reusable card-combination card looted from Sharp-Elbows' deck.

harley-davidson-1965-flh-electra-glide-p.name: Harley-Davidson 1965 FLH Electra Glide Panhead motorcycle
harley-davidson-1965-flh-electra-glide-p.effects: Stored permanently under Shitty Transportation Items rather than the floor's temporary category, meaning loot from this spliced-in area can be kept
harley-davidson-1965-flh-electra-glide-p.description: Carl's father's prized dented collector motorcycle, taken from the trailer yard outside the keyholder fight.

assorted-yard-and-household-loot-from-th.name: assorted yard and household loot from the trailer property
assorted-yard-and-household-loot-from-th.effects: Keepable loot from the time-spliced zone
assorted-yard-and-household-loot-from-th.description: Carl strips the yard: an old washing machine, several kids' bikes, rusty junk, the labeled flower pots (alliums, daffodils, asters, hyacinths), a shed of gardening supplies and soil bags, and the pieces of a laser-halved minivan. The Ford truck is too heavy to lift fully.

curio-figurines-and-dog-urn-chance.name: curio figurines and dog urn ("Chance")
curio-figurines-and-dog-urn-chance.description: Precious Moments figurines from the living room curio, plus an urn holding the ashes of a dog named Chance; Carl leaves the framed family photos except one.

photograph-of-asher.name: photograph of Asher
photograph-of-asher.description: The most recent photo of Carl's six-year-old half-brother Asher, taken from the curio.

marlboro-reds-cigarette-packs.name: Marlboro Reds cigarette packs
marlboro-reds-cigarette-packs.description: Three packs hidden in the bottom left dresser drawer of the master bedroom.

folder-of-court-documents.name: folder of court documents
folder-of-court-documents.description: A folder of court paperwork taken from the master bedroom without reading it.

9mm-glock-with-two-boxes-of-ammo.name: 9mm Glock with two boxes of ammo
9mm-glock-with-two-boxes-of-ammo.description: His father's only gun, plus two boxes of ammunition, from the master bedroom.

keys-to-the-motorcycle-and-ford-truck.name: keys to the motorcycle and Ford truck
keys-to-the-motorcycle-and-ford-truck.description: The keys matching the looted Harley and the three-tired Ford truck left in the yard.

master-bedroom-furnishings-and-clothes.name: master bedroom furnishings and clothes
master-bedroom-furnishings-and-clothes.description: The dresser with mirror, queen bed, cheap bedside tables, and all the clothes; wall photos of Asher are left behind.

centipede-arcade-cabinet.name: Centipede arcade cabinet
centipede-arcade-cabinet.description: An unplugged Centipede arcade cabinet crammed into the master bedroom, draped with clothes.

asher-s-room-belongings.name: Asher's room belongings
asher-s-room-belongings.description: A few toys, pants and shirts, and crayon drawings of airplanes and storm clouds; Carl leaves the boy's heart-shaped box of prized possessions.

floor-key.name: floor key
floor-key.effects: Key needed to descend to the ninth floor.
floor-key.description: The key dropped by the keymaster city boss; taken from the hydra's corpse right after the fight.

champion-pack.name: Champion Pack
champion-pack.effects: Adds a backup slot allowing two extra reserve totems (eight carried total, still six in the active deck) and includes two Flee cards, whose mechanics will change in the final phase.
champion-pack.description: A leather bag decorated with the team's boxer-pattern flag, given only to squads that defeat the keymaster. Contains the backup totem slots, a champion trophy for the personal space, a cooler of yellow Gatorade, and two Flee cards. Only Donut, as squad leader, could take it.

alpha-carl-totem-card.name: Alpha Carl (totem card)
alpha-carl-totem-card.effects: Level 69 totem modeled on Carl; cannot be ripped from the deck until another card is obtained.
alpha-carl-totem-card.description: The card added itself to Donut's cards against her wishes after she disposed of the other two totems.

leveled-up-frank-totem-card.name: Leveled-Up Frank (totem card)
leveled-up-frank-totem-card.effects: Level 90 Blood Assassin Night Elf totem modeled on dead crawler Frank Q; cannot be ripped until another card is obtained.
leveled-up-frank-totem-card.description: The card added itself to Donut's cards on its own even though Carl refused to keep it.

beach-battle-loot-monk-seal-and-land-cra.name: beach battle loot (monk seal and land crab corpses, gold, magical accessories)
beach-battle-loot-monk-seal-and-land-cra.description: A mile-long line of loot washed up on the Florida shoreline from a mass battle near Cuba: dozens to hundreds of dead level-65 monk seal warriors and crab pieces, an estimated nearly a million gold pieces, and hundreds of magical accessories. Carl began pulling the corpses into his inventory.

emergency-gold-benefactor-box.name: Emergency Gold Benefactor Box
emergency-gold-benefactor-box.description: Sent by Carl's sponsor, the Open Intellect Pacifist Network, Intergalactic NFC. It had been seized via Liaison action, but the seizure was overridden per Syndicate rule 855.C; the system suggested Carl open it alone.

trench-building-scrolls.name: trench-building scrolls
trench-building-scrolls.description: More of the trench-building scrolls Carl already collects, from a dozen adventurer boxes; exact quantity not stated.

turkey-deep-fryer.name: turkey deep fryer
turkey-deep-fryer.description: A deep fryer still in its packaging, from the turkey-shaped Gold Gobble Box earned in the turkey-barn fight.

jugs-of-peanut-oil.name: jugs of peanut oil
jugs-of-peanut-oil.description: Multiple jugs of peanut oil accompanying the deep fryer in the Gold Gobble Box; exact quantity not stated.

turkey-recipe-printout.name: turkey recipe printout
turkey-recipe-printout.description: A massive pile of papers printing out every recipe ever posted to a Facebook group, from the Gold Gobble Box.

upgrade-patch-burning-book.name: Upgrade Patch (burning book)
upgrade-patch-burning-book.effects: Grants the Book Burner benefit: once per day, force an opponent of Carl's level or lower to forget a spell for 30 hours (5% failure chance per opponent player level above his own); requires knowing the spell's exact name or targeting the last spell cast. The patch is destroyed if ever removed from a garment.
upgrade-patch-burning-book.description: A medium embroidered rectangular patch depicting a book on fire, from the Gold Talk of the Town Box. Affixing it to an eligible garment applies its upgrades.

wand-of-nighty-night.name: Wand of Nighty-Night
wand-of-nighty-night.effects: 10 charges. Casts a level-10 Sleep on a single non-boss target for five minutes; the target wakes if physically damaged, and multiple casts compound the effect.
wand-of-nighty-night.description: A gnarled wooden wand from the Gold Wand Box; Carl stored it in his inventory.

warrior-s-gold-helmet-of-resistance.name: Warrior's Gold Helmet of Resistance
warrior-s-gold-helmet-of-resistance.effects: Requires the wearer to wield a sword ('Warrior's' prefix), so Carl cannot use it; set aside for the gear pile for the next floor.
warrior-s-gold-helmet-of-resistance.description: An armor helmet from the hydra boss box, added to the growing stash of magical gear to hand out later.

the-shepherd-s-house-flyer.name: The Shepherd's House flyer
the-shepherd-s-house-flyer.description: The sole content of the benefactor box: a dirty, crumpled flyer for a homeless shelter in Homestead, Florida advertising Christmas breakfast at 7 AM, with the 7 AM circled in red and the 'No pets allowed' rule circled. Carl reads it as a summons to be there in about five hours, alone.

minor-find-crawler-skill-potion.name: Minor Find Crawler Skill potion
minor-find-crawler-skill-potion.effects: Drinking it grants level five in the Find Crawler skill; it does not work if that skill is already at level five or above.
minor-find-crawler-skill-potion.description: A skill potion from the Platinum That-Was-Disgusting box.

majorette-hat.name: majorette hat
majorette-hat.description: Donut's Christmas present 'from Santa': a majorette hat that Li Jun found on the previous floor.

homeless-man-s-plastic-storage-tub.name: homeless man's plastic storage tub
homeless-man-s-plastic-storage-tub.description: Sealed plastic storage bin belonging to the Residual known as Paulie/Goff, taken into inventory before its contents were examined.

wand-recharge-scroll.name: wand recharge scroll
wand-recharge-scroll.description: Scroll received as part of the trade compensation for the Residual's bin.

cracker-jack.name: Cracker Jack
cracker-jack.description: Three scrolls called Cracker Jack received in the trade with Orren for the Residual's bin.

valtay-perso-shield-platinum-edition.name: Valtay Perso-Shield, Platinum Edition
valtay-perso-shield-platinum-edition.effects: Round pinback button that takes no equipment slot; projects an inch-thick buffer against physical damage and some corporeal magic for about two minutes, then needs roughly an hour to recharge.

bull-rush-bespoke-personal-shield.name: Bull Rush, Bespoke Personal Shield
bull-rush-bespoke-personal-shield.effects: Opalescent longhorn-shaped pin attached to her collar; surrounds her in a moving bubble shield, reportedly more powerful than Carl's, with a mount option that can also cover Mongo or a vehicle at reduced strength.

enchanted-pauper-s-ring-of-the-steadfast.name: Enchanted Pauper's Ring of the Steadfast Emberus
enchanted-pauper-s-ring-of-the-steadfast.effects: Removes most health-seeping and disease-based debuff effects and numbs various other debuffs without removing them; can mute or overheat if overloaded. Only wearable by an Emberus worshipper; disguised as an enchanted Strength ring; carries the attached church debt and cannot be sold; curses non-worshippers with every debuff suffered within 50 meters.
enchanted-pauper-s-ring-of-the-steadfast.description: Carl's original strength ring re-enchanted at the Blessed Sun Enchantments shop to counter his slug condition, financed by a church loan.

stairwell-key-el-capitolio-nacional-de-c.name: stairwell key (El Capitolio Nacional de Cuba)

platinum-to-hell-in-a-handbasket-box.name: Platinum To Hell in a Handbasket Box
platinum-to-hell-in-a-handbasket-box.description: Platinum-tier loot box awarded by the Holy Hell! achievement; contents not yet opened.

quan-s-cloak.name: Quan's cloak
quan-s-cloak.effects: Earlier shown absorbing a Chain Lightning strike, leaving Quan unharmed
quan-s-cloak.description: A shining magical cloak blown off Quan by Jola's Fashion Critic spell; Carl scoops it into his inventory.

stairwell-key.name: stairwell key
stairwell-key.effects: Grants access to the ninth-floor exit stairwells
stairwell-key.description: Both of Quan's stairwell keys are awarded to Donut because she got credit for his kill.

velma.name: Velma
velma.effects: Enchanted flamethrower that is part breastplate, part weapon
velma.description: Ren's named flamethrower rig, unsnapped and given to Carl as she donates her gear before dying.

assorted-gear-from-ren-rings-clothing-po.name: assorted gear from Ren (rings, clothing, potions, scrolls including Eye in the Sky scrolls)
assorted-gear-from-ren-rings-clothing-po.effects: Includes multiple Eye in the Sky scrolls; count not stated
assorted-gear-from-ren-rings-clothing-po.description: Ren empties her inventory onto the floor and tells Carl to take everything as a donation to the war effort.

garret-tummy-acher-pet-in-pet-carrier.name: Garret (Tummy Acher pet, in pet carrier)
garret-tummy-acher-pet-in-pet-carrier.effects: Regenerating tank pet, reset to level 1; resets every five deaths and gains a new weakness each regeneration
garret-tummy-acher-pet-in-pet-carrier.description: Ren kills her pet with the Knife of Frozen Tears to reset him, then gives him to Carl to raise. Known weaknesses: sylvan crystal, consuming hobgoblin sweat, rivenwing bite, consuming undead flesh, and the new one, Reaper Gaze.

assorted-gear-from-khulan-rings-potions-.name: assorted gear from Khulan (rings, potions, glowing spear, armor items, unread magical tomes, coins)
assorted-gear-from-khulan-rings-potions-.description: Khulan also drops her equipment and a large amount of coins for Carl before the two women end their run.

ren-s-card-deck-all-non-totem-cards-incl.name: Ren's card deck (all non-totem cards, including Glow-Up and The Midnight Express)
ren-s-card-deck-all-non-totem-cards-incl.effects: Includes rare special and mystic cards; a consumable Glow-Up card and The Midnight Express consumable special card
ren-s-card-deck-all-non-totem-cards-incl.description: Ren hands over her entire card collection minus totems, meant for Donut. Carl must carry them physically (waistband) because they cannot enter his inventory.

quan-s-inventory-loot.name: Quan's inventory loot
quan-s-inventory-loot.effects: Includes many magical weapons (swords, spears, war hammers, a magical chainsaw), a high-value sock filled with nickels, a decapitating boomerang bowler hat, and almost five million gold coins
quan-s-inventory-loot.description: Carl transfers Quan's entire inventory to himself after the kill, leaving only the gold ring in Quan's hand. Much of it is loot Quan took from crawlers he killed.

build-trench-scroll.name: Build Trench scroll
build-trench-scroll.description: Carl reports the system has been seeding Build Trench scrolls into crawlers' inventories like the first-floor bandages and torches; he estimates he has 'like 50' (approximate count).

personal-space-upgrade-tier-3-doggie-doo.name: Personal Space Upgrade. Tier 3 Doggie Door
personal-space-upgrade-tier-3-doggie-doo.effects: Upgrades the personal-space door into a fast-travel waypoint system: exit through any door previously used on the same floor, with shared access for guests and party members.
personal-space-upgrade-tier-3-doggie-doo.description: A tier-3 personal space upgrade module dropped by Lucia Mar and handed to Carl by Li Jun; installed via the personal space tab of the interface. One was already installed in Imani's room for the Meadowlark party.

spunky-jefferson-the-enchanted-nickel-so.name: Spunky Jefferson the Enchanted Nickel Sock of the Elderly Miser

banjo-level-120-hell-scrub.name: Banjo – Level 120 Hell Scrub
banjo-level-120-hell-scrub.effects: A totem card for a gorilla-sized shadow-cloud creature with dark tentacles.
banjo-level-120-hell-scrub.description: Card stolen from Amayon's deck when Donut played The Thief; it flew into her hand. She later summons it and it saves Carl from the succubus.

leveled-up-frank-sober-edition-level-100.name: Leveled-Up Frank. Sober Edition. Level 100 Night Elf Blood Assassin

bottle-of-ink.name: bottle of ink
bottle-of-ink.effects: Magical black tattoo ink; Shi Maria says the remainder will be used for future power-sharing tattoos and warns not to poke too deep
bottle-of-ink.description: A beaker of bubbling black ink brewed by Shi Maria from Carl's own supplies while she controlled his body, used to tattoo the Eye of the Bedlam Bride.

eye-of-the-bedlam-bride-tattoo.name: Eye of the Bedlam Bride Tattoo
eye-of-the-bedlam-bride-tattoo.description: A self-administered stick-and-poke tattoo in the center of his chest, just above the sternum, inked with toraline-sliver ink.

celestial-benefactor-box.name: Celestial Benefactor Box
celestial-benefactor-box.effects: Unopened benefactor box; contents unknown this chapter
celestial-benefactor-box.description: Donut's first celestial-tier benefactor box, sent by the garbage truck company (Long Haul Biological Waste Management), received moments before the floor collapse.

containment-interface.name: Containment Interface
containment-interface.effects: Unregistered Valtay-built neural enhancement that adds a new menu giving access to the AI containment and the ability to trigger the system 'fuse' failsafe, which would send the system's star nova.
containment-interface.description: A bootleg brain-interface enhancement delivered via Paulie's coffee; installs over time and only works with a natural interface inside this system's enhancement zone.

multiple-items-looted-in-the-eighth-floo.name: Multiple items looted in the eighth-floor memory simulation

sushi-making-kit.name: sushi-making kit
sushi-making-kit.description: Gag reward from the Sushi Smoothie achievement.

ghillie-suit.name: Ghillie Suit
ghillie-suit.effects: Occupies her 'jacket' inventory slot but magically covers her whole body; adds a Blend into the Background skill and a Cone of Silence benefit; made of color-changing fabric that camouflages into surroundings.
ghillie-suit.description: Cat-sized camouflage suit that came with her Deathbed Hellcat class accessories; benefits stack further when worn with the matching hat.

floppy-boonie-hat.name: floppy boonie hat
floppy-boonie-hat.effects: Adds a Targeting skill for zooming in on distant mobs (like a gnomish farseerer) and further increases the range of all her spells; wearing hat and ghillie suit together buffs all benefits further, allowing line-of-sight sniping from over a mile away.
floppy-boonie-hat.description: Beige sniper-style boonie hat that came with her Deathbed Hellcat class accessories.

spellbook-of-the-floor-flak.name: Spellbook of the Floor (Flak)
spellbook-of-the-floor-flak.effects: Teaches the anti-air spell Flak when read; Mordecai says it is impressive at level 10. Not read yet in this chapter.
spellbook-of-the-floor-flak.description: Donut's Spellbook of the Floor prize, pulled from the back of the saferoom mailbox.

spellbook-shield.name: Spellbook: Shield
spellbook-shield.effects: Teaches a standard, genuine Shield spell; Mordecai notes she can chain it with her tech-based shield.
spellbook-shield.description: From her Platinum To Hell in a Handbasket box (received for looking at Amayon at the end of the previous floor); Carl received the same box.

spellbook-sentry.name: Spellbook: Sentry
spellbook-sentry.effects: Teaches Sentry, a war protection spell.
spellbook-sentry.description: From her Platinum Tserendolgor box, shaped like a jeweled German Shepherd with an army helmet.

unenchanted-cat-sized-kilt.name: unenchanted cat-sized kilt
unenchanted-cat-sized-kilt.description: A green and black tartan kilt of unclear purpose, from the Legendary Quest box for the Hell Comes to Crawler Town quest.

spellbook-you-re-not-done-yet.name: Spellbook: You're Not Done Yet
spellbook-you-re-not-done-yet.effects: Teaches You're Not Done Yet, a spell Mordecai describes as similar to Donut's Second Chance but far more insidious; he is visibly frightened of it and later advises against learning it.
spellbook-you-re-not-done-yet.description: A burned-looking black tome trailing purple smoke and black root-like tendrils, from the Hell Comes to Crawler Town Legendary Quest box.

the-tiara-of-a-thousand-lights.name: The Tiara of a Thousand Lights and Accompanying Charge Pillow
the-tiara-of-a-thousand-lights.effects: Base tiara gives +10 Charisma and holds 99 Absorption Jewels. Any enchanted hat left on the charge pillow for ten hours is drained of its magic (item ruined after even one hour); charging the tiara on the pillow transfers all stored buffs permanently, one jewel per buff, duplicates not combining. Temporary effects stay temporary and burn out their jewel; cursed, god-blessed, or debt-carrying items may not transfer properly.
the-tiara-of-a-thousand-lights.description: A custom sponsor item from Long Haul Biological Waste Management Solutions (a crest-owned company), delivered in a Celestial Benefactor box shaped like a transforming spaceship; modeled on the crest habitat Legend of a Thousand Lights. Can become one of the most powerful items in Dungeon Crawler World history with careful charging.

purple-charge-pillow.name: purple charge pillow
purple-charge-pillow.effects: Universal version of the charge pillow: drains any magical item over ten hours (item still ruined after one hour) but stored buffs transfer only to a like item type; transferring to an incompatible item loses the buff.
purple-charge-pillow.description: A second, nearly identical charging pillow from a Platinum benefactor box sent by the Apothecary.

the-cloak-of-the-benevolent-champion.name: The Cloak of the Benevolent Champion
the-cloak-of-the-benevolent-champion.effects: Unique celestial cloak: over 66 hours the wearer's lowest base stat rises to equal their highest base stat at time of first equip; grants a level-five Find Weakened skill (shows which of three-plus like creatures is nearest death); raises four random spells from the wearer's full arsenal to level 15 (one immediately, the rest over one week); raises one random skill to level 15 over 504 hours. Benefits require continuous wear — if another crawler equips it, all countdowns reset. First Celestial Item awarded in Dungeon Crawler World: Earth; will go to the museum display after the crawl, legacy stipend rules applying.
the-cloak-of-the-benevolent-champion.description: Quan's silky black-and-blue armed cloak, looted after his death (Donut got the kill credit); Carl gives it to Donut because it buffs the wearer's lowest stat. Its abilities custom-tailor to the wearer.

myspace-photo-potion.name: MySpace Photo potion
myspace-photo-potion.effects: Formerly called Charisma Cheat; for two minutes it tricks the system into reading Charisma as 30 points higher than it really is.
myspace-photo-potion.description: A potion Mordecai just crafted so the cloak locks onto an inflated 'highest stat' value.

boxes-of-ammunition.name: boxes of ammunition
boxes-of-ammunition.description: Approximately 2,000 rounds total ('maybe about 2,000' per the text), from Carl's Gold fan box.

unenchanted-leather-shoulder-holster.name: unenchanted leather shoulder holster
unenchanted-leather-shoulder-holster.description: Black detective-style gun belt with 'Carl' pressed into the leather, clearly meant for the father's Glock still in his inventory; Carl intends never to wear or use it.

loot-punch-card.name: Loot Punch Card
loot-punch-card.effects: A card with five spots for picking up Celestial items off the floor; one spot already punched (foot-shaped). Filling the card gives an unspecified special prize.
loot-punch-card.description: Physical card from the Celestial Loot achievement's gold box.

pet-biscuits.name: pet biscuits
pet-biscuits.description: Several pet biscuits from the Gold Pet Box; quantity unstated.

petscapes-coupon.name: PetScapes coupon
petscapes-coupon.effects: 500 gold off any one item at any PetScapes location in the dungeon (a store Carl has never heard of).
petscapes-coupon.description: Coupon from the Gold Pet Box.

automaton-table-supplies-xl.name: automaton table supplies (XL)
automaton-table-supplies-xl.effects: XL supply chest containing parts for a fabricator, something Carl has wanted to build for a while.
automaton-table-supplies-xl.description: From Carl's Platinum, dog-shaped Tserendolgor box.

upgrade-patch-small-reaper-spider-minion.name: Upgrade Patch. Small. (Reaper Spider Minion merchant)
upgrade-patch-small-reaper-spider-minion.effects: When affixed to an eligible garment: grants access to the Midnight Market ('safety or sanity not guaranteed'), notifications when traveling merchants are nearby, and +5% Dexterity. Fleeting item — removing it destroys it. Installing any patch also raises all of Carl's stats via his Scavenger's Daughter patch.
upgrade-patch-small-reaper-spider-minion.description: Black and silver die-cut patch of a traveling Reaper Spider Minion merchant carrying wicker baskets, from the Platinum Hell in a Handbasket box; not yet installed this chapter.

unenchanted-bagpipes.name: unenchanted bagpipes
unenchanted-bagpipes.description: Set of bagpipes from the Legendary Hell Comes to Crawler Town quest box.

third-tier-personal-space-upgrade-coupon.name: third-tier personal space upgrade coupons
third-tier-personal-space-upgrade-coupon.description: Three personal-space upgrade coupons from the Legendary Hell Comes to Crawler Town quest box.

enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-well-balanced.name: Enchanted Toe Ring of the Well-Balanced
enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-well-balanced.effects: Must be worn on the left pinky toe. Grants Heart Balance Level 5 (store one auto-trigger potion that fires at a chosen health threshold), Mind Balance Level 5 (protection from Enslavement and Conscription), and Physical Balance Level 5 (imbues the Extra Steady Balance skill and the Walk on Air skill, the latter noted as 'already obtained').
enchanted-toe-ring-of-the-well-balanced.description: A simple silver toe ring from the Legendary Spicy Box (Unbirthing achievement); its description says it was given to save Carl from danger he does not realize he is in.

hell-kissed-celestial-skill-potion-of-th.name: Hell-Kissed Celestial Skill Potion of the Prince of Woe
hell-kissed-celestial-skill-potion-of-th.effects: Grants a random level 10 to 15 Celestial-tier combat skill the drinker does not already have, drawn from any Sheol-level mob of level 200 or higher. The bottle talks in a demonic voice.
hell-kissed-celestial-skill-potion-of-th.description: Glittering black bubbling potion in a horned-skull bottle with a 'Drink Me' note, from the Celestial Heavy Metal Box (drum kit with Amayon-faced bass drums). Mordecai analyzes it first and learns the recipe.

corpse-of-herman-the-fleet-rigged-with-e.name: Corpse of Herman the Fleet (rigged with explosive satchels)
corpse-of-herman-the-fleet-rigged-with-e.effects: Bomb-laden ogre corpse; the satchels were destabilizing when taken
corpse-of-herman-the-fleet-rigged-with-e.description: Carl pulled the dead suicide-bomber ogre elite into his inventory to contain the destabilizing explosives before they detonated.

fancy-boy-s-wizard-hat.name: Fancy Boy's wizard hat
fancy-boy-s-wizard-hat.effects: Magical hat looted from the skunk battle wizard; Carl plans to drain its enchantment and discard it because it reeks
fancy-boy-s-wizard-hat.description: A blue conical wizard hat with a glittering star-and-planet pattern that sparkles, taken from the level 55 Skank Skunk Battle Wizard.

potion-of-somnolent-embrace.name: Potion of Somnolent Embrace
potion-of-somnolent-embrace.effects: Gently lulls the drinker to sleep, briefly easing worries and bad dreams.
potion-of-somnolent-embrace.description: A sleep-aid potion that soothes anxieties just long enough for rest to come.

meow-time-cat-food.name: Meow Time cat food
meow-time-cat-food.description: A cheap Earth-brand bag of cat food with a Pet Angels charity sticker, inexplicably sitting in Queen Imogen's kitchen; Carl grabs it as he escapes.

landmine-trap.name: landmine trap
landmine-trap.effects: explosive trap taken intact after being disarmed
landmine-trap.description: A landmine guarding the Prism castle door that Carl disables and pockets.

alarm-trap.name: alarm trap
alarm-trap.effects: traps that raise an alert when triggered
alarm-trap.description: Three alarm traps stolen from the Prism castle entrance.

guard-summoning-trap.name: guard summoning trap
guard-summoning-trap.effects: would normally summon four guards to its location; currently inert due to the teleportation ban
guard-summoning-trap.description: A summoning trap taken from the Prism castle door.

legendary-1914-box.name: Legendary 1914 Box
legendary-1914-box.description: Loot box awarded by the Gavrilo Princip achievement; not opened this chapter.

empress-d-nadia-s-corpse.name: Empress D'Nadia's corpse
empress-d-nadia-s-corpse.description: Donut loots the dead Empress (specific items not listed) and then takes the corpse itself into her inventory.

war-crime.name: War Crime
war-crime.effects: spellbook Donut has not yet read
war-crime.description: A spellbook from Donut's Legendary 1914 Box; the book itself bleeds and cries, which Donut finds disgusting.

dreadnaught-corpse.name: Dreadnaught corpse
dreadnaught-corpse.effects: Dead mercenary body with a sparking tech-based invisibility cloak still attached.
dreadnaught-corpse.description: The body of an invisible-cloaked Dreadnaught mercenary killed on the barracks roof, pulled into Carl's inventory.

madness-mask.name: Madness mask
madness-mask.effects: Strange faceless mask worn by the Madness Monkeywrencher disguised mercenaries.
madness-mask.description: A faceless disguise mask taken from the dead Dreadnaught mercenary on the roof.

piece-of-armor-weeper-tank.name: Piece of Armor (Weeper tank)
piece-of-armor-weeper-tank.effects: Captured war-machine inventory; captured items do not count against the inventory limit.
piece-of-armor-weeper-tank.description: Two enemy tanks called Weepers, formerly owned by the Operatic Collective, captured by Florin's forces and added to the warlord's inventory. High-tech tanks resembling WWI Mark V's with a top turret and anti-air tube.

king-rust-s-head.name: King Rust's head
king-rust-s-head.description: The severed head of King Rust of the Skull Empire, taken as battlefield loot after Rosetta used it for a retina scan.

king-rust-s-corpse.name: King Rust's corpse
king-rust-s-corpse.description: The headless body of King Rust, still wearing his magical gear, pulled into Carl's inventory during the retreat.

king-rust-s-necklace.name: King Rust's necklace
king-rust-s-necklace.description: A magical necklace taken from King Rust's body, along with several other magical items to be distributed later.

spellbook-of-run-little-gunter-run.name: Spellbook of Run, Little Günter, Run
spellbook-of-run-little-gunter-run.effects: Special Edition spellbook: teaches the spell at level 5 and it trains up faster than usual. The book is animate, with centipede legs, and leaks black liquid while begging not to be read.
spellbook-of-run-little-gunter-run.description: A living, whimpering spellbook from Carl's 1914 loot box that teaches the summoning spell Run, Little Günter, Run.

black-potion-balls.name: black potion balls
black-potion-balls.effects: Potion balls prepared by Mordecai for the assault; Carl is warned not to use them on more than two members of the same religion.
black-potion-balls.description: A batch of black potion balls Mordecai laid out on the saferoom table; Carl gathers them into his inventory, bringing his total to hundreds.

vinata-s-corpse.name: Vinata's corpse
vinata-s-corpse.description: The body of the slain naga princess Vinata, the Sepsis Whore, taken into inventory after the fight.

the-enchanted-spiked-knee-pads-the-munif.name: The Enchanted Spiked Knee Pads the Munificent Goddess Kina
the-enchanted-spiked-knee-pads-the-munif.effects: Divine item: +25% melee damage reflect, Breathe Underwater benefit, Level 15 Swim, Level 10 Iron Stomach (eat magic items to raise intelligence), Level 10 Eater of the Dead (eat corpses to randomly raise constitution/strength/dexterity); grants extra varying benefits near divine entities.
the-enchanted-spiked-knee-pads-the-munif.description: Knee pads made from the eyelashes of the sea goddess Kina, awarded to Katia from her celestial box. Narration says Katia has already unequipped and given them away, and the commentary makes clear they were intended for Carl to replace his similar existing knee pads, so Carl is the likely recipient.

the-enchanted-hairbrush-of-the-beefmaste.name: The Enchanted Hairbrush of the Beefmaster
the-enchanted-hairbrush-of-the-beefmaste.effects: Usable once per day on one male with hair 25 cm or longer; brushing for five minutes grants 30 hours of the Shining Charisma benefit (party members gain 20% of Carl's charisma), the Speedster benefit (very fast short-distance movement, combinable with his Gloom Wraith Phase), +25% constitution, and Level 10 Entourage (illusion that mirrors party members). The brush screams like a cheering crowd when used.
the-enchanted-hairbrush-of-the-beefmaste.description: A gold, jewel-handled magical hairbrush from Carl's celestial box earned in the Club Vanquisher fight — his third celestial item after the Scavenger's Daughter patch and the potion that gave him Gloom Wraith Phase. Its constitution boost makes it too valuable to give up, forcing Carl to keep his hair long.

skill-potion-pugilism.name: skill potion (Pugilism)
skill-potion-pugilism.description: A skill potion found in the platinum box that raised Carl's Pugilism skill.

wireless-pa-speaker-system-deluxe-packag.name: wireless PA speaker system (deluxe package)
wireless-pa-speaker-system-deluxe-packag.effects: Self-installing speaker network across the towers with more speakers than towers; interfaces with Donut's headset, provides a mixing board with distortion, delay, reverb and phaser controls, and lets her broadcast Bard spells.
wireless-pa-speaker-system-deluxe-packag.description: A deluxe public-address speaker system Donut bought and had installed on the tower network before the battle.

war-mage-bodies.name: war mage bodies
war-mage-bodies.effects: Corpses stored in the Graveyard section of Carl's inventory; war mage heads explode about ten minutes after death.
war-mage-bodies.description: The four dead war mages killed in the clearing, collected before their heads could detonate.

summon-traps.name: summon traps
summon-traps.effects: Two traps each summon a single specific guard (one inert because its guard is dead); the third summons fire ants.
summon-traps.description: Summon traps Carl pulled from the Madness minefield and stowed while sneaking toward the castle.

the-enchanted-tooth-cap-of-hi-yo-silver.name: The Enchanted Tooth Cap of Hi-Yo Silver
the-enchanted-tooth-cap-of-hi-yo-silver.effects: Installed on Rend's tooth; lets Carl summon Rend to his position from up to five kilometers away, three-minute cooldown; Rend may smash through walls on the way.
the-enchanted-tooth-cap-of-hi-yo-silver.description: A silver tooth cap for mounts, donated by another crawler and fitted over Rend's single round tooth by the crafting team.

advanced-alarm-trap.name: Advanced alarm trap
advanced-alarm-trap.description: Alarm traps guarding the surgical theater and throne room doors, disarmed and stored by Carl.

trap-door-trap.name: Trap door trap
trap-door-trap.description: Trap door traps set before the surgical theater and throne room entrances, taken into Carl's inventory.

white-cleaner-bot.name: White cleaner bot
white-cleaner-bot.description: A Viceroy cleaner bot, the same model as Carl's own but painted solid white, grabbed off the surgical theater floor and stored in inventory.

hell-kissed-potion-of-conscription.name: Hell-Kissed Potion of Conscription
hell-kissed-potion-of-conscription.effects: Works like a normal Conscription potion but hell-kissed; at level 18 it works on almost any non-affiliated entity regardless of protections, including most deities. Conscription potions only function on floors 9, 12, 15, 17, and 18.
hell-kissed-potion-of-conscription.description: A screaming glass bottle shaped like an eyeless demonic head with writhing tentacles, holding a roiling purple potion brewed in the Nothing by Krakaren Prime. Li Na duplicated it from the war mage's original using Li Jun's scroll of potion duplication.

stolen-catalytic-converter.name: Stolen catalytic converter
stolen-catalytic-converter.description: Prize attached to one of the two rude achievements Donut received for the dog tattoo.

stayfast-ink.name: Stayfast Ink
stayfast-ink.effects: Permanent ink that never fades under any circumstance
stayfast-ink.description: A wineskin holding 825 ml of ink, enough for several thick books; given to Carl by Milk so his writing (implied: in the Book of Voodoo/cookbook) will never disappear.

adept-s-fountain-pen.name: Adept's fountain pen
adept-s-fountain-pen.description: A basic calligrapher's fountain pen described by the system as the most versatile item anyone can own; given to Carl by Milk.

benefactor-box.name: Benefactor box
benefactor-box.description: An unopened benefactor box Donut received when Carl was not looking; Carl reasons it must be from the Apothecary since she has no second sponsor.

celestial-box.name: Celestial Box
celestial-box.description: Two more top-tier loot boxes awarded for her actions in the battle.

reaver-industries-security-sentry-dog-th.name: Reaver Industries Security Sentry Dog ("The Ditch Digger") with Mini Tactical Nuke
reaver-industries-security-sentry-dog-th.effects: Lion-sized tech-based security robot carrying an armed Mini Tactical Nuke anti-personnel explosive.
reaver-industries-security-sentry-dog-th.description: A large Reaver security robot dog, frozen solid by Elle's ice spell, that Carl scooped into his inventory before its nuke could detonate.

landmines.name: landmines
landmines.description: Landmines the Reavers had placed around a buried espresso-stand saferoom entrance; Carl gathers them up on arrival.

flesh-golem-body-of-princess-lunette.name: Flesh golem body of Princess Lunette

carl-s-book-of-boom.name: Carl's Book of Boom
carl-s-book-of-boom.effects: Second volume split from the Book of Voodoo; each page holds a skill personally donated by a departing crawler, and eating a page grants that skill briefly (e.g. one sampled page gives level 10 Lockpick for five minutes).
carl-s-book-of-boom.description: A roughly 500-page personal volume of donated skill pages with thank-you notes from crawlers Carl helped save.

a-banquet-fit-for-a-princess.name: A Banquet Fit for a Princess
a-banquet-fit-for-a-princess.effects: A book of skill and spell pages personally donated to Donut by departing crawlers; eating a page grants the skill or spell temporarily.
a-banquet-fit-for-a-princess.description: A tribute book filled with pictures, kind notes, and donated spells from crawlers leaving the dungeon.

knee-pads.name: knee pads
knee-pads.effects: Celestial-tier protective gear received from Katia that fits Donut.
knee-pads.description: Knee pads Katia passed on to Donut before leaving; a celestial item.

quadruped-weapon-mount-the-scorpion-s-bi.name: Quadruped weapon mount "The scorpion's bite"
quadruped-weapon-mount-the-scorpion-s-bi.effects: Non-magical bandolier accessory for a quadruped that mounts multiple ranged weapons, which can be fired and reloaded via inventory actions.
quadruped-weapon-mount-the-scorpion-s-bi.description: A back-worn weapon harness Donut received in the gold benefactor box from the Apothecary.

enchanted-repeating-crossbow-of-the-scav.name: Enchanted Repeating Crossbow of the Scavenger Mother of Mothers
enchanted-repeating-crossbow-of-the-scav.effects: Katia's large repeating crossbow; noted as stronger when women are in the wielder's party.
enchanted-repeating-crossbow-of-the-scav.description: A deadly enchanted repeating crossbow, bigger than Donut, formerly Katia's, now mounted on Donut's weapon harness.

body-of-huanxin-jinx.name: Body of Huanxin Jinx
body-of-huanxin-jinx.description: Carl loots Huanxin Jinx's corpse into his inventory, with Donut's loot permission, to avoid upsetting Eileithyia.

katia-s-card-depicting-annie-the-skull-h.name: Katia's card depicting Annie the skull-headed bird
katia-s-card-depicting-annie-the-skull-h.description: A card from the previous floor showing Annie the skull-headed bird, left behind on the saferoom table by Katia; Donut takes it.

big-shot-chicken-food-truck.name: Big Shot Chicken food truck
big-shot-chicken-food-truck.effects: The party's racing vehicle for the tenth floor's seven heats; repaired to 100% between heats while the garage attendant lives; cannot be stolen or teleported.
big-shot-chicken-food-truck.description: A roughly twenty-foot food truck themed after the Big Shot Chicken restaurant chain, with a faux-1920s chrome front end and a large plastic tommy gun on the roof that partially blocks the windshield.

tie-dyed-headband.name: tie-dyed headband
tie-dyed-headband.description: A tie-dyed headband discarded in disgust by Jasha the bugbear; Donut quietly takes it into her inventory.

avernus-creeper-thorn.name: Avernus Creeper Thorn
avernus-creeper-thorn.effects: Each thorn can grow into a full-sized Avernus Creeper plant, one of the most invasive species in the universe, though they will not sprout on this floor.
avernus-creeper-thorn.description: Multiple thorns plucked from Rend's skin after Team Sparkles' plant mount ran him over; Mordecai asks Carl to save them all and keep them in inventory until reaching the safe room. Exact quantity not stated.

oozy-form.name: Oozy Form
oozy-form.effects: Special edition spell book that trains its spell faster than normal.
oozy-form.description: A living, slime-oozing spell book that arrives via the safe room mailbox and scurries around the room; Donut declines it, so Carl takes it.

the-assassin-s-veil.name: The Assassin's Veil
the-assassin-s-veil.effects: Precognition Benefit: warns wearer if anyone nearby is preparing an offensive or binding spell or readying a skill. Only wearable by a female of a Feline-class species; enemies of Sekhmet become automatically hostile to the wearer.
the-assassin-s-veil.description: Egyptian-style beaded headdress worn by the court of the goddess Sekhmet, meant to warn off those who would harm the bearer. One of Donut's class-accessory hats.

the-nemes-of-pestilence.name: The Nemes of Pestilence
the-nemes-of-pestilence.effects: Pet-only accessory. Eternal Protector of Rage: pet becomes invulnerable for 60 seconds (once per day) and gains 2x strength when within three meters of an owner whose health is below 10%. Bringer of Death: pet bites have a 75% chance to inflict the Cruel Sepsis debuff.
the-nemes-of-pestilence.description: A pharaoh-style striped fabric nemes hat intended for Donut's pet Mongo; one of the class-accessory hats.

the-wrap-of-sekhmet-s-compassion.name: The Wrap of Sekhmet's Compassion
the-wrap-of-sekhmet-s-compassion.effects: Pet-only accessory. Eternal Protector of Health: pet becomes invulnerable for 60 seconds (once per day) and gains the Heal Party Member skill when within three meters of an owner whose health is below 10%. Bringer of Resurrection: fatal bites have a 75% chance to cast a level 10 Raise Bootlicker on the corpse.
the-wrap-of-sekhmet-s-compassion.description: A red patterned headband intended for the pet Rend; one of the class-accessory hats.

emergency-gremlin.name: Emergency Gremlin
emergency-gremlin.effects: Summoning scroll: the summoned gremlin can repair a vehicle that breaks down mid-race; this variety can also fix biological mounts.
emergency-gremlin.description: Heavy green metal-like summoning scrolls; Donut receives roughly twenty of them (exact count not stated) across many boxes.

spell-book-unidentified.name: spell book (unidentified)
spell-book-unidentified.description: The legendary default winner box contains a spell book that quickly disappears into Donut's inventory; it is identified in the next chapter.

the-danforth.name: The Danforth
the-danforth.effects: Enchanted crossbow bolt that does no physical damage but anchors the struck body part in place for (2 seconds x the shooter's Crossbow skill level), with messy physics if the target is moving.
the-danforth.description: A quiver holding about ten glittering crossbow bolts (count approximate) from the legendary Lorena Bobbitt box.

the-crupper-of-the-benevolent-champion.name: Crupper of the Benevolent Champion
the-crupper-of-the-benevolent-champion.effects: Matching item to Donut's champion cloak (abilities revealed next chapter).
the-crupper-of-the-benevolent-champion.description: A plain silver fine chain mail crupper from the celestial slap-chop box; Mordecai identifies it as a match to the Cloak of the Benevolent Champion.

claw-caps.name: claw caps
claw-caps.effects: Translucent red caps that mute the claws' glow (appearing purple); can be removed and re-armed at will via inventory, protecting against accidental cuts.
claw-caps.description: Rosetta buys four claw caps from the safe room shop interface so Donut's boon claws don't destroy things by accident.

spray-paint-can-white.name: spray paint can (white)
spray-paint-can-white.description: An unenchanted can of white spray paint from the Bronze Vanessa Williams Box.

bomb-blanket.name: Bomb Blanket
bomb-blanket.effects: Throwable potion that prevents smaller bombs from detonating if tossed onto them in time.
bomb-blanket.description: Five Bomb Blanket potions from the Bronze Daddy's Little Secret Box; Mordecai had mentioned them before.

trollish-boom-box.name: Trollish Boom Box
trollish-boom-box.effects: Upgraded version of the hob-lobber; a thrown explosive shaped like a shoebox on a stick.
trollish-boom-box.description: New explosive type received from the boxes; Carl plans to test them in his bomber's studio first.

hotlist-expansion.name: Hotlist Expansion
hotlist-expansion.effects: Skill potion: drinking it expands the 10-space hotlist into 20 spaces.
hotlist-expansion.description: Skill potion from the Gold Payload Box (WWII gravity-bomb shaped); not drunk this chapter.

quadruped-baby-carrier-backpack.name: quadruped baby carrier backpack
quadruped-baby-carrier-backpack.effects: Carries a quadruped buckled to Carl's chest; while worn, the passenger transforms along with Carl's Phase or Oozy spells.
quadruped-baby-carrier-backpack.description: A pink chest-mounted carrier from a Gold Adventurer Box, intended for carrying Donut; she refuses to use it.

rishi-s-household-junk.name: Rishi's household junk
rishi-s-household-junk.description: The legendary Naga fan box (cobra-head shaped, earned before executing Rishi) dumps a ceiling-high mountain of fire-damaged garbage from Rishi's home into the safe room: wrecked furniture, clothes, electronics, a GreaserBall participation trophy, and bootleg Princess Donut makeup palettes. The AI says most is junk but a useful thing or two may be hidden inside.

peach-flavored-busch-light.name: peach-flavored Busch Light
peach-flavored-busch-light.description: Radoslav tosses Carl a peach-flavored beer from the bugbears' magically refilling cooler; Carl declines to drink it but keeps the can in his inventory.

busch-light.name: Busch Light
busch-light.description: Radoslav hands Carl a regular Busch Light from the never-ending cooler.

trucker-hat-i-d-hang-a-little-with-chad.name: trucker hat ('I'd Hang a Little with Chad')
trucker-hat-i-d-hang-a-little-with-chad.description: A cheap green-and-yellow trucker hat bought from a Hungry Eyes hat vendor; Donut wants it for her mood wall.

beer.name: beer
beer.description: A bottle of beer Imani slides over to Carl at the Lollipop bar table.

shirley-temple-with-extra-cherries.name: Shirley Temple with extra cherries
shirley-temple-with-extra-cherries.description: A bowl containing a Shirley Temple with extra cherries that Imani pushes toward Donut.

device-from-hedy.name: device from Hedy
device-from-hedy.description: An unspecified device Donut confirms she picked up from Hedy before the race; its function is not described in this chapter.

donut-drive-system.name: Donut drive system
donut-drive-system.effects: Lever rig attached to the steering wheel letting Donut steer by rocking back and forth, with sticks that press the pedals.
donut-drive-system.description: A janky cat-driving apparatus built by the team's mechanic over a few hours; it now sits in Donut's inventory awaiting refinement by Carl.

enchanted-juvenile-macropus-dominus-skin.name: Enchanted Juvenile Macropus Dominus Skin
enchanted-juvenile-macropus-dominus-skin.effects: Fills a costume slot as an outer garment; grants Protection from Seeping Acid and a level 10 Jump skill; 12-hour timed item
enchanted-juvenile-macropus-dominus-skin.description: The skin of a dead joey from the boss's pouch, wearable as a full-body kangaroo costume, pulled off the engineer's corpse that Genesis retrieved.

platinum-boss-box.name: Platinum Boss Box
platinum-boss-box.description: Boss-kill loot box awarded to each of Carl and Donut for killing Prison Pocket.

c-w-u-invoice-note.name: C&W&U invoice note
c-w-u-invoice-note.effects: Carries a hidden message readable only via Carl's Escape Plan skill
c-w-u-invoice-note.description: A blank shop invoice handed over by a generated gremlin, stating the C&W&U shop opens after the third heat; its hidden message from Akuma demands Carl kill two teams next heat and offers smuggling friends to the Pineapple Cabaret.

big-shot-chicken-job-application.name: Big Shot Chicken job application
big-shot-chicken-job-application.description: A non-magical, bent, half-shredded, stained fast-food job application received as the '76 Buccaneers achievement reward.

skill-potion-platinum-hotlist-expansion.name: Skill Potion. Platinum Hotlist Expansion
skill-potion-platinum-hotlist-expansion.effects: Expands the 20-space hotlist to 25 spaces and unlocks the Contingency skill for programmable hotlist automations
skill-potion-platinum-hotlist-expansion.description: An upgrade potion from Carl's Platinum Boss Box that enlarges his hotlist and adds automation capability.

smart-companion-royal-pet-throne-ultimat.name: Smart Companion Royal Pet Throne, Ultimate Diamond Pampered Pet Princess edition
smart-companion-royal-pet-throne-ultimat.effects: Gyro-stabilizing pet seat that protects its assigned princess in crashes, monitors vitals, offers stress relief, feeds snacks on demand, and talks (Donut named it Dorota).
smart-companion-royal-pet-throne-ultimat.description: A talking pet car seat, a real commercial product in the wider universe, installed in the food truck's passenger seat for Donut; it manifests a fluffy bed when its assigned princess sits in it.

note-for-carl.name: Note for Carl
note-for-carl.description: A message from Akuma scrawled in black crayon on a kiddie menu, looted from the dead upgrade imp's counter, telling Carl he was supposed to come alone.

disintegration-trap-module.name: disintegration trap module
disintegration-trap-module.effects: A trap module Carl deactivated and collected from the hidden door behind the poster.
disintegration-trap-module.description: The hidden disintegration trap that had been rigged to the secret door in the upgrade room; Carl disarmed and looted it.

bucket-of-secretions.name: bucket of secretions
bucket-of-secretions.effects: Drinking about half a potion bottle's worth grants roughly 30 minutes of protection against hostile environments/toxic air.
bucket-of-secretions.description: A bucket of foul-smelling white liquid labeled 'secretions', sold by bat vendors as acid-rain/air countermeasure; Louis purchased it for Carl.

mehmet-s-page-from-carl-s-book-of-boom.name: Mehmet's page from Carl's Book of Boom

lucienne-s-racing-helmet.name: Lucienne's racing helmet
lucienne-s-racing-helmet.effects: Cursed with 'Michale Graves's disease'; Donut declares it worthless.
lucienne-s-racing-helmet.description: The small, cracked racing helmet of Lucienne of team Sparkles, fished out of the gore in the truck after she was killed at the finish line.

bautista-s-breakfast-blend-tea.name: Bautista's breakfast blend tea
bautista-s-breakfast-blend-tea.effects: Grants the drinker's vehicle or mount a 25% constitution buff; the system classifies it as a potion, so it counts against potion cooldowns.
bautista-s-breakfast-blend-tea.description: A cup of Bautista's guild breakfast tea infused with special tea leaves from a local vendor, served at the farewell gathering.

hidden-can-bomb-planted-in-team-free-lov.name: hidden can-bomb (planted in Team Free Love's cooler)

dwight-s-magical-horn.name: Dwight's magical horn
dwight-s-magical-horn.description: The magical horn of the unicorn racer Dwight, taken from his head and placed in Donut's inventory immediately after she killed him at the race start.

wide-brimmed-brown-hat.name: wide-brimmed brown hat
wide-brimmed-brown-hat.description: A wide-brimmed brown hat Donut pulls from the safe room mailbox and squeals over; consistent with her new class's Gurkha hats described later.

tome-of-gatekeeper.name: Tome of Gatekeeper

stats.duration: (5 minutes + 1 minute per level of spell) × [Your Intelligence / (Target's Constitution + Charisma)]

tome-of-gatekeeper.effects: Removes the target's ability to use any class-associated skills or abilities for a short time; against a deity it temporarily strips their Worshipper Base Associated skill and spell set. Valid targets: any opponent, NPC, deity, or OI entity.
tome-of-gatekeeper.description: Donut's new spell book, an unusual paperback marked as an Advance Reader Copy: it can be installed now but will not function until the 12th floor. She has not yet read or installed it.

legendary-box.name: Legendary Box
legendary-box.description: A legendary loot box awarded for spotting the shadow mimic before it revealed itself.

carrier-backpack.name: carrier backpack
carrier-backpack.description: A carrier backpack Mordecai finished making for Carl; it sits waiting on Carl's crafting table.

legendary-steed-saddle-upgrade.name: Legendary Steed (saddle upgrade)
legendary-steed-saddle-upgrade.effects: Upgrade for Mongo's saddle allowing a cart to be attached to him; the cart adds no weight and Mongo can still jump.
legendary-steed-saddle-upgrade.description: A saddle upgrade for Donut's pet Mongo obtained while opening her loot boxes.

gurkha-class-outfits-for-mercenaries-and.name: Gurkha class outfits for mercenaries and pets
gurkha-class-outfits-for-mercenaries-and.effects: The camel-colored wide-brimmed Gurkha hats raise multiple melee-fighting skills of the mercenary wearers by three levels; matching black-and-gold mount blankets boost mount constitution by 20% and grant the Steady Charge skill.
gurkha-class-outfits-for-mercenaries-and.description: A set of wild clothing Donut received via her new Gurkha class for outfitting her mercenaries, pets, and mounts; the hats resize themselves to fit each wearer.

kukri-claw-caps.name: kukri claw caps
kukri-claw-caps.effects: Foot caps for Mongo that greatly increase his slicing ability.
kukri-claw-caps.description: Special claw caps fitted to Donut's pet Mongo's feet for the coming fight; part of the Gurkha class gear.

bristle-quill.name: bristle quill
bristle-quill.description: A dripping quill yanked from Cerni Pinta, a tortured bristle alien tourist hanging in webbing inside Club Scolopendra.

enchanted-spiked-kneepads-of-the-munific.name: Enchanted Spiked Kneepads of the Munificent Goddess Kina

memorial-crystal-emberus.name: Memorial Crystal. Emberus.
memorial-crystal-emberus.description: An amber-hued glowing crystal found half buried in the arena dirt after Emberus's death.

events.description: Plain boxer shorts Carl was wearing when the world collapsed.
events.evidence: wearing boxers, leather jacket, pink Crocs
wearing boxers, leather jacket, pink Crocs

events.description: Heavy leather jacket grabbed on the way out into the freezing night.
events.evidence: pulled a heavy leather jacket on
pulled a heavy leather jacket on

events.description: His ex-girlfriend's too-small pink Crocs — the only footwear available.
events.evidence: squeezed my feet into a pair of my ex-girlfriend's Crocs
squeezed my feet into a pair of my ex-girlfriend's Crocs
chapter opens with Dungeon Floor 1 message
Carl focuses on Donut in entry hallway
crawler designation message after entering hallway

events.name: Crazy Cat Lady
events.description: Awarded for entering the World Dungeon with a cat in tow.
events.evidence: achievement wall right after entering dungeon

events.description: Unopened pet-themed loot box awarded for the Crazy Cat Lady achievement.
events.evidence: reward line of Crazy Cat Lady achievement

events.name: Trailblazing Crazy Cat Lady
events.description: Awarded for being the very first crawler to enter the World Dungeon accompanied by a cat.
events.evidence: second achievement in entry achievement wall

events.description: Unopened top-tier pet loot box from the first-cat-in-dungeon achievement.
events.evidence: reward line of Trailblazing Crazy Cat Lady

events.name: Early Adopter
events.description: Awarded for being among the first 5,000 crawlers to enter a new World Dungeon.
events.evidence: third achievement in entry achievement wall

events.description: Unopened adventurer loot box from the Early Adopter achievement.
events.evidence: reward line of Early Adopter achievement

events.name: Empty pockets
events.description: Awarded for entering the dungeon with no supplies at all.
events.evidence: fourth achievement in entry achievement wall

events.description: Unopened adventurer loot box from the Empty pockets achievement.
events.evidence: reward line of Empty pockets achievement

events.name: Why aren't you wearing pants?
events.description: Awarded for entering the dungeon without pants on.
events.evidence: fifth achievement in entry achievement wall

events.description: Unopened clothing loot box from the no-pants achievement.
events.evidence: reward line of no-pants achievement

events.name: Unarmed combat
events.description: Awarded for entering the World Dungeon without bringing a weapon.
events.evidence: sixth achievement in entry achievement wall

events.description: Unopened weapon loot box from the Unarmed combat achievement.
events.evidence: reward line of Unarmed combat achievement

events.name: Loner
events.description: Awarded for entering the dungeon without any human companions; the system mocks the choice.
events.evidence: final achievement in entry achievement wall

events.name: Fall into an obvious trap
events.description: Awarded for stepping into a baited dark alley; the reward text is only a taunt.
events.evidence: stepping into Da Tutorial Guild alley trap

events.cause: Both sandals flew off while fleeing the Goblin Murder Dozer.
events.evidence: Crocs go flying while running from dozer

events.name: You've discovered and read an official dungeon sign
events.description: Awarded for reading official dungeon signage for the first time.
events.reward: All official dungeon signage now highlighted; nearby guilds appear on the minimap.
events.evidence: reading Tutorial Guild sign near junction

events.text: A batch of new achievement and other notifications appeared (queued unread in corner of vision) after Donut slipped into the guild during the goblin fight; contents not shown.
events.evidence: notification wall while goblins attack at guild door

events.text: Carl kills the two remaining goblins and the Murder Dozer explodes; new notifications appear on his screen but their contents are locked behind the unfinished tutorial and never shown.
events.evidence: after killing goblins on the murder dozer

events.text: Mordecai grants Carl access to the Crawler Menu; health bar, minimap, and notification folder appear on his HUD.
events.evidence: Mordecai starts tutorial, Crawler Menu granted

events.text: Goblin Pass examined: a permanent forearm tattoo that cannot be hidden without a purchased coverup sleeve and shows through armor. events.effects: Negates all melee-based damage debuffs (Stun, Knockback, Disarm, Out-of-Breath); does not provoke a negative reaction from trolls. Mordecai calls it a top under-armor tanking garment, normally Platinum/Legendary box loot, returning the wearer to full health in as little as two minutes.

events.effects: Grants resistance to poison and ice-based attacks and adds Anti-Piercing resistance to all worn armor; a Nightgaunt that sees the wearer may react badly.

events.item: Enchanted Crown of the Sepsis Whore
events.effects: A Fleeting, Unique item: it crumbles to dust if removed and a replacement generates elsewhere in the dungeon. All attacks (including magical) gain a 15% chance to inflict the Sepsis debuff. Wearing it permanently places the wearer in the royal line of succession of the Blood Sultanate on the ninth floor; royals must slay the Sultan and all other royal family members before descending to the tenth floor, and removing the crown does not remove this status.
events.description: A small cat-sized jeweled tiara with dark, smoky gems and a swirling purple center stone, from a Legendary Quadruped Box awarded for being the first cat to enter the dungeon.

events.name: Dungeonpreneur

events.reward: One gold coin per kill other crawlers make with the invented device; if Carl survives the dungeon, the payments continue for life (even across future seasons) at the prevailing gold-to-credit exchange rate.

events.description: A couple hundred gold coins from the loot boxes; exact amount unstated, bringing the party hoard to just over 1,000 coins.

item-based spell from BigBoi Boxers, moved to hotlist
events.description: Item-based spell requiring no mana; lost if the boxers are unequipped. Creates a static sphere blocking physical attacks and mob bodies (not magic or non-corporeal entities). Radius 3 meters + 50 centimeters per intelligence level (current 4.5 meters); duration 5 seconds + 1 second per spell level (current 20 seconds); 30-hour cooldown.

events.text: Carl and Donut are automatically enrolled in the Crawler Assisted Outreach Program and assigned Zev of Borant as their PR agent; she opens a dedicated chat channel to them in their interface. Odette holds first right of refusal for interviews at each floor opening, with at least one additional interview obligated per floor.

events.cause: Opened; each box contained a lottery-style scratch-off ticket.

events.effects: Scratching one of its six spots applies a random effect to her next attack against a mob; one hour cooldown between scratches; cannot be used inside a saferoom.
events.description: A red-and-green scratch-off ticket depicting a troll-like creature tossing gold coins. Roughly twenty possible symbols, mostly beneficial (e.g. mob drops 5,000 coins, doubled damage against the mob) but some harmful (e.g. mob splits in two, mob becomes invulnerable for 30 seconds).
events.evidence: Donut's ticket from platinum box, description read aloud

events.effects: Scratching a spot mid-battle casts a spell at the closest red-tagged mob: 50/50 chance of a level-15 Fireball or a glob of healing Strawberry Custard; thirty minute cooldown between scratches.

events.effects: Launches wheeled bombs backward from the moving chopper via a gravity-fed ramp; includes two side chambers with lever-operated drop doors.

events.evidence: five-hour launcher build in Big Shot Chicken

events.effects: Remote-detonated via a magical trigger held by Carl.
events.description: The largest wheeled bomb, kept in Donut's inventory for launching: three boom jugs, a clay jug of goblin oil, a small jar of gunpowder, and the last of Carl's hobgoblin pus, rolling on free weights used as wheels.

events.effects: Ignite on impact or when hit with a low-powered magic missile.

events.effects: Wick-lit shrapnel grenades; Donut can light the wicks mentally like torches thanks to her quadruped status.
events.description: A few gunpowder-and-shrapnel wheeled bombs with long fuses, made specifically to rip open grub pupa sacs; exact quantity not stated.
events.evidence: gunpowder babies made for the final stretch

events.effects: Last-resort explosives deemed too dangerous for Donut to carry.

events.cause: Donut gave Mordecai permission to use her two coupons to upgrade the alchemy table to level three.
events.evidence: Mordecai's upgraded alchemy table in personal space

events.effects: Combines a Gold Standard Healing Potion and Troll's Second Wind for near-immortality via constant healing and regeneration for 30 seconds; does not stop instant-death effects; adds about 10 hours to potion sickness; only two can ever be taken.
events.description: A custom emergency potion brewed by Mordecai on the level-three alchemy table.
events.evidence: Mordecai presents crafted potions at counter

events.effects: Permanently raises Constitution by a random amount from one to four; only two may ever be drunk in the dungeon.
events.description: Two stat potions Mordecai brewed specifically for Donut.
events.evidence: stat potions slid over to Donut

events.cause: First Superb Constitution Buff Potion raised it by one point.
events.evidence: Donut complains potion only gave one point

events.cause: Second Superb Constitution Buff Potion raised it by three, for a net gain of four.
events.evidence: second potion after countdown wore off

events.cause: Donut drank both potions to permanently raise her Constitution.
events.evidence: both stat potions drunk at counter

events.text: With her anklet, her brush buff, and a temporary 10-point Hooligan class boost active only on this floor, Donut's effective Constitution totaled 21.
events.evidence: constitution math after drinking potions

events.effects: A full potion vial can be transferred into the ball, which can then be thrown at enemies to apply the potion on impact; breaks if thrown too hard.
events.description: About twenty rubbery avocado-like throwing balls Mordecai crafted, meant to pair with Carl's xistera and attack potions.
events.evidence: pile of green balls on the counter

events.text: Carl received a handful of unnamed achievements during the day, only one of which awarded a prize box.
events.evidence: narration before Gold Looter box prize

events.effects: Raises the Determine Value skill.
events.description: A skill potion awarded from a Gold Looter box.
events.evidence: Gold Looter box prize

events.cause: Carl drank the skill potion immediately.
events.evidence: drunk right after receipt
after drinking, inventory now shows item rarity

events.text: With the improved Determine Value skill, Carl's Stuffed Kimaris Figure showed as his only Unique item and remained his most valuable; Carl's Doomsday Scenario was listed as Pretty Damn Rare and least valuable.
events.evidence: filtering inventory by rarity

events.effects: Adjusted to fit cat species; magically affixes to a Mongoliensis as a mount; rider also gains Anti-Piercing Resistance.
events.description: A garish blue and red tasseled saddle from Donut's Platinum fan box, letting her ride Mongo.
events.evidence: Donut's Platinum fan box prize
Carl clicks saddle onto Mongo's back

events.text: Mordecai attacked the Prize Carousel host Chaco, was flagged Naughty, and was put in a seven-day timeout; the manager room vanished, leaving the party without their manager for most of the remaining floor time.

events.effects: Officially described as a humorous chicken-and-goblin recipe book.

events.text: Carl's Escape Plan skill buzzed on opening the Cookbook, revealing hidden text addressing him directly and declaring the book's true purpose is to help burn the system down.
events.evidence: hidden words appear on first page

events.text: The Dungeon Anarchist's Cookbook is revealed as a 24th-edition hidden guide (potions, explosives, traps, secret societies, shortcuts) passed between crawls; its hidden text is invisible to showrunners and viewers but not the System AI, erases if Carl reveals its existence, and the book leaves his inventory on death or retirement.
events.evidence: book's introductory pages after opening

events.text: Carl gained an extra page in his messaging-menu scratchpad; anything written on it will be included in the book's 25th edition.
events.evidence: new scratchpad tab per book instructions

events.text: System announcement: a new sponsor interaction tab will appear in the interface, populating when sponsorship bidding ends in about 15 hours; some train lines will be down for maintenance.
