events.text: Quest Complete: The Gate of the Feral Gods. Carl assembled all three pieces (two watches plus winding box) and activated the artifact, opening a twenty-foot teleport portal that moved his group from the ocean floor to the beach outside Pandinus.
events.evidence: activating winding box on ocean floor

events.cause: Taken on the beach to stave off decompression vomiting.
events.evidence: after teleporting to Pandinus beach

events.name: Who Let the Gods Out?
events.description: Awarded for allowing a feral god to enter the current realm; the system compares it to dropping a grenade down your own pants and warns that other gods dislike feral gods and tend to react.
events.reward: No tangible reward; the system quips the aftermath will make the Dungeon Crawler World blooper reel.
events.evidence: achievement popup while fleeing into Pandinus

events.cause: Went up three levels from the Lusca city boss battle.
events.evidence: Carl reviews levels in the safe room

events.cause: Went up one level from shared experience for the Lusca kill (same level-up Donut announced during the fight).
events.evidence: Carl notes Donut was level 39

events.name: Rock Bottom!
events.description: Awarded for diving more than 1,000 meters below the surface and surviving without a deep-diving suit.
events.evidence: achievement review in safe room

events.name: Fight the Power!
events.description: Awarded seven times at once, one for each plaintiff. Carl and the AI were named co-defendants in a Syndicate Court action brought by third parties and won; the AI notes crawlers usually lose such suits.
events.evidence: achievement review in safe room

events.name: Janet Jackson's Nipple!
events.description: Awarded for being featured during a live special event broadcast, likened to playing the Super Bowl halftime show.
events.reward: Platinum Fan Box; viewer voting enabled on its prize, box opens in 30 hours.
events.evidence: achievement review in safe room

events.name: Soft Vore!
events.description: Awarded for being swallowed whole by a much larger monster and escaping without being chewed; delivered in the AI's disturbingly enthusiastic voice. Katia did not receive this one.
events.evidence: achievement review in safe room

events.name: Flex in the City!
events.description: Awarded for killing a city boss with five or fewer crawlers participating.
events.reward: None additional; the AI points to the boss box already received.
events.evidence: achievement review in safe room

events.text: Carl received about 10 other minor achievements (mostly for activity on the ocean floor), granting a handful of regular Adventurer and I'm Wet boxes, plus his first Gold Boss Box for the Lusca kill.
events.evidence: after the named achievements list

events.text: Adventurer and bronze I'm Wet boxes yielded mostly common loot: junk clothing and weapons, healing potions, and mana restoration potions. A handful more Water Breathing scrolls came from the bronze I'm Wet boxes.
events.evidence: opening minor loot boxes

events.effects: Grants invisibility when drunk.
events.description: A single invisibility potion found among the minor box loot.
events.evidence: opening minor loot boxes

events.effects: Explosive.
events.description: Hobgoblin dynamite received among the minor box loot; quantity not stated.
events.evidence: opening minor loot boxes

events.effects: Stronger tier of healing potion.
events.description: A new, higher tier of healing potion starting to appear in Carl's loot; Mordecai says it signals good progression. Quantity not stated.
events.evidence: opening minor loot boxes

events.effects: If a pack of dinosaurs attacks the party, they eat the drinker last; lasts 30 hours.
events.description: An ominous consumable that makes attacking dinosaurs save the drinker for last. Mordecai reacted with an 'uh-oh'.
events.evidence: concerning potion from minor boxes

events.description: One gold piece from each of the seven Silver Summary Judgement boxes.
events.evidence: opening seven Summary Judgement boxes

events.effects: Each certificate is one share of common stock in a publicly-traded company based in the home system of one of the seven lawsuit plaintiffs; all traded via the I.R.F. exchange and averaging about 10 credits per share.
events.description: Seven physically varied stock certificates (paper, an inscribed egg, printed glass). The first is one share of Sigmund Textiles Foundry in the Gun-ya system of the Skull Empire, last recorded at 10.422 credits per share. Mordecai notes crawlers should not be able to hold anything with credit value.
events.evidence: opening seven Summary Judgement boxes

events.effects: Non-magical 8x10 photos identifying the mothers of the seven Syndicate Court plaintiffs, effectively revealing which factions sued for the gate.
events.description: Seven ordinary photographs, each portraying the mother of a lawsuit plaintiff, e.g. Queen Consort Ugloo of the Skull Empire (mother of Prince Stalwart) and Epitome Noflex of the Dream. Mordecai warns Carl to keep them in his inventory.
events.evidence: opening seven Summary Judgement boxes

events.effects: Wearer can breathe underwater as well as on land; per Mordecai it grows gills and makes the skin slimy, so only wear it in water.
events.description: A glowing, sparkling blue ring from the Gold I'm Wet Box that grants amphibious breathing.
events.evidence: Gold I'm Wet box prize

events.effects: Can be worn on the neck, hair, or face. Grants +5 to the Detect Traps skill, a level-15 Tripper spell castable once every five hours, and the Remote Detonator benefit (magically remote-detonate any explosive the wearer designates, with extra trigger options via a Sapper's table).
events.description: A black paisley bandana from Carl's first gold boss box, named for Drakea, the author of the 22nd edition of the Dungeon Anarchist's Cookbook.
events.evidence: gold boss box prize
item-based spell on the kerchief

events.effects: A patch jacket with unlimited upgrade slots: the +1 to all base stats grows by +1 per compatible patch added, doubled if an eligible Back Patch is attached. Also grants Desperado Club access (already held), Naughty Boys Employment Agency access (already held), and +50% range and accuracy for all thrown explosives.
events.description: A black sleeveless canvas jacket with a subtle purple glow from the Platinum Spicy Box, replacing Carl's ruined leather jacket as his signature protection item.
events.evidence: Platinum Spicy box, first item

events.effects: When affixed to an eligible garment: +5% Strength and immunity from cloud-based attacks. Patches are fleeting: removing one destroys it.
events.description: A half-dollar-sized screen-printed patch depicting planet Earth (under Borant regency), meant to be sewn onto the patch jacket.
events.evidence: Platinum Spicy box, second item

events.description: A small, unenchanted sewing kit with white and red thread spools, for attaching patches to the jacket.
events.evidence: Platinum Spicy box, third item
Carl ties the bandana around his head

events.name: The Remote Detonator Benefit
events.description: Magically detonate any explosive he has designated, from a distance.
events.evidence: Remote Detonator benefit on the back patch

events.text: New group quest received: 'Where the Red God Glows'. All crawlers in bubble 543 are forced in, with Carl's party designated Host. Orthrus the giant puppy (Juvenile Gate Guardian, Level 10) must survive the lightning storm and escape the sharks; if he dies, Emberus will turn the bubble into a kiln. Reward listed only as 'a surprise'.
events.evidence: group quest popup during the sandstorm

events.cause: Thrown at a feral scorpion-man pazuzu during the fight at the lightning towers; it clipped him in the head.
events.evidence: fighting feral pazuzu in the sandstorm

events.cause: Donut delivered the gate to Elle (Carl intends to get it back before leaving the floor, so it is a temporary handoff for the planned rescues).
events.evidence: Carl notes Elle now has the gate

events.text: After the lightning trap killed Quetzalcoatlus (the guardian of Anser's tomb), the Subterranean Quadrant was liberated, completing all four quadrants; bubble 543 popped and all four stairwell locations opened. Carl was caught in the strike's ground current, losing almost half his health, and Donut kept Orthrus alive by casting Heal Critter from the flying house.
events.evidence: bubble notifications after the final lightning strike
while flying drop bear toward Orthrus

events.cause: read from the plane to lure Orthrus out of the bubble

events.evidence: quest complete popup after dog exits bubble

events.text: New group quest 'The Dumber of the Flunkies': reunite Orthrus with Emberus. Carl's party designated Host; no opting out; failure means being smote by Emberus. Completion reward is a Platinum Quest Box.
events.evidence: new quest popup while flying between bubbles
skill went up another notch curving around bubble

events.text: World quest 'Get Orthrus' issued to all fifth-floor crawlers: kill the puppy before it reaches Emberus for one million gold, five level-up potions, and a pet monkey. Carl's group-quest party is also made eligible for the kill rewards, putting the dog (and their own quest) at risk.
events.evidence: world quest popup during chase

events.item: potion ball of Mordecai's Special Brew
events.effects: Heals the target instantly and makes it near-invulnerable for thirty seconds; confirmed to work on Orthrus.
events.description: A throwable ball made by combining Mordecai's special brew potion with another item Mordecai had provided, via the inventory system.
events.evidence: Carl combines potion in inventory mid-flight
Quan is level 48, one higher than Carl

events.cause: thrown and remote-detonated near the disco-ball smoke cloud around Quan
events.evidence: detonated near rainbow smoke plume

events.text: Carl chooses to worship the god Emberus via the god tab (his only options were Grull and Emberus), accepting the terms sight-unseen as a gambit to summon the god.
events.evidence: clicks worship Emberus during death spiral

events.cause: Carl instructed her to take it before they abandoned the plane
events.evidence: take the half-splat potion, before jump

events.cause: thrown at Quan; summoned Emberus because Carl now worshiped a deity
events.evidence: grenade thrown from top wing

events.text: Carl receives the Divine Intervention buff from the celestial grenade: invulnerable for sixty seconds. The buff is invisible, with no indicator over his head.
events.evidence: system message after Emberus is summoned

events.cause: cast on Donut after the crash landing knocked her to 5% health
events.evidence: heals unconscious Donut after impact

events.text: World quest 'Get Orthrus' failed (nobody killed the puppy). Penalty: all safe rooms serve only monkey soup and saltine crackers for the rest of the floor.
events.evidence: quest failed popup after fight

events.description: Reward for completing the group quest 'The Dumber of the Flunkies' by reuniting Orthrus with Emberus.
events.evidence: quest complete popup, platinum quest box

events.text: Two new tattoos appear on the backs of Carl's hands, one sun on each, glowing vaguely orange — a mark of his new worship of Emberus.
events.evidence: notices glowing sun tattoos after battle

events.title: Acolyte of Emberus
events.effects: Church rank Acolyte (capped at Devotee without a cleric/paladin class). Rules: never harm fellow Emberus worshippers, offer a drop of blood daily at a temple or the new personal-space sun shrine, tithe 5% of looted gold into the shrine, own no Hellik-blessed gear, complete all church quests. Benefits: access to all Emberus temples, Emberus and Hellik temples plus worshippers marked on map, +100% experience for killing Hellik worshippers, immunity to Burn, 10% chance for physical attacks to inflict Burn, free access to Club Vanquisher, and a boon every five consecutive days of worship.
events.evidence: adherent-of-Emberus system notification

events.text: Boon from Emberus for reuniting him with Orthrus: Carl's constitution is raised by 25% for 30 hours (temporary buff; no absolute value shown).
events.evidence: boon message after Emberus chat

events.text: Church quest 'Find Out Who Killed My Son': identify who murdered Geyrun and why. No time limit, but Carl will be smote if he tries to exit the 18th floor before it is complete. Reward depends on the culprit.
events.evidence: first church quest from Emberus

events.text: Church quest 'Kill Hellik': Emberus orders Carl to kill his twin brother Hellik before reaching the 12th floor or be smote. Reward: a Celestial God Box, with a bonus if the death is painful.
events.evidence: second church quest from Emberus

events.description: A simple strength ring of the kind commonly found on the first two floors, pulled off Quan's severed arm.

Carl puts it on as his fifth ring

events.effects: Grants the Ripe benefit (all creatures below 50% health are marked on the map) and the Loaded benefit (all non-hidden creatures wearing magical gear are marked on the map).
events.description: An amber-stoned ring named for an infamous orc crawler notorious for stealing kills and loot at the last moment; taken from Quan's severed arm.
events.evidence: second ring from severed arm, description shown
Carl adds the ring to his finger

events.description: An unenchanted gold band from Quan's index finger, engraved inside with 'For Daddy'; the system calls it worthless. Carl keeps it (still on the severed finger) in his inventory.
events.evidence: third, unenchanted ring from the arm

events.name: Man of God
events.description: Awarded for pledging himself to a religion for the first time, with the system mocking humanity's search for meaning and noting his actions now have consequences.
events.reward: Nothing tangible; the 'reward' is sarcastic (certainty of being right and the promise of eternal life).

events.name: Disarming Personality
events.description: Awarded for ripping a fellow crawler's arm off with his bare hands.

events.effects: Locks a person's wrists together; requires at least 200 strength to break; no key — locked and unlocked magically by Carl, with an optional safe word or phrase.

events.effects: Teaches the Ping spell; the book glows and adds the spell to Carl's list when opened.

spell adds itself when tome opened
events.effects: Costs 5 mana, five-minute cooldown; sends an audible ping revealing distance and location of all non-crawler, non-red-tagged targets within one kilometer plus 500 meters per ten points of intelligence; targets hear the ping but not its source; at level 5 it can be imbued with Fear.

events.name: Emberus Worship Benefits
events.description: Burn immunity, a 10% chance to inflict Burn, temple mapping, bonus experience against Hellik worshippers, Club Vanquisher access, and a boon for every five days of worship.
events.evidence: holy benefits listed upon accepting Emberus

events.description: Tribal-style suns tattooed on the back of each hand, glowing faintly orange - the mark of Emberus worship. The rays differ subtly: curled on the right, triangular on the left.

events.text: Donut's Legendary Diva spells cost no mana and are cast by singing in key, with power scaling with singing quality. Standing Ovation raises the party's Dexterity and strengthens their regular spells, Entourage creates illusionary copies of each party member, and Encore heals the party and can remove multiple debuffs. She also has two offensive song spells, a psionic attack and an ice attack, which are unusable until she gets a Golden Throat (autotune) enchantment item.

events.effects: Back upgrade patch; benefits hidden until affixed to an eligible garment and worn. Divine item: gains varying abilities, buffs, or debuffs near certain deities and demons. Fleeting item, destroyed if removed. Only one back patch may be affixed at a time.
events.description: Full-sized black back patch embroidered with a white pyramid of skulls and transparent fiberoptic-like stitching, the sole content of the Celestial Predator Box.
events.evidence: celestial box explodes into fireflies leaving a patch

events.name: Fashion of the Gods!
events.description: Awarded for receiving a divine item; divine items hide their upgrades until equipped and gain new powers (or nasty debuffs) in the presence of deities and certain demons.
events.reward: Nothing tangible (just something new to be anxious about).
events.evidence: achievement pops while handling the divine patch
upgrade potion adds three, moving it to 13

events.cause: Applied to raise Find Traps by three levels to 13, enough to see nearly any trap not hidden by a trapmaster.
events.evidence: upgrade potion applied to Find Traps

events.item: Enchanted Auto Buckler of the Peach Pit
events.evidence: Carl pulls the old buckler off his left arm

events.item: Enchanted Right Back Atcha Personal Shield
events.evidence: new bracer replaces the Peach Pit buckler
Carl sews the small patch onto his jacket first

events.text: Carl's Enchanted Anarchist's Battle Rattle jacket grants +1 to all stat points per affixed patch; he now has five small patches (earth, arrows, bomb, Midnight Epicure, peace symbol), and a back patch doubles the patch stat benefit.
events.evidence: Carl reviews jacket mechanics before sewing
back patch glows when installed, jacket put on

events.cause: The Scavenger's Daughter back patch upgrade (Plus 25 points to Dexterity).
events.evidence: equip message lists patch upgrades

events.cause: The Scavenger's Daughter back patch upgrade (Plus 25 points to Strength).
events.evidence: equip message lists patch upgrades

events.text: The Scavenger's Daughter patch upgrades: all patch stat point benefits doubled; notification whenever a deity enters the area; Climb benefit (better climbing); Soul Reaper benefit (melee kills charge a new Soul Essence interface bar that can be dumped into a single devastating melee attack, with capacity growing each fill-and-drain cycle); Mysterious Bone Key benefit (one-time key that opens any lock in the dungeon, but destroys the jacket and all patches and permanently consumes one of Carl's bones). Additional varying benefits near divine entities.
events.evidence: system message after equipping the divine item

events.name: The Climb Benefit
events.description: He can climb surfaces far better than any human should.
events.source: The Scavenger's Daughter divine back patch
events.evidence: The Climb benefit. / You can climb stuff better.

events.name: The Soul Reaper Benefit
events.description: Melee kills charge a Soul Essence bar; draining it empowers a single devastating melee attack whose effect depends on the stored essence. Capacity grows with each drain.
events.source: The Scavenger's Daughter divine back patch
events.evidence: the patch is charged with the essence of the fallen

events.name: The Mysterious Bone Key Benefit
events.description: A one-time key that opens any lock in the dungeon — destroying the patch, the jacket, and one of Carl's own bones in the process.
events.source: The Scavenger's Daughter divine back patch
events.evidence: Creates a one-time-use key to open a lock. Any lock.

events.text: Squad tracker system message reads Monsters Collected: 0 of 6, meaning Carl and Donut's squad must fill six monster slots. Their squad flag is a pair of heart-covered boxers labeled The Royal Court of Princess Donut, with a bomb patch matching Carl's jacket; its foot-long enchanted spike sticks into any creature, even ghosts, below 5% health.
events.evidence: Monsters Collected counter and flag examination

events.description: After a ghost's kick strips his clothes off, Carl pockets the working rental iPhone (no service), a wallet with credit cards, Euro banknotes, and a few American five dollar bills, plus the pants, shoe, sock, and underwear; all replay loot turns to dust when the floor collapses.
events.evidence: pantsless ghost on capitol steps, Carl loots everything

events.description: English-language tourist map of downtown Havana with transit instructions, plucked from a ghost's hands and used for navigation.
events.evidence: Carl plucks map from ghost tourist

events.text: A new floor combat system announces itself with Combat Started and Combat Complete / Deck has been reset messages in an unfamiliar voice during the fight with The Experience (a level 55 mob), hinting at card-based combat rules not yet explained.
events.evidence: strange combat notifications during first mob fight

events.description: Glowing magical red beret looted (along with the rest of the corpse's loot) from The Experience, the Che Guevara-headed dog monster Donut killed; its stats made Donut gasp but were not shown.
events.evidence: Donut loots monster and takes the beret

events.effects: Consumable utility card: temporarily combines two monster cards into a single creature; summoning length equals the shorter of the two summoning phases, and both monsters are tagged out for the rest of combat afterward. Destroyed on use.
events.description: Postcard-sized card found in The Experience's inventory; Carl could not take it because cards may only be held by the squad leader, so it goes to Donut.
events.evidence: card from mob inventory, squad-leader-only warning
beret appears on Donut's head as she models it

events.text: Narrative confirms Donut currently has one player killer skull over her head (compared against Katia's two and Imani's twelve).
events.evidence: Carl weighs Anton's two skulls against friends' counts

events.text: Donut states her current class is master telephone psychic, and that on the previous floor she was a bard (legendary diva) who needed no patron.
events.evidence: class talk with Sister Ines on the beach

events.text: New Quest: The Chowder War. Choose a side in the war between the Monk Seals and the Red Maníseros Land Crabs and end the conflict either way. Reward: a Platinum Quest Box.
events.evidence: quest popup after examining Yago the monk seal

events.cause: Carl drinks it to approach the monk seal unseen during the flag capture attempt.
events.evidence: Carl downs potion as Paz draws seal aggro

events.item: Geraldo. Monk Seal Picket Sentry (Totem Card)
events.effects: Level 70 monk seal totem automatically activated as the first member of the party's six-slot squad.
events.description: A totem card captured by flagging a second monk seal on the beach, adding Geraldo as the team's first squad member.
events.evidence: chapter opens, second card collected on beach
essence bar fills during duende fight, notification sounds

events.text: Mechanics of Carl's new Scavenger's Daughter essence bar: about ten duende kills fill it; The Daughter's Kiss drains it to massively empower his next melee attack (it vaporizes a duende and shatters the sidewalk); carrying a full bar is said to be dangerous, and the bar grows larger and the attack stronger with each fill-and-drain cycle.
events.evidence: Carl tests Daughter's Kiss on fleeing duende
narration says her poison-glob skill is currently level five

events.effects: Utility card that doubles the time a summoned totem lasts; reusable, not a one-time consumable.
events.description: Two common utility cards collected after clearing the duende swarm.
events.evidence: cards collected after duende fight

events.effects: Utility card that adds 25% to a mob's health for the duration of combat; reusable, not a one-time consumable.
events.description: A common utility card collected after clearing the duende swarm.
events.evidence: cards collected after duende fight

events.text: After the recap, the top ten list repopulates with Donut at number two and Carl at number three (Prepotente remains first).
events.evidence: top ten list repopulates after redacted recap

events.text: System announcement lays out card/deck combat rules: squads need six active totems; while deck combat runs, the squad leader loses spells and inventory access; consumable cards used even in the new saferoom practice arenas are gone forever. Crawlers have 13 days before the floor's assault begins.
events.evidence: Cascadia's long card-system announcement

events.text: System status on entering the mock battle arena (December 12; assault begins December 24): the party's deck holds one of six totems and five total cards (Geraldo's totem, two Time Extends, the Stout card, and the consumable Combo card). Donut acts as squad leader, drawing and playing the cards.
events.evidence: arena entry header and Donut's first drawn hand

events.text: Carl's readied inventory before entering the cemetery: six healing-potion/Emberus-blessed holy gooper grenades, fifteen spider automatons (ten pre-armed with regular explosives), several healing-potion-infused smoke curtains, and a dwindling supply of levitate potions.
events.evidence: Carl preps inventory outside cemetery gate

events.cause: Drunk to float above the cemetery and scout the fog-covered interior.
events.evidence: aerial scouting before entering cemetery

events.cause: Thrown into a dense patch of undead as a test; kills three mobs.
events.evidence: healing potion ball tossed from above

events.text: New compulsory Quest: Pueblo de los Olvidados. The party is locked inside a corrupted Medium Ghommid Settlement (the Colón Cemetery) and must find and eradicate the spirit that turned the ghommid villagers into unsettled undead before they can leave. Reward: exit from the town, reactivation of the settlement and its shops, plus a Silver Quest Box. Carl notes lines where the AI talked about itself were scrubbed from his copied quest text.
events.evidence: quest popup after gate locks behind party

events.effects: Set trap that roots a corporeal target in place for sixty seconds, lowers spirit resistance, and summons a random non-corporeal entity to attack or possess the victim.
events.description: A spirit-summoning trap Carl disarms on the cemetery road and takes into his inventory.
events.evidence: Carl disarms first trap inside gate

events.cause: All remaining curtains (at least three) thrown to clear paths through the ghommid horde; Carl runs out before reaching the saferoom.
events.evidence: curtains tossed during running fight to saferoom

events.cause: Thrown to clear the mass of undead blocking the way to the saferoom.
events.evidence: gooper clears path toward the Yellow Zone

events.name: Interloper!
events.description: Earned for having a red-tagged NPC boss enter their saferoom uninvited; the system jokes about the dangers of letting strangers into your home.
events.evidence: chapter opens, orisha Asojano enters saferoom

events.text: Quest Update for Pueblo de los Olvidados: the orisha Asojano is the source of the ghommid infection; repair his shrine in the town temple, kill him, or kill everyone to free the town.
events.evidence: quest update popup after Asojano explains himself

events.effects: No magical properties; a joke prize keychain mace tube.
events.description: An old, expired keychain self-defense mace tube with no game effects.
events.evidence: Donut opens bronze Stranger Danger box

events.effects: Consumable snare card that cuts an opponent totem's summoning time by 90%; the reduction persists through the combat even on resummon.
events.description: The party's first snare card, depicting a pair of legs tied together.
events.evidence: gold T'Ghee box from practice room opened

events.effects: Auto-tunes singing within one and a half steps of the correct note, assists forming bard-based songs and spells, and amplifies all speech and song at twice normal volume, adjustable up to five times.
events.description: An enchanted golden, amethyst-encrusted headset microphone voted on by Donut's fans; a working version of the prop she used at the masquerade.
events.evidence: Donut's platinum fan box from masquerade singing
headset poofs onto her head with sparkles
she stores it in inventory as secret weapon

events.effects: No magical properties; a joke prize.
events.description: The same expired keychain mace tube Donut received, from Carl's adventurer/Stranger Danger boxes.
events.evidence: Carl opens his first boxes, nothing good

events.effects: Entitles Carl plus one companion to a free one-hour massage at the Penis Parade or Penis Palace spa in the Desperado Club.
events.description: A newspaper-clipping style coupon voted on by fans, good for a spa massage at a Desperado Club strip club.
events.evidence: Carl's gold fan box from meeting Imogen

events.effects: Consumable battle cards.
events.description: A pair of consumable Flee cards given by Katia from her team's surplus.
events.evidence: Katia shares surplus cards from the Lads

events.effects: Utility battle card; Carl takes another copy for his deck.
events.description: An additional Stout utility card from Katia's card pile.
events.evidence: Carl grabs another Stout card

events.effects: Utility card that increases a totem's supply of mana points.
events.description: A utility battle card Carl had not seen before, taken from Katia's pile.
events.evidence: Carl picks new utility cards from Katia

events.effects: Utility card that increases a mob's speed by 25%.
events.description: A utility battle card taken from Katia's pile; Carl notes it would make Geraldo extremely fast.
events.evidence: Carl picks new utility cards from Katia

events.effects: A step up from Pustule's smoke curtains; combining four with a stun grenade widens the stun's range and extends it up to thirty seconds.
events.description: A case of twelve smoke mantles bought for 30,000 gold from a Desperado Club market booth via Li Na's team.
events.evidence: supply run flashback before the night gauntlet

events.effects: A modified stun grenade combined with four smoke mantles; mass-paralyzes non-corporeal undead in a wide area for up to thirty seconds.
events.description: A beachball-sized stun bomb Carl built with Mordecai; he also built six smaller smoke missiles and six single-target anti-undead missiles into Bomo's new armor.
events.evidence: Mordecai helps Carl build bombs before the run

events.cause: Rolled at the ghommid horde as the party exited the saferoom; the blast froze the surrounding undead.
events.evidence: combat starts, Carl rolls the stun grenade

events.text: System message: 'The Scavenger's Daughter has been fed. Unleash her wrath.' Carl's back patch fully recharged with essence, mostly from ghommid kills, turning the bar black.
events.evidence: mid-fight after killing ghommids near temple path
Donut sings with autotuned, amplified voice

events.text: Donut's bard song grants the party a 'limbered' buff: party dexterity increased by 50%.
events.evidence: system message after Donut's first verse

events.text: The dexterity buff is canceled because Donut botches the second verse; the system mutes her from singing for five minutes.
events.evidence: system message after failed second verse
Donut pulls the headset away after being muted

events.text: Carl activates Daughter's Kiss, draining his essence bar; the empowered punch breaks the donkey-headed ghommid apart instead of exploding it.
events.evidence: fight with donkey-headed ghommid splitter

events.name: Crybaby!
events.description: Earned for being hit with the Terrified debuff, which locks his legs for fifteen seconds or until the frightening monster dies; the AI mocks him for it.
events.reward: None; the system refuses to reward those who soil themselves in public.
events.evidence: donkey snake shrieks at Carl by temple door

events.cause: Held point-first so the lunging ghommid donkey snake impaled itself, flagging the monster.
events.evidence: Carl flags the donkey snake at the doorway

events.item: Skylar Spinach. Two-Thirds of an Enraged Ghommid Splitter
events.effects: T'Ghee totem card of a non-corporeal, affliction-dealing splitter mob from Cuba; splits into two pieces on a fatal blow until the primary dies twice, gaining Constitution each iteration. Notable attacks: Iced, Dodge, Terrifier, Splitter, plus 5 additional skills and spells. Auto-activated into the squad because slots were empty.
events.description: A rare totem card obtained by flagging the tough donkey-headed ghommid splitter; captured at two-thirds strength since Carl had already killed it once.
events.evidence: card resolves after combat inside the temple
headset pops onto her head to warn Carl

events.name: You have discovered a Celestial Thorn Room!
events.description: Earned for finding a rare Celestial thorn room, a chamber that forces a consequential choice where every option carries a benefit and a cost.
events.reward: Nothing tangible; the system quips that every rose has its thorn.
events.evidence: temple freezes after Anton grabs shrine piece

events.text: Thorn room boss battle: seven broken Orisha shrines, only one repairable using the Sun of Reawakening gem. Offered rewards ranged from smite avoidance and a Confusing Fog spell (Legba), a trainable Lightning spell (Shango), fatal-blow protection plus 50% charisma and Heal Party (Oshun), quest victory (Asojano), health-seep immunity plus 50% Constitution (Inle), to Yemaya, whose repair grants each squad a special card and a boon from the goddess Eileithyia. Prompted by Katia's secret message, Carl chooses Yemaya.
events.evidence: AI announces the seven shrine choices

events.cause: Drunk mid-battle to top up her mana while chaining spells.
events.evidence: Donut says she had to take a potion

events.cause: Shoved into the dying city boss Asojano's head to flag him.
events.evidence: Carl jumps the dying boss with a flag

events.effects: The level 130 city boss Asojano, Lord of Smallpox, captured as a battle card by flagging him at the moment of death.
events.description: Asojano puffs away and turns into a card when Carl flags him; no card text is shown in this chapter.
events.evidence: boss puffs away, turning into a card

events.text: Carl is infected with SlugPox from flagging Asojano: boils repeatedly form and burst on his body, each releasing a hostile Pox Slug of escalating level (level 1, then 2, and so on). Healers on hand cannot cure it, and he needs the All Healer inside Club Vanquisher.
events.evidence: SlugPox warning after flagging the boss

events.cause: The holy-oil-coated metal door block vanished when the temple transformed into the Temple of Yemaya.
events.evidence: chock disappears as temple is restored

events.text: Quest Complete: Pueblo de los Olvidados. The medium ghommid settlement is liberated, the surviving ghommids are cured, the deck is reset, and the party may exit. The temple becomes the Club Vanquisher Entrance Temple of Yemaya, Carl and Donut are greeted as Friends of Yemaya, and the goddess Yemaya re-ascends; the promised squad card and Eileithyia's boon are not yet delivered in this chapter.
events.evidence: wall of victory messages after gem placed

events.text: With the deity Ogun present, Carl's Scavenger's Daughter jacket patch opens her eyes and grants a temporary effect: All-Smash Related Attacks +300%. When Ogun leaves, the benefit begins fading, dissipating fully over the next ten minutes.
events.evidence: back patch vibrates when Ogun's lightning arrival

events.text: Carl's slug-parasite affliction continues: boils keep forming and popping, releasing hostile slugs of rising level (a level-3 slug, then a level-5 slug, then an even bigger one marked like a danger dingo). Mongo eats each one.
events.evidence: boils popping throughout the Ogun confrontation

events.text: Carl casts Black Nimbus, a spell that came with his new nipple ring, on Anton; it temporarily strips all benefits of worshipping a god and removes Anton's shunned mark.
events.evidence: Carl steps forward to counter Ogun's smite

events.cause: Clicked a Heal scroll on Anton during Ogun's punishment.
events.evidence: scrolls used right after Black Nimbus

events.cause: Clicked a second Heal scroll on Paz immediately after the first.
events.evidence: scrolls used right after Black Nimbus

events.cause: Given to the dying Paz, who drank it despite protesting; it could not cure his god-inflicted permanent debuff.
events.evidence: Carl tends Paz after Ogun departs

events.text: With two deities present, Carl's Scavenger's Daughter patch opens her eyes and grants two temporary effects: from Yemaya, all healing skills and spells cost no mana and have no cooldown; from Eileithyia, damage Carl deals to female opponents is quartered while all female party members strike with 500% melee power. The benefits begin a ten-minute fade once both deities leave.
events.evidence: patch notification as Yemaya and Eileithyia appear

events.text: Eileithyia touches Carl's shoulder and scratches Donut, giving Carl a Rapid Healing buff notification (Donut likely received the same).
events.evidence: goddess greets crawlers before addressing Yemaya

events.effects: A non-consumable mystic card that lets the holder steal a card from someone else.
events.description: A mystic-type card with the black border of mythical rarity, gifted by the goddess Yemaya in thanks for the shrine repair; it completes the party's collection of all five card kinds.
events.evidence: Yemaya's reward cards to Donut and Sister Ines

events.text: Yemaya teleports the mercenary Bomo to Larracos on the ninth floor at his own request, leaving Carl and Donut without his Teleport to Stairwell spell.
events.evidence: Bomo asks to rejoin the Sledge, vanishes

events.text: Eileithyia grants the party a floor boon lasting the rest of the floor while they stay in her grace: Protection from Spiritual Possession, 10% healing speed, and plus 10% Constitution. She can revoke it at any time.
events.evidence: yellow light of grace fills the temple

events.text: Carl receives a quest called Spiders from Eileithyia, a deity he does not worship: kill five spiders in her name every day until told to stop, with the reward of another boon. Via secret deity messages, she reveals the quest is mainly a cover so she can message Carl and grant boons, tied to a secret bargain about the Faction Wars (accepting Odette as team Adjutant in exchange for votes to turn off ninth-floor team protections); Carl signals agreement.
events.evidence: New Quest Spiders popup and hidden deity messages

events.text: Carl's parasite condition is identified by Yemaya as slugpox, deliberately inflicted by her brother; a healer can cure it and he should do so quickly. The emerging slugs keep escalating (one now carries a tiny hatchet).
events.evidence: Yemaya addresses Carl's popping boils

events.text: Carl's slugpox keeps escalating in the Club Vanquisher vestibule: each boil hurts more and deals more damage, and the latest slug is level 10 and hand-sized. Pater Coal warns the pox will kill him once the slugs match his own strength.
events.evidence: boils popping while arguing with door guard Potsy

events.text: Pater Coal, high cleric of Emberus, casts a spell that temporarily suppresses Carl's slugpox, stopping new boils from forming.
events.evidence: boil on stomach dissipates in the sun temple

events.text: Progress on Carl's Emberus god quests: during the foot-washing sacrament, Emberus reveals that Katia's crossbow and Donut's oak bracelet are both crucial to solving Geyrun's murder, but neither path can be explored without the memorial crystal held by another crawler. A crystal exists for Apito even though she still lives, which Carl must investigate.
events.evidence: Pater Coal's trance in Temple of the Ending Sun

events.text: Emberus grants Carl a floor boon lasting the rest of the floor while he stays in Emberus's grace: The Martyr's Path (every outdoor step is marked on the map; if his health hits 5% he can release a gout of flame from each marked spot, resetting the steps; it triggers automatically on death) plus 10% Strength.
events.evidence: boon notification after the foot-washing sacrament

events.text: Carl gets his slugpox cured by the club healer, paying about 10,000 gold for it.
events.evidence: Carl recaps the cure back at the saferoom

events.text: A mantaur guard accuses Carl of breaking a club rule, and he is banned from Club Vanquisher for three days.
events.evidence: Carl's saferoom recap after the healer visit

events.name: The Martyr's Path
events.description: At death's door he can release flame from every outdoor step he has taken; it triggers automatically if he dies.
events.evidence: Martyr's Path granted by the high priest washing his feet

events.name: Secondary Gig!
events.description: Earned for taking on guild work on top of everything else; the system sarcastically mocks Carl for adding yet another job to his overloaded plate.
events.reward: A new Guild Jobs tab, a sub-tab of quests, is added to Carl's interface.
events.evidence: Carl touches Katia's Guild of Suffering skin slip

events.text: Carl, via his Guild of Suffering membership (spiderweb elbow tattoo), gains access to Katia's guild job: kill Astrid, level 125 Bloodlust Sprite and Assistant Manager of the Desperado Club, with proof of death required. The job is owned by Katia; speaking its details aloud cancels the contract and revokes guild access.
events.evidence: Night Wyrm task read from the skin slip

events.text: Status banner: three of the six totems have been collected, with eight days and one hour remaining until phase two of the floor.
events.evidence: system banner at chapter opening, December 15th

events.text: Carl notes in passing that Donut now has fire resistance, relevant because his Martyr's Path boon could ignite his marked footsteps near her.
events.evidence: Carl musing about Martyr's Path dangers while walking

events.text: While clearing low-level mobs across Havana for an hour, the party collects several utility cards they likely won't use, since stuffing the deck with them lowers the odds of pulling totem cards.
events.evidence: mob-clearing sweep heading south through Havana

events.text: Mordecai explains that Donut's Charisma is still very high but its effect on NPCs is weakening due to resistance scaling; by the tenth floor bopcas will merely like her and will be surly toward Carl despite his decent Charisma.
events.evidence: Mordecai greets them about bopca attitude

events.text: Mordecai has secretly been hiring six room attendants (himself in his slot plus five compression-sick changelings, including Ruby) using Carl and Donut's supply money, costing them a bit more than 1,000 gold a day.
events.evidence: makeup incident leads to attendant confession

events.text: Carl and Donut attend the fifth Faction Wars preproduction meeting acting in their capacity as sponsors: they are co-warlords of the third team, which Donut introduces as the Princess Posse, seated among nine other faction representatives.
events.evidence: round-table introductions at production facility meeting

events.cause: Grinding on mobs (mostly talking birds) while traveling toward the Desperado Club.
events.evidence: grinding recap before entering Desperado Club

events.cause: Grinding on mobs (mostly talking birds) while traveling toward the Desperado Club.
events.evidence: grinding recap before entering Desperado Club

events.text: Carl maxed out his Scavenger's Daughter bar three times and drained it via melee attacks; his melee attacks are noticeably stronger as a result.
events.evidence: grinding recap before entering Desperado Club

events.description: One more snare-type card added to their card collection while grinding.
events.evidence: cards collected during grinding recap

events.effects: Drains a totem's mana; unlike their Hobble card, it is not consumable.
events.description: A card that saps mana from an opposing totem and can be reused.
events.evidence: cards collected during grinding recap

events.effects: Picks a random card from the opponent's hand and sends it to the discard pile.
events.description: A rare mystic card for the totem card game.
events.evidence: cards collected during grinding recap

events.effects: Undead, ranged, healer totem mob from Ecuador. Notable attacks: Blood into Lead, Resurrect Totem, Bullet Hell, plus 20 additional skills and spells. Collecting it auto-placed the totem into the squad since slots were open; it cannot be removed or traded until the squad is full except by tearing the card.
events.description: A unique totem card depicting an anime-style Jesus firing an uzi. Florin gave it away because his charisma was too low to control the totem, which once shot him during a summons.
events.evidence: Florin slides card across table to Donut

events.text: With HeyZoos added, their totem squad stands at four of six: the monk seal, the donkey snake ghommid splitter, Asojano, and HeyZoos.
events.evidence: squad tally after accepting Florin's card

events.name: We Want Meat! We Want Meat!
events.description: Earned for entering a male strip club establishment (the Penis Parade in the Desperado Club); the notification riffs on the history of all-male revues and notes performer quality supposedly improves on deeper floors.
events.reward: 10 gold pieces to use as tips, plus a bottle of hand sanitizer.
events.evidence: entering the Penis Parade strip club

events.description: Tip money granted by the We Want Meat! achievement.
events.evidence: achievement reward on entering strip club

events.description: Thrown in with the We Want Meat! achievement reward.
events.evidence: achievement reward on entering strip club

events.name: The Molly Maguires
events.description: Earned for finding and entering the main room of a hidden guild he belongs to (the Guild of Suffering). The unusually long notification digresses through types of secret societies and the history of the Molly Maguires coal-miner group before dismissing itself; it later rewrote itself into a shorter standard achievement in his history tab.
events.evidence: touching the spiderweb-carved door in Guild of Suffering

events.description: A little pinback button reading 'Certified pole dancer,' given by the dwarf patron Rosemarie after Carl limped out of the strip club cubicle. Katia mentions she got one too.
events.evidence: chat with Katia recapping strip club exit

events.description: A simple, silver-colored blank tiara bought from the gnome jeweler at the Desperado Club as a mount for the citrine gem.
events.evidence: gnome jeweler visit at Desperado Club

events.cause: The citrine gem was installed into the blank tiara for a 25,000 gold fee, becoming part of the new enchanted tiara.
events.evidence: jeweler installs citrine into tiara

events.item: Enchanted Tiara of the Inebriated Dragonfly
events.effects: When equipped: 50% reduction to potion cooldown, +10 intelligence, protection from poisoning (already obtained), negates the dexterity debuff when inebriated, and grants a level 10 Hover skill.
events.description: A citrine-encrusted tiara themed on a children's story about a drunk dragonfly locked out by his wife. Created by installing Donut's Flawless Jeweler's Gem into a blank silver tiara.
events.evidence: tiara description after gem installation

events.item: Enchanted Tiara of the Inebriated Dragonfly
events.evidence: Donut rises into air the moment she equips it
tiara grants level 10 Hover skill

events.text: The tiara's Hover skill has a five-minute cooldown and is limited to five uses per day; it lets Donut hover two to three feet off the ground for about 90 seconds, is not negated by damage, and gently lowers her if activated while falling, so both Carl and Donut now have fall protection.
events.evidence: Hover skill mechanics explained after equipping

events.cause: Went stealth to approach the massed land crab migration near the crashed cars.
events.evidence: going stealth before rounding the bend

events.text: Quest update for The Chowder War: the crab migration has begun as a sneak attack massed on the southern coast, and the quest prompts them to choose a side between the land crabs and the monk seals.
events.evidence: Chowder War quest update near crab migration

events.name: Hover
events.description: Hover a few feet off the ground for about 90 seconds, up to five times a day; softens falls.
events.evidence: hover two to three feet off the ground, five times per day

events.cause: Taken to read the stats of Raul, the land crab they planned to flag.
events.evidence: Donut sizes up Raul from hiding

events.text: Carl now has ten Phase Through Walls potions, which keep appearing in loot boxes. Each lasts nine seconds with a duration-plus-one-second cooldown, and his potion cooldown is now low enough that he could otherwise chain potions.
events.evidence: planning the crab trap under the box truck

events.cause: Downed to phase through the box truck's walls while attacking Raul.
events.evidence: nine-second timer as Carl rushes the truck

events.cause: Dropped into the truck's cargo hold to panic the crab.
events.evidence: smoke curtain dropped mid-fight

events.cause: A second phase potion downed to re-enter the truck and finish flagging the crab.
events.evidence: second potion before pulling the flag

events.cause: Jabbed into Raul once his health was low enough, converting him into a card.
events.evidence: flag jabbed into cornered crab

events.description: A common totem card of Raul, a level 70 Red Maniseros Land Crab and Sun-Kissed Cloud Rank Warrior captured by flag. Carl caught the card and handed it to Donut; it is the fifth of their six squad slots.
events.evidence: card flies to Carl's hand, handed to Donut

events.effects: Taken once a day, it protects until the drinker sleeps against the Bedlam Bride's Unending Insanity and Lights Out spells plus a host of other fear-based debuffs and afflictions.
events.description: A protection potion Mordecai brewed from expensive ingredients; several were made, enough for the entire guild.
events.evidence: Mordecai brews protection before spider encounter

events.cause: Carl and Donut each took their daily dose before facing Shi Maria.
events.evidence: already taken theirs for the day

events.cause: Carl used both of his two table upgrade coupons on Mordecai's alchemy table so the Liquid Therapy potions could be brewed.
events.evidence: coupons spent on alchemy table

events.text: Quest update for The Chowder War: crawler Sister Ines Quiteria has allied with the Monk Seal Abbot and warned him of the crabs' sneak attack; the quest again asks whose side they will take.
events.evidence: Chowder War update after crab migration clears

events.text: New quest received: The Bedlam Bride, an escort quest to get Shi Maria (the level 140 city boss Reaper Spider Minion, a demi-god) to the city of Larracos. Reward is listed only as 'a simple prize for a simple quest.' Shi Maria agrees to be flagged into a totem card in exchange for transport, and Carl moves to injure her enough to use the card magic.
events.evidence: quest notification during parley with Shi Maria

events.effects: Snare card: when an enemy totem it is cast on times out, that totem card is discarded whether the totem died or not.
events.description: A snare card dropped by the Legendary monster La Ciguapa after Mongo killed her; useful against enemy totems too powerful to kill outright.
events.evidence: cards dropped after Mongo kills La Ciguapa

events.effects: Single-use consumable special effect card castable on any active card or totem; permanently rewrites the target card to reflect its current buffed state (e.g. doubled summon time becomes permanent).
events.description: A mythic consumable card, not on the list of known card types, dropped by La Ciguapa; could make an already-strong totem card far more powerful if stacked with buffs first.
events.evidence: second card from La Ciguapa drop

events.text: Carl learns the Daughter's Kiss charged attack from his Scavenger's Daughter back patch is nearly twice as powerful when the captured soul essence is all from a single species, and the residual effect mimics an amplified version of that species' attack (e.g. Howling Gull scream). A charged punch disintegrated a Yuanat and left a ten-foot crater.
events.evidence: grinding north, filling and draining back patch

events.text: Phase Two rules announced: at 12:01 AM December 24th all crawlers teleport to a selection room; any totem cards not in the active deck will be lost; from Phase Two on they may never hold more or fewer than six totems; a key location 10-15 km away must be reached within 48 hours to open their stairwell in Phase Three.
events.evidence: Cascadia's daily update in roadside saferoom

events.cause: Entry fee for the Mock Battle Arena at medium difficulty; the price was raised from 2,000 to 2,500 gold.
events.evidence: Donut complains price rose entering arena

events.text: Carl and Donut set their ready deck at 18 cards: six totems (Geraldo the monk seal, Asojano the orisha, Skylar Spinach the ghommid splitter, Uzi Jesus, Raul the crab, Shi Maria the spider); six utility cards (two Time Extend, Stout, Blue Stuff, Greased Lightning, the consumable Combo); three snare cards (Hobble, Hole in the Bag, Damnation); two mystic cards (Thief, Force Discard); and one consumable Flee card.
events.evidence: assembling 18-card deck before mall simulation

events.text: When Shi Maria opens her fifth eye during the simulated battle, Carl is physically affected — dizziness and a pull toward the 'river' in his mind — even though the simulation is supposed to be safe, suggesting her mental ability partially bypasses the arena safeties and his assumed immunity.
events.evidence: Carl drops to a knee during reindeer fight

events.text: After beating the Mock Battle Arena at challenge difficulty (Shi Maria devoured the level 50 Mushika in seconds), Carl and Donut unlock 'Secret Challenge Mode': three free medium-plus fights against opponents who carry their own decks.
events.evidence: after challenge win against rat monster Mushika

events.text: Donut receives a notification that Empress D'Nadia sold her sponsorship; her new sponsor is Long Haul Biological Waste Management Solutions.
events.evidence: sponsorship notification during verdant hills fight

events.text: Carl and Donut lose their first deck-versus-deck secret challenge fight against Der Schachmeister, whose mystic cards locked Donut's deck, revealed her hand, and burned the Shi Maria card for the battle; a wolf totem struck Donut, ending the simulation. The deck was reset afterward with no permanent card loss.
events.evidence: Simulation Failed after Die Kartoffelwoelfin hits Donut

events.cause: Carl threw a low-powered hob-lobber to instantly kill The Luau, her conga-playing companion, and her Mo'o totem in the third secret challenge fight.
events.evidence: third deck fight against The Luau

events.text: Carl and Donut lose the second of their three free deck-versus-deck fights to a mud-blob deckmaster whose snare cards (including a Damnation like their own) picked off Shi Maria, Raul, and Asojano; they win the third fight in seconds when Carl kills the deckmaster directly. Donut notes they have run out of money from repeated arena practice.
events.evidence: recap of second and third secret challenge fights

events.cause: Carl flips Clarabelle a gold coin as an apology/tip to get Samantha admitted into the Desperado Club.
events.evidence: coin flipped to Clarabelle at club entrance

events.effects: Trap designed to snare demons.
events.description: Traps taken from the trapped secret corridor behind the Desperado Club shops while Carl disarmed the passage for the Astrid ambush.
events.evidence: disarming traps in secret corridor, keeps five

events.text: Carl rigs the ambush corridor: two-part teleport traps that send any summoned NPC guards to a locked closet upstairs, plus a blast-resistant wall ratcheted in place and sandbags to reinforce the door holding fifteen unknown, red-tagged monsters behind the defunct Demon Tamer's Guild arena.
events.evidence: trap setup in secret corridor before ambush

events.cause: Carl drinks an invisibility potion from inventory to cross the restaurant past the demon Minge unseen.
events.evidence: downed potion before crossing open space

events.cause: Carl throws a music-playing alarm ball ('Material Girl') at the club's front entrance as a distraction for the demon.
events.evidence: alarm ball thrown toward club entrance

events.text: System notifications confirm roughly a dozen of Carl's teleport traps activate on the club guards rushing the secret corridor, and his IED detonates ('worked really well'), expending those placed devices during the botched Astrid ambush.
events.evidence: teleport trap and IED activation notifications

events.description: The wings Carl rips from the fairy Astrid to break her spell freezing Katia; fairies store their magic in their wings. Carl stores them in his inventory.
events.evidence: Carl rips off Astrid's wings, pockets them

events.text: The Night Wyrm's assassination target Astrid is killed: Carl de-wings her and Katia's totem finishes her, with Katia keeping the body to deliver for the quest. Before dying, Astrid reveals she is the Night Wyrm's wife and that she unleashed 'vorpals' to fight the level 225 Minor Feral Demon Minge loose from the Nothing.
events.evidence: kitchen fight, Astrid killed by Katia's spike

events.cause: Rolled toward the restaurant to screen against the demon Minge
events.evidence: kitchen defense as Minge tears apart the club

events.cause: Given to Samantha for drinks before entering the Desperado Club that day
events.evidence: Carl recalls giving Samantha drinking money

events.text: System messages confirm the Night Wyrm's assassination job is done: Astrid is dead and the job completes once Katia delivers the body. Katia (the body carrier) receives the payment orchid, not Carl.
events.evidence: Job Update and Job Complete system messages

events.cause: Mounted on a spider automaton's trap slot and triggered against Minge
events.evidence: trap slot loaded before sending spiders at Minge

events.cause: Detonated against Minge, splashing her with blood water from Emberus's shrine
events.evidence: three spiders reach Minge and detonate

events.cause: Set off by the third and fourth spider bots to burn and blind Minge
events.evidence: smoke curtains fill the restaurant with burning fog

events.cause: Destroyed while dismantling the wall bracers sealing the vorpal room; the vorpals broke out anyway
events.evidence: system message: automaton destroyed before completing task

events.text: Guild upgrades bought earlier at Donut's urging (paid by Imani): a barracks upgrade with room for about 20 mercenaries, and a training arena called the Mercenary Arcade.
events.evidence: Katia's message about barracks and training arena

events.cause: Redeemed (valued at 100,000 gold) to hire the Penis Parade crew as mercenaries on a two-floor contract
events.evidence: Rosemarie's shop interface, coupon covers hiring cost

events.text: Hired 15 mercenaries for the Faction Wars army on a two-floor contract: the 13 male Penis Parade dancers plus Doctor Bones the skeleton DJ and Bucket Boy the crocodilian. They transferred directly to the guild barracks.
events.evidence: hired crew appears at personal-space barracks

events.effects: Totem card added to the active deck
events.description: A totem card of a mythic creature from Iceland, given to Donut from Katia's excess totem cards before the club trip.
events.evidence: Katia gives Donut an excess totem card

events.cause: Used to combine two problem totem cards into one new totem
events.evidence: deck rework to make room for Jola

events.cause: Merged into a single new totem via the Combo card
events.evidence: two problem cards combined during deck rework

events.effects: New totem created by combining two existing totems and buffing them with utility cards
events.description: The product of the Combo card merge; described only as a jerk who likes fighting.
events.evidence: Donut reports the new combo guy

events.cause: Used to make the combined totem's buffed state permanent
events.evidence: Glow-Up locks in the combo change

events.text: Carl selects Baroness Victory, esquire (orc attorney and judge, formerly of the Skull Empire), as the Princess Posse's Faction Wars adjutant, over candidates Ripper Wonton and Drick.
events.evidence: adjutant choice on Odette's show

events.text: Per a new twist, only squad leaders transfer to the phase-two choosing chamber; Donut is teleported there alone (Carl remains behind temporarily, all other crawlers held in stasis).
events.evidence: Transferring now; Donut alone in globe room

events.text: Donut picks Miami from the handful of highlighted locations on the globe as the squad's new area.
events.evidence: Carl watches Donut pick Miami on screen

events.text: Donut wins her first solo card battle: Mongo kills the deckmaster Robert the Human (level 40) outright just as his totems (Robert the Doll and Dog) deploy, ending the fight instantly.
events.evidence: Mongo bites Robert's throat on screen

events.description: White old-school sailor's hat taken from the corpse of the defeated deckmaster Robert the Human.
events.evidence: Donut collects the dead man's sailor hat

events.text: Cards from the defeated deck float as loot; Donut may keep all non-totem cards, but keeping a totem would require discarding one of the squad's existing totems.
events.evidence: cards appear after the fight ends

events.text: Phase two placement: entering Monroe County, Florida (the Keys) on December 24th. Level collapse in 6 days, 22 hours; the key location is marked on the map with 46 hours remaining to collect it. A glitch initially reported Iowa for Donut, and the system visibly relocated the key mound to a few miles west of them.
events.evidence: arrival system messages in the old house
Donut wearing the looted sailor hat

events.text: Quest update for 'Find out Who Killed my Son' (Emberus): with the goddess Nekhebit returned, Carl must find a Nekhebit temple and roughly question the priestess about the murder.
events.evidence: Emberus quest update notification

events.text: Quest Complete: Spiders. The system directs them to a temple of Eileithyia to receive a boon; Carl believes the quest existed only as a messaging channel for Huanxin and expects no real reward, especially since Donut cannot access Club Vanquisher.
events.evidence: Quest Complete notification after Eileithyia's message

events.text: Donut states her Torch spell is level 14 and has been stuck there for a while.
events.evidence: Donut answers Carl's question about Torch

events.text: Sailor hat properties per Carl and Donut: wearing it costs 10 intelligence points and blocks Donut's new Hover skill, but it lowers the card draw countdown by two seconds. Donut plans to wear it until her cards are done, then switch back to her new dragonfly tiara.
events.evidence: Carl and Donut discuss the hat's tradeoffs

events.effects: One of nine tokens required before fighting the key boss
events.description: A poker-chip-sized coin dropped by Robert the Human; nine tokens must be collected to unlock the final key fight.
events.evidence: Donut confirms the first token reads one

events.cause: Thrown via xistera at the level 45 Babababoon deckmaster, killing it before it drew a card
events.evidence: parking lot fight with Santa-hat Babababoon

events.effects: Second of nine tokens needed for the key fight
events.description: Poker-chip-sized coin etched with the number two, dropped by the Babababoon deckmaster.
events.evidence: token among Babababoon's loot cards

events.cause: Experience from defeating the token-holding deckmaster mobs
events.evidence: level check during the token fights

events.cause: Experience from defeating the token-holding deckmaster mobs
events.evidence: level check during the token fights

events.text: Donut's pet Mongo reaches level 40 during the token fights.
events.evidence: Mongo finally hits level 40

events.effects: Tokens three through five of the nine needed for the key fight
events.description: Coins collected from deckmaster opponents three through five (a level 50 turtle creature and two guarded deckmasters), each five levels higher than the last.
events.evidence: fights three through five summarized; sixth coin collected later

events.cause: Thrown at the level 65 ghost-class Reef Explorer but deflected by a Swordfish Interloper's water jet, detonating early
events.evidence: opening of the sixth token fight

events.text: The combined totem is revealed: Lazarus-A-Bang-Bang, made by comboing Uzi Jesus and Asojano. Card text: T'Ghee Card, Unique totem, level 140, Constitution 80 (it appeared at 20 and Donut buffed it to 80 before the Glow-Up froze it), summoning duration 200 seconds, only 2 utility slots (the originals had 7), ranged/healer/affliction-dealing mob with attacks including a face-melting bazooka, Apocalyptic Plague, Resurrect Totem, plus 25 additional skills and spells.
events.evidence: Carl reads the orange Lazarus-A-Bang-Bang card

events.effects: Each boosts a totem's chance to land attacks; multiple copies stack on one totem while counting as a single buff
events.description: Accuracy-buff utility cards looted from the defeated Reef Explorer's bloated deck.
events.evidence: thirty Precision cards in the Reef Explorer loot

events.text: The soul bar on Carl's Scavenger's Daughter back patch is almost full, causing an itching unease; he debates holding the charge for the key boss since the deckmaster mobs are the only kills available to refill it.
events.evidence: Carl's neck itches near the dirt mound

events.item: assorted non-totem cards from the Reef Explorer's deck
events.effects: Adds the defeated deck's non-totem cards to the squad's collection
events.description: Donut grabs all remaining non-totem cards from the roughly 40-50 card drop, discarding the totems (a Swordfish Interloper and a rare squid creature).
events.evidence: Donut grabs all other cards, discards totems

events.effects: Sixth of nine tokens needed for the key fight
events.description: Coin collected from the defeated level 65 Reef Explorer deckmaster.
events.evidence: Donut collects the sixth coin

events.name: (unnamed swordfish kill achievement)
events.description: Earned for killing the swordfish guy with his foot.
events.evidence: notifications review in Donut's room
skill base noted while reviewing notifications

events.text: Carl's pedicure kit item bonus supposedly raises Smush an additional three levels, but the skill still displays as 14; item buffs get wonky when pushing skills toward 15.
events.evidence: Smush buff discussion after skill level

events.name: Cascadia's Turquoise Taco!
events.description: Awarded for ending a card fight before either side drew a single card, humiliating executive producer Mudskipper Cascadia, who is already on edge after the previous floor's disaster.
events.evidence: first notable achievement in notification list

events.name: Smeat!
events.description: Awarded for participating in deck combat as a minion bodyguard for a squad leader; mocks the recipient as expendable cannon fodder.
events.evidence: second notable achievement in notification list

events.name: Cascadia's Turquoise Taco! (squad leader version)
events.description: Squad-leader version of the achievement for ending a card fight before it began; the prize is the same.
events.evidence: Carl notes Donut has the squad leader version

events.text: Donut has many more achievements than Carl, most tied to specific card moves from her first and last deck fights; contents not itemized.
events.evidence: Donut going through her rewards

events.effects: Places a permanent trench 2.3 meters deep and 2 meters wide; length scales with user's intelligence plus strength; scrolls can be combined to add sleeping quarters and other front-line accessories
events.description: A set of WWI-themed trench-digging scrolls found inside the Silver Spam Box.
events.evidence: opening the Silver Spam box

events.item: The Filthy Little Crawler's Book of Voodoo
events.effects: Cannot be traded, destroyed, or discarded; when near another crawler who owns the same book, their name is permanently added; owners can exchange private messages by writing under a name, or broadcast by writing under their own entry
events.description: A thick blank leather book from the Gold Prepotente Box, styled after old convention voodoo message boards; most crawlers in the dungeon received one.
events.evidence: Gold Prepotente box screams open

events.item: The Filthy Little Crawler's Book of Voodoo
events.effects: Same enchanted messaging book as Carl's; her entry appears in his copy the moment hers materializes
events.description: Donut opens her own Gold Prepotente Box and receives the same untradeable messaging book.
events.evidence: Donut opens her Prepotente box

events.name: Cliffhanger!
events.description: Earned for scaling a cliff face bare-handed; the AI mocks that scrolls and spells exist so nobody has to do that anymore.
events.reward: None (the AI jokes it will refrain from commenting on his toenails)
events.evidence: after climbing onto the Iowa cliff edge

events.text: System warning: Carl's equipped cloak has aggravated the Nightgaunt deckmaster (The Visitor, level 70, Deckmaster Seven of Ten), which becomes so enraged at the cloak it never plays a single card.
events.evidence: warning at start of Visitor fight

events.cause: Thrown at the farmhouse roof at the invisible moth creatures, collapsing part of the unstable ground
events.evidence: opening move of the Visitor fight

events.text: Donut's Jola card (given by Katia) is revealed in play: a house-sized Persian cat, Jola the Yule Cat, with a giant lit candle melted to his head, a 75-second summon timer, and lots of health. The candle reveals invisible creatures, and he grows stronger against naked or child opponents. He kills the Visitor deckmaster directly.
events.evidence: Jola summoned against the Visitor

events.effects: Paralyzes a totem for ten seconds and makes it glow brightly
events.description: Consumable snare cards looted from the defeated Visitor's 30-card drop; Carl calls them a great prize.
events.evidence: loot after the Visitor's defeat

events.item: remaining non-totem cards from the Visitor's deck
events.effects: Adds the defeated deck's non-totem cards to the squad's collection
events.description: The squad takes all the cards they can from the 30-card drop, leaving the 18 totems behind (a legendary level-110 Demon Queen and Quench Moths).
events.evidence: we took the token and all cards

events.effects: Seventh of nine tokens needed for the key fight
events.description: Token collected from the defeated Visitor, Deckmaster Seven of Ten.
events.evidence: we took the token after the fight

events.name: Prepare for Trouble!
events.description: Triggered for being forced to fight two card-wielding opponents at once (the turkey shaman and the spiked ogre); winning will complete the nine tokens needed to face the final boss.
events.reward: If they survive: a turkey deep-fryer and access to archived posts of the Grandma's Cooking Recipes Facebook group
events.evidence: double combat starts in the turkey barn

events.text: Current active deck shown: 18 cards. Totems: Shi Maria, Jola, Lazarus A-Bang-Bang, Skylar Spinach, Geraldo, Raul. Utility: Stout (x2), Time Extend, Greased Lightning (x2). Snare: Hobble (consumable), Hole in the Bag, Damnation, Lamp (consumable). Mystic: The Thief, Force Discard. Special: Flee (consumable).
events.evidence: deck list displayed at chapter open

events.text: System message: Carl's Extinction Sigil charm effect (from zeroing out a lizard species on the fourth floor, which normally pacifies the lizards' natural enemies — turkeys) is negated by the boss event.
events.evidence: notification when the turkey fight starts

events.cause: Dropped to cover Mongo's rescue and later to hold off the idolized turkeys

events.cause: Tossed at the pecking turkeys; it detonated prematurely due to the barn's heat lamps

events.cause: Hurled at Sharp-Elbows but exploded prematurely in midair, hurting Carl
events.evidence: sticky bomb detonates halfway to the ogre

events.text: System warning: the barn's environment (heat lamps) can cause Carl's bombs to explode prematurely — even holding one is dangerous. The heaters were placed specifically to counter his explosives.
events.evidence: warning when examining a hob-lobber

events.text: Warning against Donut's stolen Unk Tehi Temple Guard: the creature inflicts 20% more damage on Carl due to his Extinction Sigil.
events.evidence: Unk Tehi Temple Guard description warning

events.cause: One regular sphere thrown at Sharp-Elbows, one spiked sphere thrown at Tom, breaking the boss turkey's wing
events.evidence: banger spheres thrown during the double fight

events.text: Carl casts his Fear spell at full strength, zeroing out his mana; the spell is stated to be level 11. Tom's Ralphie mystic card then dispels all active magic and mutes spellcasting for Carl's team, with the AI noting Carl counts as a minion during card battles.
events.evidence: Fear cast then dispelled by Ralphie card

events.text: The Scavenger's Daughter back patch announces it has been fed (soul bar full). Carl activates the Daughter's Kiss skill — it works despite the spell mute — and the resulting kick obliterates Tom (level 75 turkey shaman, Deckmaster Eight of Ten), bowing out the barn wall.
events.evidence: Daughter's Kiss kill on Tom

events.effects: Holds an unclaimed token needed for the key fight
events.description: Carl scoops the mangled boss turkey corpse into his inventory before it can slide off the collapsing cliff; it cannot be looted mid-combat.

events.cause: Drunk back-to-back to survive being impaled and blinded by Sharp-Elbows' venomous spikes

events.cause: Drunk after Raul broke the necro staff; instantly restored Carl's sight

events.text: After the fight (Combat Complete, Deckmaster Nine, Sharp-Elbows level 80, killed by Raul), Carl has multiple debuffs including Woozy, Sore as Shit, and Bonked. events.evidence: debuff list after Combat Complete

events.effects: Holds an unclaimed token needed for the key fight
