events.text: Donut's magical staff totem (imbued with Clockwork Triplicate) was stolen by Judge Lucian's steal-totem snare (Snatched!) and later snapped in half by Shi Maria; the staff card itself stayed persistent in Donut's hand.
events.evidence: staff teleports to hydra after Snatched message

events.cause: drunk after Alpha Carl's shrapnel hob-lobber shredded Carl's left arm
events.evidence: Carl heals after diving from shrapnel blast

events.cause: second invisibility drunk before moving in on the hydra
events.evidence: Carl downs invisibility after healing his arm

events.cause: picked up from the ground (one Carl dropped) and drunk manually mid-fight
events.evidence: Donut tosses head back drinking dropped potion

events.cause: Donut slapped the dropped potion ball against Mongo's leg to turn him invisible
events.evidence: potion ball breaks on Mongo, he disappears

events.cause: orcish-steel collar locked onto the fire head's neck, then shrunk with the shrink wand to decapitate it
events.evidence: collar clicks onto Bea's mom's neck

events.text: An unnamed achievement notification appeared and went unread when Carl decapitated the fire-resistant head with the shrunken snap collar.
events.evidence: achievement flashes as the head flies away

events.cause: smashed against the decapitated neck stump to cauterize it; the splashback set Carl on fire
events.evidence: last jug shattered on neck wound

events.text: While burning, Carl received a notification from his god Emberus that came and went unread.
events.evidence: Emberus notification while Carl burns

events.cause: drunk to spread out healing while on fire; kept health out of the red
events.evidence: Carl self-heals while napalm-like fire burns

events.text: Shi Maria's magic third eye connected directly into Carl's mind without blinding or maddening him (the protective potion did not work as expected); she 'muffled the echo of his kin' and told him he will soon owe her a favor.
events.evidence: spider's eye bores into Carl mid-fight

events.text: Carl and Donut defeated the Reminiscence Hydra of Malicious Compliance, a Level 125 City Boss and keymaster with nine heads shaped like people they knew (Winner! Combat Complete).
events.evidence: hydra body bursts, Winner and Combat Complete

events.effects: Key needed to descend to the ninth floor.
events.description: The key dropped by the keymaster city boss; taken from the hydra's corpse right after the fight.
events.evidence: key taken from hydra corpse before looting yard

events.effects: Shows all remaining bosses in the area; it showed none left.
events.description: The map that city bosses always drop, taken from the hydra's corpse along with the floor key.
events.evidence: map from corpse shows no bosses remaining

events.title: Devotee (rank in the church of Emberus)
events.effects: Via the 'Baptism by Fire' church message: highest rank obtainable for a non-Cleric/non-Paladin. Burn immunity upgraded so heat and lava environments (like Sheol) no longer deal environmental damage (direct contact with fire/lava still harms). Tithe raised from 5% to 10% effective immediately.
events.evidence: church message after hydra fight, Baptism by Fire

events.effects: Devotee rank benefit: castable once a day, cooldown resets with the obeisance countdown, costs no mana but requires Emberus' good graces. Creates a sphere of Sheol Fire around the caster with a 1-foot body buffer, expanding 0.15 meters per spell level (1.5 meters at level 10); the fire instantly destroys wood, melts rock, and is extremely dangerous to flesh.
events.evidence: spell description Carl reads after rank message

events.effects: Adds a backup slot allowing two extra reserve totems (eight carried total, still six in the active deck) and includes two Flee cards, whose mechanics will change in the final phase.
events.description: A leather bag decorated with the team's boxer-pattern flag, given only to squads that defeat the keymaster. Contains the backup totem slots, a champion trophy for the personal space, a cooler of yellow Gatorade, and two Flee cards. Only Donut, as squad leader, could take it.
events.evidence: boxer-patterned bag beside the hydra's card deck

events.text: The dead hydra's deck was left as loot: four totems (Alpha Male Carl level 69, King Croissant level 60, Bam-Bam the level-70 Belly Acher, and Leveled-Up Frank, a level-90 Blood Assassin Night Elf modeled on dead crawler Frank Q), plus a Steal Totem mystic card, two Cripple snares, the Temporary Shield utility Donut had stolen and used, and several Time Extend and Heal Totem utilities. Carl and Donut decided to keep none of the totems; Donut ripped King Croissant and Bam-Bam to dust.
events.evidence: deck floating over burned trailer, totems examined

events.effects: Level 69 totem modeled on Carl; cannot be ripped from the deck until another card is obtained.
events.description: The card added itself to Donut's cards against her wishes after she disposed of the other two totems.
events.evidence: remaining totem cards blink and add themselves

events.effects: Level 90 Blood Assassin Night Elf totem modeled on dead crawler Frank Q; cannot be ripped until another card is obtained.
events.description: The card added itself to Donut's cards on its own even though Carl refused to keep it.
events.evidence: remaining totem cards blink and add themselves

events.name: Devotee Rank Benefits
events.description: Immunity to environmental heat and lava, plus a once-daily level-10 Self-Immolation (tithe rises to 10%).
events.evidence: upgraded burn immunity and daily self-immolation at Devotee rank

events.item: beach battle loot (monk seal and land crab corpses, gold, magical accessories)
events.description: A mile-long line of loot washed up on the Florida shoreline from a mass battle near Cuba: dozens to hundreds of dead level-65 monk seal warriors and crab pieces, an estimated nearly a million gold pieces, and hundreds of magical accessories. Carl began pulling the corpses into his inventory.
events.evidence: line of glittering Xs along the beach

events.text: System message on entering the saferoom: Carl's Book of Voodoo updated with multiple entries.
events.evidence: message when entering the Christmas-decorated saferoom

events.name: LBC!
events.description: Awarded for successfully taunting an opponent with a garment: Carl's cloak, made from the flesh of a Nightgaunt's friend, enraged the Nightgaunt deckmaster and kept it from using its deck.
events.reward: Gold Talk of the Town Box
events.evidence: first notable achievement Carl opens at party

events.name: Pincushioned!
events.description: Awarded for having both eyeballs pierced at the same time, which happened when Sharp Elbows slammed into Carl.
events.evidence: second achievement, eyeball piercing from Sharp Elbows

events.name: Watch where you're waving that thing, big boy.
events.description: Awarded for deploying a wand in battle and using it creatively to help kill an opponent (the shrink wand on the snap collar).
events.reward: Wand-casting skill raised to level five, plus a Gold Wand Box
events.evidence: third achievement, wand used creatively
achievement reward raises wand-casting to five

events.name: Mass Layoffs!
events.description: Given because Carl survived a boss battle whose boss was created wholly by the system AI with no input parameters; the AI treats it as its own milestone after seizing control from Borant.
events.reward: Nothing except the satisfaction of surviving into this new era
events.evidence: final achievement, AI brags about its breakthrough

events.description: Sent by Carl's sponsor, the Open Intellect Pacifist Network, Intergalactic NFC. It had been seized via Liaison action, but the seizure was overridden per Syndicate rule 855.C; the system suggested Carl open it alone.
events.evidence: benefactor box message after abdication announcements

events.description: More of the trench-building scrolls Carl already collects, from a dozen adventurer boxes; exact quantity not stated.
events.evidence: dozen adventurer boxes opened in Carl's room

events.description: A few invisibility potions from the adventurer boxes; exact quantity not stated.
events.evidence: adventurer boxes yield a few invisibility potions

events.description: Carl's first naturally looted Size-Up potion, meaning more will drop in the future and he won't need to trade Prepotente for them.
events.evidence: first natural size-up potion from adventurer boxes

events.description: A deep fryer still in its packaging, from the turkey-shaped Gold Gobble Box earned in the turkey-barn fight.
events.evidence: Gold Gobble box opened first among gold boxes

events.description: Multiple jugs of peanut oil accompanying the deep fryer in the Gold Gobble Box; exact quantity not stated.
events.evidence: Gold Gobble box contents with fryer

events.description: A massive pile of papers printing out every recipe ever posted to a Facebook group, from the Gold Gobble Box.
events.evidence: pile of recipe papers in Gobble box

events.effects: Grants the Book Burner benefit: once per day, force an opponent of Carl's level or lower to forget a spell for 30 hours (5% failure chance per opponent player level above his own); requires knowing the spell's exact name or targeting the last spell cast. The patch is destroyed if ever removed from a garment.
events.description: A medium embroidered rectangular patch depicting a book on fire, from the Gold Talk of the Town Box. Affixing it to an eligible garment applies its upgrades.
events.evidence: newspaper-shaped Talk of the Town box reveals patch

events.effects: 10 charges. Casts a level-10 Sleep on a single non-boss target for five minutes; the target wakes if physically damaged, and multiple casts compound the effect.
events.description: A gnarled wooden wand from the Gold Wand Box; Carl stored it in his inventory.
events.evidence: wand clatters from wand box, examined later

events.description: Gold from the hydra boss box, which Carl considered mostly a dud.
events.evidence: boss box from hydra fight contains gold

events.effects: Requires the wearer to wield a sword ('Warrior's' prefix), so Carl cannot use it; set aside for the gear pile for the next floor.
events.description: An armor helmet from the hydra boss box, added to the growing stash of magical gear to hand out later.
events.evidence: unusable helmet from boss box

events.description: The sole content of the benefactor box: a dirty, crumpled flyer for a homeless shelter in Homestead, Florida advertising Christmas breakfast at 7 AM, with the 7 AM circled in red and the 'No pets allowed' rule circled. Carl reads it as a summons to be there in about five hours, alone.
events.evidence: benefactor box holds only crumpled shelter flyer

events.effects: Drinking it grants level five in the Find Crawler skill; it does not work if that skill is already at level five or above.
events.description: A skill potion from the Platinum That-Was-Disgusting box.
events.evidence: platinum box from eyeball achievement yields potion

events.cause: Carl drank it immediately without questioning it
events.evidence: Carl downs the skill potion at once
skill potion grants level five Find Crawler

events.description: Donut's Christmas present 'from Santa': a majorette hat that Li Jun found on the previous floor.
events.evidence: Louis reports Donut opened her present

events.name: The Book Burner Benefit
events.description: Once per day, force an opponent of his level or lower to forget a named spell for 30 hours.
events.evidence: Book Burner benefit on the +15% intelligence patch
scouting shelter area, notes new skill only level 5

events.text: Carl receives a fan box notification while his viewer count spikes outside the shelter.
events.evidence: after casting detection spells at the shelter

events.description: Sealed plastic storage bin belonging to the Residual known as Paulie/Goff, taken into inventory before its contents were examined.
events.evidence: grabs Residual's bin during shelter breakfast memory

events.cause: Traded to liaison Orren under threat of death; bin contained Valtay neural enhancement pills, a communication device, a weapon, a shield, and dirty clothes.
events.evidence: forced trade negotiated in Orren's office with Quasar

events.description: Scroll received as part of the trade compensation for the Residual's bin.

events.description: Three scrolls called Cracker Jack received in the trade with Orren for the Residual's bin.

events.text: As part of the trade, Orren agrees the four cretins may stay in the team's service for the duration of the ninth floor.

events.text: Carl asks for the Gate of the Feral Gods back early; Orren refuses, though it will return automatically when the floor collapses.
events.evidence: after trade, Orren refuses early return

events.text: Admin message confirms the squad holds a stairwell key for El Capitolio Nacional de Cuba; location marked on the map. Two of five squads in the Cuba zone hold keys.
events.evidence: system messages after transfer back to Cuba

events.cause: Used two of the three Cracker Jack scrolls to unlock a pair of alien personal shields for himself and Donut.
events.evidence: unlocking looted hunter tech in the guildhall

events.effects: Round pinback button that takes no equipment slot; projects an inch-thick buffer against physical damage and some corporeal magic for about two minutes, then needs roughly an hour to recharge.
events.evidence: clips newly unlocked shield button to himself

events.effects: Opalescent longhorn-shaped pin attached to her collar; surrounds her in a moving bubble shield, reportedly more powerful than Carl's, with a mount option that can also cover Mongo or a vehicle at reduced strength.
events.evidence: shield pin attached to Donut's collar after unlocking

events.text: Carl's alarm trap and three of his teleport traps placed at the Capitol exit stairwell activate, teleporting at least three intruders to scattered locations.
events.evidence: trap activation notifications after key announcement
admits riding skill hit level nine on return trip

events.text: Warning received that their location was pinpointed by an Eye in the Sky spell; Mordecai identifies it as a rare level-10 scroll that reveals their position and level to the caster.
events.evidence: warning message shortly after leaving Guanajay

events.text: Quest failed: The Chowder War.
events.evidence: quest failure notice while scouting the beach

events.text: New quest received: Hell Comes to Crawler Town. Win condition is simply surviving and getting down the stairs without being dragged into hell during the Demon Eviction event. Stated reward: a Legendary Quest Box plus a commemorative set of bagpipes (Donut's version offered a kilt instead).
events.evidence: new quest text after Chowder War fails

events.effects: Lowers the draw countdown on her cards by two seconds.
events.evidence: swaps to sailor hat preparing for card battle

events.text: Current active deck of twenty-two cards displayed. Totems: Shi Maria, Jola, Lazarus A-Bang-Bang, Skylar Spinach, Geraldo, Raul. Utility: Stout, Time Extend, Greased Lightning, Heal Totem, Temporary Shield. Snare: Hobble (consumable), Hole in the Bag, Damnation, Lamp (consumable), Cripple (consumable). Mystic: The Thief, Steal Totem (consumable), Force Discard, Golden Combo. Special: Sentient Staff Totem, Flee (consumable).
events.evidence: active deck screen shown before the boss battle

events.text: Recap confirms Donut's Laundry Day spell was previously raised to level 15 using Carl's Pawna's Tears potion after Samantha denied being able to enhance spells.
events.evidence: narration recapping previous-floor spell boost

events.text: Carl's Book of Voodoo updates with an entry for Sister Ines Quiteria.
events.evidence: notification as combatants land around them

events.cause: Traded in his first dungeon ring to the Club Vanquisher jeweler as the required base for the new Emberus enchantment.
events.evidence: hands strength ring to satyr jeweler

events.text: Carl takes an interest-free loan of 990,000 gold from the Temple of Emberus after a 10,000 gold down payment (total price 1,000,000 gold). He cannot renounce his religion while under debt; payments go to the Emberus shrine.
events.evidence: loan system message in enchantment shop

events.name: Columbia House!
events.description: Earned for taking on a financial debt; the system compares the deal to mail-order music clubs that lured customers with cheap albums and negative option billing, warning he now has to pay one way or another.
events.reward: A mocking prediction of a debt-ridden life, plus a cassette copy of Gloria Estefan's 1989 album Cuts Both Ways.
events.evidence: achievement popup after taking the loan

events.item: Enchanted Pauper's Ring of the Steadfast Emberus
events.effects: Removes most health-seeping and disease-based debuff effects and numbs various other debuffs without removing them; can mute or overheat if overloaded. Only wearable by an Emberus worshipper; disguised as an enchanted Strength ring; carries the attached church debt and cannot be sold; curses non-worshippers with every debuff suffered within 50 meters.
events.description: Carl's original strength ring re-enchanted at the Blessed Sun Enchantments shop to counter his slug condition, financed by a church loan.
events.evidence: jeweler returns re-enchanted ring, description shown

events.item: Enchanted Pauper's Ring of the Steadfast Emberus
events.evidence: slips re-enchanted ring onto his finger
Quan at level 70, one above Carl's own

events.item: stairwell key (El Capitolio Nacional de Cuba)
events.cause: Quan Ch swapped hands with Donut and used Force Play to make her squad play a Flee card; the key transferred automatically to Team Quan along with Team Quiteria's key.
events.evidence: forced Flee card, key transfer system messages

events.name: The Fumble
events.description: Awarded for losing the key at the worst possible moment, compared to Earnest Byner fumbling the ball at the goal line with everything on the line.
events.reward: None; only a warning that this is how they will be remembered if they do not fix it.
events.evidence: achievement after teleporting away from lost battle
narration on skills while loading spider automatons

events.text: Carl deploys all four of his two-part teleport trap sets: trigger halves armed in the clearing, anchor halves loaded onto spider automatons and into Samantha.
events.evidence: trap setup before launching Samantha toward capitol

events.cause: Drunk to sneak into the capitol building fight
events.evidence: before rushing up the capitol stairs

events.name: Holy Hell!
events.description: Awarded for being in the presence of a Demon Prince of Sheol after Amayon involuntarily entered the realm.
events.reward: Platinum To Hell in a Handbasket Box

events.item: Platinum To Hell in a Handbasket Box

events.text: Quest update for 'Find out who killed my son': Carl is told he can question the demon prince Amayon about who murdered Geyrun.
events.evidence: quest update after Amayon's arrival

events.effects: Earlier shown absorbing a Chain Lightning strike, leaving Quan unharmed
events.description: A shining magical cloak blown off Quan by Jola's Fashion Critic spell; Carl scoops it into his inventory.
events.evidence: cloak lands at Carl's feet after Fashion Critic

events.text: An unnamed achievement popped up when Carl grabbed Quan's cloak; he dismissed it without reading it.
events.evidence: achievement waved away after taking cloak

events.cause: Carl returns the ring to the dying Quan at his request
events.evidence: Carl extracts ring from severed arm in inventory

events.text: Donut receives kill credit and the bounty for the death of top-ten crawler Quan Ch ("A champion has fallen. A bounty has been claimed.").
events.evidence: system message and Donut's chat after Quan dies

events.effects: Grants access to the ninth-floor exit stairwells
events.description: Both of Quan's stairwell keys are awarded to Donut because she got credit for his kill.
events.evidence: Donut's chat: given both keys after Quan's death

events.effects: Enchanted flamethrower that is part breastplate, part weapon
events.description: Ren's named flamethrower rig, unsnapped and given to Carl as she donates her gear before dying.
events.evidence: Ren kicks flamethrower to Carl in capitol chamber

events.item: assorted gear from Ren (rings, clothing, potions, scrolls including Eye in the Sky scrolls)
events.effects: Includes multiple Eye in the Sky scrolls; count not stated
events.description: Ren empties her inventory onto the floor and tells Carl to take everything as a donation to the war effort.
events.evidence: items appear on floor after Ren gives up

events.cause: The capitol stairwell exit collapsed and all its associated keys were destroyed
events.evidence: warning message when capitol exit is rendered inaccessible

events.item: Garret (Tummy Acher pet, in pet carrier)
events.effects: Regenerating tank pet, reset to level 1; resets every five deaths and gains a new weakness each regeneration
events.description: Ren kills her pet with the Knife of Frozen Tears to reset him, then gives him to Carl to raise. Known weaknesses: sylvan crystal, consuming hobgoblin sweat, rivenwing bite, consuming undead flesh, and the new one, Reaper Gaze.
events.evidence: Ren slides pet carrier to Carl

events.text: A host of pet-themed achievements appeared and were dismissed when Carl took Garret's carrier into his inventory; none were read.
events.evidence: after accepting the pet carrier

events.item: assorted gear from Khulan (rings, potions, glowing spear, armor items, unread magical tomes, coins)
events.description: Khulan also drops her equipment and a large amount of coins for Carl before the two women end their run.
events.evidence: Khulan drops items after Ren's prompt

events.item: Ren's card deck (all non-totem cards, including Glow-Up and The Midnight Express)
events.effects: Includes rare special and mystic cards; a consumable Glow-Up card and The Midnight Express consumable special card
events.description: Ren hands over her entire card collection minus totems, meant for Donut. Carl must carry them physically (waistband) because they cannot enter his inventory.
events.evidence: Ren hands cards after rearranging her deck

events.effects: Includes many magical weapons (swords, spears, war hammers, a magical chainsaw), a high-value sock filled with nickels, a decapitating boomerang bowler hat, and almost five million gold coins
events.description: Carl transfers Quan's entire inventory to himself after the kill, leaving only the gold ring in Quan's hand. Much of it is loot Quan took from crawlers he killed.
events.evidence: looting Quan's body before leaving the capitol

events.text: Carl's Ring of Divine Suffering will gain a new feature on the next floor: he will be able to mark as many people as he wants at once. On the current floor the mark takes thirty seconds to form.
events.evidence: Carl considers marking Ren while leaving building

events.text: Donut now displays a new golden player killer skull from getting kill credit on Quan.
events.evidence: narration after Samantha teleport reunion

events.text: Inventory review: the cloak taken from Quan is a celestial item; top loot also includes the Eye in the Sky scrolls, magical tomes, Velma the flamethrower, and the nickel sock.
events.evidence: Carl's mental inventory while sneaking through Havana

events.text: Carl states he holds exactly five Eye in the Sky scrolls, which Mordecai says are very rare and can locate a crawler outside saferooms.
events.evidence: chat with Mordecai about finding Sister Ines

events.description: Carl reports the system has been seeding Build Trench scrolls into crawlers' inventories like the first-floor bandages and torches; he estimates he has 'like 50' (approximate count).
events.evidence: Carl's broadcast asking who else got Build Trench scrolls

events.text: Carl confirms he has the Super Spreader spell (same as Doctor Bones's, but weaker): he can only infect one person once per hour.
events.evidence: chat with Imani about the Sluggalo-infected mercenaries

events.text: Carl directs Mordecai to take the pile of satchel bombs from his bomber's studio (made for the next floor) and list them on the open market so other crawlers can use them for demolition.
events.evidence: group chat instructions about clearing stairwell chambers

events.text: New regional quest 'Where the Sidewalk Ends' with Carl and Donut's squad designated as host; a two-hour countdown timer appears. All crawlers in Cuba are added. Options: do nothing (Sheol portal inhales the Cuba region in two hours and stays open for the rest of the floor), free Amayon from his shell (timer resets, quest becomes a World quest), or kill Amayon, who is invulnerable on this floor (reward: a Celestial Hell Boon).
events.evidence: quest notification after Carl rallies the crawlers

events.item: Personal Space Upgrade. Tier 3 Doggie Door
events.effects: Upgrades the personal-space door into a fast-travel waypoint system: exit through any door previously used on the same floor, with shared access for guests and party members.
events.description: A tier-3 personal space upgrade module dropped by Lucia Mar and handed to Carl by Li Jun; installed via the personal space tab of the interface. One was already installed in Imani's room for the Meadowlark party.
events.evidence: Li Jun hands Carl module Lucia Mar dropped

events.cause: Lent to Zhang for the controlled demolition of the church over the stairwell
events.evidence: Carl lends shrink wand before church demolition

events.text: Full description shown for Spunky Jefferson the Enchanted Nickel Sock of the Elderly Miser, already in Carl's inventory (looted from Quan). A sapient, incremental-damage weapon upgraded twice, originally from a Legendary loot box on the fifth floor: damage grows 1.5% per qualifying kill (currently at a 238% damage increase), grants the wielder +10 dexterity plus 10%, has a 5% chance to inflict Bonked, and killed mobs have a 20% chance to burst into gold coins equal to inventory value plus level x 10.
events.evidence: Carl reads nickel sock description seeking flail for Dong

events.item: Spunky Jefferson the Enchanted Nickel Sock of the Elderly Miser
events.cause: Given to the stripper Dong Quixote as a flail-like replacement for his oversized lance
events.evidence: Carl hands sapient sock weapon to Dong

events.text: Donut casts Minion Army for the first time (spell book received near the end of the first floor). The spell is level one: it turns 2% of enemies in the casting area against their comrades, freezes the caster for the four-to-five-minute cast, and her Charisma boosts widened the target selection and shortened the cast time. Enhanced by Imani and Samantha to roughly 5%.
events.evidence: Donut casts Minion Army on demon horde

events.text: Carl deploys his last spider automaton from inventory at the roof stairwell, carrying both ends of a teleport trap so the snake city boss can be teleported in place on command via a remote clicker.
events.evidence: automaton with teleport trap placed at roof door

events.text: Carl uses his new Nighty-Night wand on Sister Ines; it instantly knocks her unconscious with a two-minute timer over her.
events.evidence: wand zaps charging Sister Ines unconscious

events.text: Temporary effect from the goddess Ysalte: any potions quaffed or physically handled outside the inventory turn to vinegar, and will return as more powerful versions of themselves when she is banished.
events.evidence: Ysalte presence notification after Sister Ines dies

events.text: Quest 'Where the Sidewalk Ends' completes. A new hybrid world quest begins: 'The Missing Piece', part one called 'The Big O' — a ten-minute card battle against the level 250 goddess Ysalte (who cannot be killed and has an enhanced version of Sister Ines's deck) while Amayon plays his six totems; Amayon must be freed from her control and powered up before the timer ends or everyone without keys dies.
events.evidence: quest swap after Amayon takes true form

events.text: A new interface graphic shows each team's card count: Donut has 21 cards, with the first six (the totems) highlighted. Ysalte has about 17, Amayon only his six totems.
events.evidence: card counter graphic at battle start

events.effects: A totem card for a gorilla-sized shadow-cloud creature with dark tentacles.

events.cause: Used to strike the succubus Naamah; it bounced off her head doing little damage

events.cause: Clicked from inventory during succubus fight; it had turned to vinegar due to Ysalte's debuff

events.name: A Kombucha!

events.evidence: achievement flashes after vinegar potion

events.text: Quest update for The Missing Piece Part One: Amayon lacks the power and souls to cast his return spell. The world must be refilled with the same kind of souls that triggered the demon eviction event, then Amayon must be powered up before the timer runs out.
events.evidence: mid-battle quest update during world freeze

events.cause: Consumable card (one of two Ren gave Carl before she died) played by Donut; it transferred deckmaster control to Carl for three minutes, then expired
events.evidence: Donut plays Midnight Express on Carl's signal

events.text: An unnamed achievement appears as Midnight Express takes effect and Carl temporarily becomes the deckmaster ('Tag! You're it!') for three minutes, gaining Donut's hand of cards while she is freed to cast spells and use her inventory.

events.text: Revealed: Donut had earlier used Golden Combo to temporarily fuse the Lazarus and Skylar Spinach totems, then added Ren's other consumable card, Glow Up, to make the combination permanent — a powerful combo since one card may be carried to the next floor. Alpha Male Carl fills the freed totem slot.
events.evidence: Carl recalls deck setup while holding cards

events.cause: Carl played the Time Extend card on the Raul totem to keep him summoned after Midnight Express expired

events.text: Carl pulls the teleport trap trigger from his inventory and steps into it, teleporting himself to the trap he had previously hidden inside Samantha.

events.text: Carl deliberately drives his health below 5% inside Samantha and clicks an interface button: 'The Martyr's Path has engulfed your steps' and 'The Scavenger's Daughter has enhanced your fire attack' — every outdoor footstep he made bursts into Sheol fire, bathing Amayon in it and setting the whole city ablaze. His health bottoms out at 2% with a line of debuffs.
events.evidence: Martyr's Path button pressed at 7% health

events.text: Quest Complete: The Missing Piece, Part One. The timer stopped with two seconds left because crawler Paz Lo (a totem) slew the goddess Ysalte, ending the card battle threat.
events.evidence: quest complete after Paz kills Ysalte

events.text: With Ysalte banished, all of Carl's vinegar-infused potions outside his inventory are enhanced, and her remaining effects will fade with time. The Scavenger's Daughter looks away but keeps her eyes open to Amayon's presence.
events.evidence: Ysalte leaves realm, vinegar debuff lifts

events.cause: Used while trapped and taking damage in Amayon's fist after a Heal potion came up vinegar
events.evidence: heal scroll used inside demon's hand

events.cause: Multiple Heal potions downed while being crushed and cooked near Sheol fire; one was still vinegar, later ones worked at half effectiveness
events.evidence: repeated Heal potions in Amayon's hand

events.text: Donut reports that the Alpha Carl and Raul totems timed out and returned to her deck, and that Raul is now much stronger after fertilizing the crab eggs.
events.evidence: Donut message after quest completes

events.cause: Totem dragged into hell by a demon crab, eliminating the card
events.evidence: demon crab grabs Lazarus, Totem Eliminated message

events.text: System announces the Scavenger's Daughter has been fed and is ready to unleash; Carl's interface bar crackles with black energy but he holds off using it.
events.evidence: notification after punching crab in tower

events.text: Donut's Golden Combo card misfires: Alpha Carl is killed before combining, and the system redirects the combo to Carl himself, merging him with the Shi Maria totem.
events.evidence: Golden Combo redirect message after Alpha Carl dies

events.text: System warning: Carl is temporarily combined with the Shi Maria totem; if slain in this state he will not regenerate. The combination times out in 180 seconds or when the battle ends.
events.evidence: warning message at chapter open
Shi Maria unequips shirt before tattooing chest

events.cause: Shi Maria used the sliver with Carl's alchemy supplies to brew tattoo ink
events.evidence: sliver placed in beaker, becomes black ink

events.effects: Magical black tattoo ink; Shi Maria says the remainder will be used for future power-sharing tattoos and warns not to poke too deep
events.description: A beaker of bubbling black ink brewed by Shi Maria from Carl's own supplies while she controlled his body, used to tattoo the Eye of the Bedlam Bride.
events.evidence: beaker fills with black liquid, pulled into inventory

events.cause: Shi Maria (controlling Carl) combined potions and dropped two for the burned Mongo and Katia
events.evidence: potions dropped for the animal and doppelganger

events.cause: Dropped for Li Jun after his eye was taken
events.evidence: third gold potion dropped for Li Na's brother

events.text: Carl receives a magical chest tattoo, identified by the system only as 'Tattoo. The Eye of the Bedlam Bride' with no other description. It binds Shi Maria's presence to him even after her card leaves play; the tattooed eye blinks then fades invisible against his skin.
events.evidence: system label after self-inflicted chest tattoo

events.text: The totem combination times out: Carl returns to his regular form and Shi Maria returns to Donut's deck.
events.evidence: timeout message near chapter end

events.item: Eye of the Bedlam Bride Tattoo
events.description: A self-administered stick-and-poke tattoo in the center of his chest, just above the sternum, inked with toraline-sliver ink.
events.evidence: rapid-fire the tattoo into myself... right into the center of my chest

events.cause: Mass kills of zombies and other monsters when the Sheol fire destroyed the city
events.evidence: Donut reports levels during retreat from tower

events.cause: Mass kills when the Sheol fire destroyed the city
events.evidence: Donut reports levels during retreat from tower

events.cause: Donut ripped the card as soon as the battle ended so Shi Maria could never hurt Mongo again
events.evidence: Donut says she ripped the spider card

events.cause: Group agreed to rip the card to set Paz free; card now read 'Paz Lo. The God Predator. Level 101 Former Crawler.' No player killer skull was awarded
events.evidence: Donut rips Paz card against Carl's shoulder

events.text: Zhang returns Carl's shrink wand after using only two charges, leaving it with seven charges; Carl also holds a rare Wand Recharge scroll in reserve.
events.evidence: Zhang messages about wand before church approach

events.text: Quest update 'The Missing Piece, Part Two: Falling Up': Amayon's spell now covers the whole world; a 30-minute timer appears, after which everything is sucked upward into the hell portal, letting crawlers ride an enclosed stairwell chamber to the next floor.
events.evidence: quest update system message near church

events.text: Carl spends the Princess Posse's first of three Faction Wars Emergency Action Items; the AI decides in their favor, sending all pending action-item votes to the audience.
events.evidence: Warlord interface emergency action item exchange

events.text: Key Faction Wars votes resolve: the Princess Posse's items pass, removing safety protections for faction sponsors (98% audience vote) and adding a tenth NPC team based in Larracos led by Juice Box and Sir Ferdinand (52%); the playing field is also enlarged.
events.evidence: pending action item result messages

events.effects: Unopened benefactor box; contents unknown this chapter
events.description: Donut's first celestial-tier benefactor box, sent by the garbage truck company (Long Haul Biological Waste Management), received moments before the floor collapse.
events.evidence: Donut announces celestial box before ascent

events.text: A voice in Carl's mind (the Bedlam Bride) warns he failed to diffuse the Scavenger's Daughter soul power with his Daughter's Kiss, calling it a dangerous mistake to let it linger.
events.evidence: voice in mind as jacket centipede image glows

events.text: Quest Complete: Hell Comes to Crawler Town.
events.evidence: quest complete message after ascent

events.text: Quest Complete: The Missing Piece.
events.evidence: quest complete message after ascent
sucked into portal, Welcome to Faction Wars

events.text: The epilogue reveals Donut's Celestial box was sent by former crawlers Tipid and Rosetta from the converted garbage freighter Homecoming Queen.
events.evidence: Rosetta mentions the Celestial box they sent Donut

events.text: Fifty thousand former crawlers hidden aboard the Homecoming Queen are cleared for on-boarding as troopers for the Princess Posse, Carl and Donut's Faction Wars team.
events.evidence: Tipid's intercom announcement after on-boarding clearance

events.effects: Unregistered Valtay-built neural enhancement that adds a new menu giving access to the AI containment and the ability to trigger the system 'fuse' failsafe, which would send the system's star nova.
events.description: A bootleg brain-interface enhancement delivered via Paulie's coffee; installs over time and only works with a natural interface inside this system's enhancement zone.
events.evidence: Carl drinks Paulie's coffee during frozen-time message

events.text: System message confirms the Containment Interface is installing; Carl will be notified when the handshake completes.
events.evidence: installation notification after drinking the coffee
opening system message, welcome to Faction Wars

events.text: Floor-entry status screen: Views 231 septillion, Followers 887 Quadrillion, Favorites 420 Quadrillion, Leaderboard rank 1, Bounty 6,000,000 gold.
events.evidence: ninth floor welcome system message

events.description: Loot box awarded on entering the ninth floor; not opened in this chapter.
events.evidence: floor-entry congratulations message

events.item: Multiple items looted in the eighth-floor memory simulation
events.cause: Previous floor's ruleset removed items looted from the memory simulation from inventory; specific items not listed in the text.
events.evidence: inventory warning in floor-entry messages

events.text: Dungeon mail via Liaison action: the Gate of the Feral Gods is now available and waiting in a dungeon mailbox (not yet collected).
events.evidence: dungeon mail notification in floor-entry messages

events.text: Quest update for The Bedlam Bride: Carl reached the city with the spider (as the chest tattoo) but must release her to complete the quest and get its prize.
events.evidence: Bedlam Bride quest update popup on arrival

events.text: Carl has a worsening Soul Poisoning condition from unspent soul energy stored in the Scavenger's Daughter back patch; it causes jolts of pain, is partially blocked by his Pauper's ring of the Steadfast Emberus, and unequipping the jacket while soul power is full would be catastrophic.
events.evidence: health menu condition after stomach-cramp pain

events.name: Sushi Smoothie
events.description: An improvised achievement for obliterating a dead level 41 Concierge Shark with a Daughter's Kiss punch, vaporizing the corpse.
events.evidence: after punching dead shark in alley

events.description: Gag reward from the Sushi Smoothie achievement.
events.evidence: Sushi Smoothie achievement reward line

events.text: Carl and Donut onboard into Faction Wars as warlords of the Princess Posse: territory spot three, army of 58 soldiers with no officers, unapproved recruits waiting at headquarters, and between-floor protection enhancements removed. Eight enemy teams form 'The Bloc' and declare war on the Princess Posse.
events.evidence: Faction Wars onboarding wall of text

events.text: Warlord Donut accepts a truce with Team Retribution (the NPC defenders), making the Princess Posse allied and allowed to remain in Larracos after the 60-hour ceasefire ends.
events.evidence: Donut accepts Team Retribution truce offer

events.text: Donut has an unopened celestial sponsor box and received a 'Former Child Actor' accessory kit in the saferoom mailbox, not yet collected.
events.evidence: Donut urging Carl to the saferoom

events.cause: Ninth-floor class selection (chose with Mordecai's help).
events.evidence: Donut reveals new class in alley

events.text: Deathbed Hellcat is a long-range sniper/assassin class: quadruples the range of her existing spells, gives much higher dexterity and higher instakill odds, at-will invisibility, keeps most of her previous floor's spells, and comes with a hat and a ghillie suit. No numeric values stated.
events.evidence: Donut describes her new class perks

events.effects: Occupies her 'jacket' inventory slot but magically covers her whole body; adds a Blend into the Background skill and a Cone of Silence benefit; made of color-changing fabric that camouflages into surroundings.
events.description: Cat-sized camouflage suit that came with her Deathbed Hellcat class accessories; benefits stack further when worn with the matching hat.
events.evidence: Donut's class accessory suit laid out in saferoom

events.effects: Adds a Targeting skill for zooming in on distant mobs (like a gnomish farseerer) and further increases the range of all her spells; wearing hat and ghillie suit together buffs all benefits further, allowing line-of-sight sniping from over a mile away.
events.description: Beige sniper-style boonie hat that came with her Deathbed Hellcat class accessories.
events.evidence: class accessory hat examined in saferoom

events.description: Collected from the dungeon mailbox after being delivered via Liaison action.
events.evidence: Carl collects it from saferoom mailbox

events.effects: Teaches the anti-air spell Flak when read; Mordecai says it is impressive at level 10. Not read yet in this chapter.
events.description: Donut's Spellbook of the Floor prize, pulled from the back of the saferoom mailbox.
events.evidence: Donut pulls floor-prize spellbook from mailbox

events.cause: Given to Mongo to heal his burns (potion originally crafted by Carl while he was part spider).
events.evidence: Donut says she already gave it to Mongo

events.text: Several of their potions were upgraded at the end of the previous floor: a Heal Pet potion became a super-rare 'Revitalize Pet' potion (can regrow Mongo's wing in about an hour), and a regular mana refill became 'Mana Blessing'. Mordecai takes them to analyze on his upgraded alchemy table before use.
events.evidence: Mordecai examines upgraded potions in saferoom

events.text: Donut's Heal Critter spell is stated to be level 8, which is why the party no longer uses Heal Pet potions.
events.evidence: narration while discussing the Heal Pet potion

events.text: Carl has over fifty accumulated achievements and boxes to open, the most ever at once; the minor achievements yielded dozens of bronze and silver Adventurer Boxes. He also still has an unopened Gold fan box from the homeless shelter visit plus assorted quest and achievement boxes.
events.evidence: Carl starts opening backlog of achievements

events.name: Lord of the Flies!
events.description: Awarded for setting a trap module targeting a fellow crawler that was triggered (the teleport traps at the Capitol building, set off by Ren's team).
events.reward: A Gold I Have the Conch Box.
events.evidence: first notable achievement in backlog

events.name: Demons Hate This One Trick!
events.description: Awarded for teleporting demon-possessed bodies (Samantha teleporting the beach with the possessed zombie shells).
events.reward: All of Carl's food boxes now give an unlimited supply of Pea Soup.
events.evidence: achievement about teleporting the demon-possessed

events.name: Chickenshit Exploit!
events.description: Awarded for breaking the game system with an exploit used to help kill a fellow crawler (teleporting card totems into the Quan/Ren battle).
events.evidence: exploit achievement in backlog

events.name: Celestial Loot!
events.description: Awarded for picking a celestial item up off the floor (Quan's cloak); doing it four more times triggers something special.
events.evidence: achievement from grabbing Quan's cloak

events.name: Used Pet!
events.description: Awarded for adopting a pet once bonded to someone else, though Carl notes he had not actually adopted Garret the tummy acher yet.
events.evidence: rambling pet-adoption achievement

events.name: Uh oh. It talks.
events.description: Awarded for discovering a sapient weapon (Jefferson the nickel sock, looted from Quan and currently held by Dong Quixote).
events.evidence: sapient weapon achievement

events.name: Touched by a Demon!
events.description: Awarded for being physically touched by a demon lord of Sheol and surviving; the only such achievement ever handed out.
events.reward: A cassette copy of Dio's 1983 album Holy Diver plus a Celestial Heavy Metal Box.
events.evidence: demon lord touch achievement

events.name: Crab Chowder!
events.description: A crude joke achievement referencing an act Carl denies doing.
events.reward: All food items in Carl's food boxes can now be served 'chowder style'.

events.name: Unbirthing!
events.description: Delivered in the AI's deep voice for Carl teleporting into and back out of Samantha's neck hole; the AI's unsettling enjoyment implies trouble ahead.

events.name: Male Pattern Baldness
events.description: Awarded for getting his hair singed off.

events.name: Hitchhiker
events.description: Awarded for receiving the Eye of the Bedlam Bride tattoo; gave no information about the tattoo itself.

events.text: Emberus quest update ('Find out who killed my son'): Carl must proceed to the Temple of Issitoq in a Club Vanquisher and treat with the High Cleric of the Watch to gain permission for Emberus to break the god-demon truce, and he still needs to obtain the item Soul Crystal—Apito.
events.evidence: quest update in Emberus menu

events.text: Donut opens a huge backlog of adventurer boxes (more boxes than Carl, mostly from card-based achievements), yielding large sums of gold plus more Build Trench scrolls, some Fill Trench scrolls, and Temporary Water Source scrolls. No quantities stated.
events.evidence: adventurer boxes open one by one in saferoom

events.effects: Teaches a standard, genuine Shield spell; Mordecai notes she can chain it with her tech-based shield.
events.description: From her Platinum To Hell in a Handbasket box (received for looking at Amayon at the end of the previous floor); Carl received the same box.
events.evidence: demon-head platinum box yields spellbook

events.effects: Teaches Sentry, a war protection spell.
events.description: From her Platinum Tserendolgor box, shaped like a jeweled German Shepherd with an army helmet.

events.description: A green and black tartan kilt of unclear purpose, from the Legendary Quest box for the Hell Comes to Crawler Town quest.
events.evidence: Legendary Quest box first item

events.effects: Teaches You're Not Done Yet, a spell Mordecai describes as similar to Donut's Second Chance but far more insidious; he is visibly frightened of it and later advises against learning it.
events.description: A burned-looking black tome trailing purple smoke and black root-like tendrils, from the Hell Comes to Crawler Town Legendary Quest box.
events.evidence: smoking black tome from Legendary Quest box

events.item: The Tiara of a Thousand Lights and Accompanying Charge Pillow
events.effects: Base tiara gives +10 Charisma and holds 99 Absorption Jewels. Any enchanted hat left on the charge pillow for ten hours is drained of its magic (item ruined after even one hour); charging the tiara on the pillow transfers all stored buffs permanently, one jewel per buff, duplicates not combining. Temporary effects stay temporary and burn out their jewel; cursed, god-blessed, or debt-carrying items may not transfer properly.
events.description: A custom sponsor item from Long Haul Biological Waste Management Solutions (a crest-owned company), delivered in a Celestial Benefactor box shaped like a transforming spaceship; modeled on the crest habitat Legend of a Thousand Lights. Can become one of the most powerful items in Dungeon Crawler World history with careful charging.
events.evidence: Celestial Benefactor sponsor box, tiara on red pillow

events.effects: Universal version of the charge pillow: drains any magical item over ten hours (item still ruined after one hour) but stored buffs transfer only to a like item type; transferring to an incompatible item loses the buff.
events.description: A second, nearly identical charging pillow from a Platinum benefactor box sent by the Apothecary.
events.evidence: Apothecary platinum benefactor box

events.cause: Carl smashed the vial on Mongo to regrow his missing wing; the Healing buff appeared over Mongo. Mordecai learned the recipe first with his upgraded alchemy table.
events.evidence: Carl smashes sparkling potion on Mongo

events.text: Donut starts draining hats for the tiara: her new boonie hat goes on the red charge pillow and her Tiara of the Inebriated Dragonfly on the purple pillow. Both will lose all magical properties (ten-hour transfer, no completion notification, items lock magnetically to the pillows).
events.evidence: hats placed on charge pillows on counter

events.item: The Cloak of the Benevolent Champion
events.effects: Unique celestial cloak: over 66 hours the wearer's lowest base stat rises to equal their highest base stat at time of first equip; grants a level-five Find Weakened skill (shows which of three-plus like creatures is nearest death); raises four random spells from the wearer's full arsenal to level 15 (one immediately, the rest over one week); raises one random skill to level 15 over 504 hours. Benefits require continuous wear — if another crawler equips it, all countdowns reset. First Celestial Item awarded in Dungeon Crawler World: Earth; will go to the museum display after the crawl, legacy stipend rules applying.
events.description: Quan's silky black-and-blue armed cloak, looted after his death (Donut got the kill credit); Carl gives it to Donut because it buffs the wearer's lowest stat. Its abilities custom-tailor to the wearer.
events.evidence: Carl pulls Quan's cloak from inventory for Donut

events.item: The Cloak of the Benevolent Champion
events.cause: Carl gives the celestial cloak to Donut so its lowest-stat buff raises her constitution.
events.evidence: Carl hands cloak over before Donut equips it
Donut glows as she teaches herself Shield

events.cause: Read to learn the Shield spell before equipping the cloak.
events.evidence: she learns Shield from the new spellbook

events.effects: Formerly called Charisma Cheat; for two minutes it tricks the system into reading Charisma as 30 points higher than it really is.
events.description: A potion Mordecai just crafted so the cloak locks onto an inflated 'highest stat' value.
events.evidence: Mordecai hands Donut his freshly made potion

events.cause: Mordecai states her true base charisma while explaining the MySpace Photo potion trick.
events.evidence: It's going to think your base Charisma is 193 instead of 163.

events.cause: Drunk immediately before first equipping the cloak.
events.evidence: Donut drinks the potion

events.name: Catfish
events.description: A rude achievement received for faking a higher Charisma with the potion trick. No reward mentioned.

events.item: The Cloak of the Benevolent Champion

events.item: The Tiara of a Thousand Lights

cloak's immediate spell buff after first equip

events.text: The cloak's remaining random buffs: Wall of Fire, Heal Critter, and Puddle Jumper show blinking experience icons and will rise to level 15 over about a week; one unidentifiable skill will rise to level 15 over three weeks (504 hours). Bad Attitude at 15 can now be cast on large groups; it lowers resistances and temporarily drains intelligence.
events.evidence: Donut identifies blinking spell icons after equip

events.text: Current levels stated in passing: Torch spell level 14, Laundry Day spell level 15, litterbox navigation skill level 6. Her Fireball spell comes from a bracelet and her Hover skill from the Inebriated Dragonfly tiara.
events.evidence: discussion of which spells the cloak might buff

events.text: Carl's lower-tier adventurer boxes yield more Build Trench scrolls, Invisibility potions, Size-Up potions, a dozen Temporary Water Source scrolls plus a few potion versions of the same spell, a few strange 'Emergency Repair' scrolls, and random odds and ends.
events.evidence: first several boxes open in saferoom

events.description: Approximately 2,000 rounds total ('maybe about 2,000' per the text), from Carl's Gold fan box.
events.evidence: Gold fan box contents

events.description: Black detective-style gun belt with 'Carl' pressed into the leather, clearly meant for the father's Glock still in his inventory; Carl intends never to wear or use it.

events.effects: Seeking missile attachments Carl needs to build more missiles; quantity unstated ('a group').
events.description: From the Gold I Have the Conch Box; he had been out of them for some time.
events.evidence: Gold I Have the Conch box contents

events.effects: A card with five spots for picking up Celestial items off the floor; one spot already punched (foot-shaped). Filling the card gives an unspecified special prize.

events.description: Several pet biscuits from the Gold Pet Box; quantity unstated.

events.effects: 500 gold off any one item at any PetScapes location in the dungeon (a store Carl has never heard of).

events.evidence: Platinum Tserendolgor box contents

events.item: Upgrade Patch. Small. (Reaper Spider Minion merchant)
events.effects: When affixed to an eligible garment: grants access to the Midnight Market ('safety or sanity not guaranteed'), notifications when traveling merchants are nearby, and +5% Dexterity. Fleeting item — removing it destroys it. Installing any patch also raises all of Carl's stats via his Scavenger's Daughter patch.
events.description: Black and silver die-cut patch of a traveling Reaper Spider Minion merchant carrying wicker baskets, from the Platinum Hell in a Handbasket box; not yet installed this chapter.
events.evidence: spider patch from platinum demon box

events.description: Set of bagpipes from the Legendary Hell Comes to Crawler Town quest box.
events.evidence: Legendary quest box contents

events.description: Three personal-space upgrade coupons from the Legendary Hell Comes to Crawler Town quest box.
events.evidence: Legendary quest box contents

events.effects: Must be used at an owned town; Mordecai says it will be needed for their castle. Carl and Donut each already have a lower-tier one from the Tina quest floors earlier.
events.description: A differently colored fourth coupon from the same Legendary quest box.
events.evidence: Mordecai identifies the fourth coupon

events.item: Enchanted Toe Ring of the Well-Balanced
events.effects: Must be worn on the left pinky toe. Grants Heart Balance Level 5 (store one auto-trigger potion that fires at a chosen health threshold), Mind Balance Level 5 (protection from Enslavement and Conscription), and Physical Balance Level 5 (imbues the Extra Steady Balance skill and the Walk on Air skill, the latter noted as 'already obtained').
events.description: A simple silver toe ring from the Legendary Spicy Box (Unbirthing achievement); its description says it was given to save Carl from danger he does not realize he is in.
events.evidence: Legendary Spicy Box yields silver toe ring

events.item: Hell-Kissed Celestial Skill Potion of the Prince of Woe
events.effects: Grants a random level 10 to 15 Celestial-tier combat skill the drinker does not already have, drawn from any Sheol-level mob of level 200 or higher. The bottle talks in a demonic voice.
events.description: Glittering black bubbling potion in a horned-skull bottle with a 'Drink Me' note, from the Celestial Heavy Metal Box (drum kit with Amayon-faced bass drums). Mordecai analyzes it first and learns the recipe.
events.evidence: Celestial Heavy Metal drum kit box

events.item: Enchanted Toe Ring of the Well-Balanced
events.evidence: Carl slips ring onto left pinky toe

events.text: Carl configures the ring's Heart Balance to auto-trigger a gold-standard healing potion if his health reaches 2%. He then gets an ominous notification: 'Mind Balance is currently triggered', with no further explanation — implying something is already attempting enslavement/conscription-type influence on him.
events.evidence: notifications right after equipping toe ring

events.text: The ring lists the Walk on Air skill as 'already obtained' even though Carl did not previously have it — it was Li Jun's signature skill, implying Carl gained it when Shi Maria fed him Li Jun's eye, as she said he might gain some of his skills.
events.evidence: 'already obtained' line in toe ring description

events.item: Hell-Kissed Celestial Skill Potion of the Prince of Woe
events.cause: Carl drinks it after Mordecai's analysis; it briefly shows him a vision of Sheol.
events.evidence: Carl pops cork and drinks talking potion
system message after drinking skill potion

events.text: Gloom Wraith Phase details: non-corporeal bursts of speed (lunge attack) that let Carl and equipped items pass through unenchanted weapons, flesh, and obstacles, scrambling unprotected biological targets; effectively combines Path of Destruction and Rush. Each burst halves his mana (minimum 2 mana to use). At level 5 a designated item can stay corporeal; at level 10 he can stay non-corporeal up to five seconds with a thirty-second cooldown; at level 15 the cooldown drops to six seconds. A demon skull button is added to his interface.
events.evidence: skill description after Gloom Wraith Phase gained

events.text: Carl bonds with a level-1 male Tummy Acher pet (formerly Ren's pet Garret) after feeding it chowder; a new Bonded Pets tab appears in his interface, with extra options from the guild's pet stable. The pet's stats are buffed 125% from five prior deaths and it carries five weaknesses.
events.evidence: system message after meatball eats Carl's chowder

events.text: Carl names his bonded Tummy Acher pet Sir Rendlegore, honoring Ren.
events.evidence: naming box, Louis suggests Sir Rendlegore

events.cause: Katia applies the tonic to regrow Carl's eyebrows, eyelashes, and hair; a mishap leaves his hair waist-length for about two weeks.
