events.description: A blue conical wizard hat with a glittering star-and-planet pattern that sparkles, taken from the level 55 Skank Skunk Battle Wizard.
events.evidence: Donut says she got the hat, now putrid

events.text: Mordecai applies a modified Glory Bound gel (mixed with Tough Love and Removal Order potions) to Carl so he can enter Shi Maria's memories and attempt to trap her there.
events.evidence: field hospital, green gel rubbed on Carl

events.effects: Gently lulls the drinker to sleep, briefly easing worries and bad dreams.
events.description: A sleep-aid potion that soothes anxieties just long enough for rest to come.
events.evidence: Imani hands Carl and Katia sleep potions

events.cause: Drunk to fall asleep before the Glory Bound treatment takes hold.
events.evidence: Carl drinks the potion, warm tingling

events.text: As Carl falls unconscious he receives a warlord notification: the Lemig Sortion offers free passage to their headquarters to treat for peace.
events.evidence: warlord notification interrupts Katia at chapter end

events.text: On waking, Carl receives the system message that the Scavenger's Daughter has been fed and her wrath can be unleashed.
events.evidence: system message as Carl wakes in castle

events.text: Quest failed: The Bedlam Bride (the quest to bring Shi Maria to Larracos).
events.evidence: Quest Failed notification after waking

events.text: The Removal Order treatment works: Shi Maria is trapped like an egg inside Carl's chest-eye tattoo. Carl now controls the eye, can see through it, and can deliberately release her as a last resort.
events.evidence: Hapanzi traps spider; Carl controls tattoo eye

events.text: Containment mechanic clarified: Carl can summon the trapped Shi Maria exactly once; if he does, she chooses either to take over his body or expel herself back into her own form. She is effectively another one-use weapon in his arsenal.
events.evidence: replay of Hapanzi's explanation during adjudication

events.description: A cheap Earth-brand bag of cat food with a Pet Angels charity sticker, inexplicably sitting in Queen Imogen's kitchen; Carl grabs it as he escapes.
events.evidence: Carl grabs cat food bag while dodging Houston

events.cause: Dropped as a bomb at Architect Houston while escaping; no kill message received.
events.evidence: Carl drops bomb before teleporting out

events.cause: Bag dropped on the rough teleport landing, spilling across the field hospital floor.
events.evidence: cat food spills after teleport landing

events.cause: Drunk to heal a gunshot wound through his shoulder from Architect Houston's rifle.
events.evidence: Carl slams healing potion after landing

events.text: Carl gains a new interface tab: the Containment Interface, installed after negotiating an exchange involving Paulie's plastic tub. It monitors the device that installed the AI into the primal engine, including a Fuse page (2 of 5 benchmarks show Conditions Met) with an Override Conditions And Activate Fuse button.
events.evidence: system message: Containment Interface tab available

events.text: Carl presses the Disconnect Containment option, rendering the Containment Unit inert and permanently disabling the fuse/failsafe; the interface blanks except for the solar-system map, where the dot over Earth begins to grow.
events.evidence: Carl presses Disconnect Containment at gas station

events.text: Faction Wars Phase One rules display: 180 hours remaining, no offensive magic, no teleport-based magic or skills, entry to enemy territory only via designated corridors (scouts exempt). 9 of 10 teams remain; the Princess Posse holds Spot 3; the Democratic Sortion is eliminated.
events.evidence: battle rules screen at chapter start

events.text: Warlord system message: their Throne has been locked in place and all faction throne-room locations are now visible on the map.
events.evidence: throne locked message among warlord messages

events.text: Carl and Donut share a 15-member Personal Guard platoon (chosen by Donut); assigned guards get a 25% constitution bonus while physically near either warlord.
events.evidence: guard platoon explained as Donut arrives

events.text: Carl gains the Soul Poisoning debuff from keeping his jacket's glowing patch powered too long; the system prompts him to activate the Daughter's Kiss skill to disperse it.
events.evidence: debuff notification before leaving for southern front

events.cause: Thrown like a grenade at charging Rot Sticker Behemoths in front of the trench.
events.evidence: dynamite tossed over trench edge at pets

events.text: The dynamite blast earns Carl several experience notifications and a new achievement; the achievement's name and reward are not shown.
events.evidence: notifications after dynamite explosion

events.text: Carl's Daughter's Kiss is confirmed to be a self-buff skill, so it remains usable despite the Phase One ban on offensive magic; he prepares it but cancels the activation without using it.
events.evidence: Carl readies then closes Daughter's Kiss

events.text: System buff 'Titivated' applied while mages stack protection spells in the trench: projectiles do less damage.
events.evidence: buff message while waiting in trench before assault

events.text: Imani casts Total Ear Protection on Donut (a sound-blocking bubble like a privacy shell) ahead of the Dazzler barrage; she also casts it on Mongo.
events.evidence: Imani's spell bubble around Donut's head

events.cause: Carl drinks it as the squad goes over the top of the trench
events.evidence: Carl downs potion during the charge

events.cause: Donut drinks hers at the same moment as Carl
events.evidence: Donut drinks potion beside Carl

events.cause: Carl smashes a second potion against Mongo's backside to turn the dinosaur invisible
events.evidence: potion smashed on Mongo before Donut mounts

events.text: Enemy magic diffusion spell triggers a 'Deshrouded' system message: all temporary war protection spells, invisibility, illusions, and ear protection are stripped mid-charge.
events.evidence: fireworks diffusion spell over No Man's Land

events.cause: thrown at the enemy heavy pulse gun emplacement, silencing it
events.evidence: tossed at heavy gun from behind a rock

events.cause: tossed in front of the squad toward the heavy gun for concealment
events.evidence: smoke tossed after gun goes quiet

events.text: System message: Carl's Soul Poisoning is worsening and he will now suffer from a debuff called Post Coital Blues.
events.evidence: soul poisoning warning during the charge

events.cause: read in the crater to dig a channel enjoining the enemy trench, exploiting craft magic to bypass protections
events.evidence: scroll read in crater before trench breach

events.cause: combat during the trench assault (kill made with the Daughter's Kiss stomp); new level number not stated
events.evidence: notification wall after waking in the trench

events.text: Soul poisoning debuff 'Post Nut Clarity' knocked Carl unconscious for about 25 seconds the moment he used his Daughter's Kiss attack.
events.evidence: Imani explains blackout after the stomp

events.effects: explosive trap taken intact after being disarmed
events.description: A landmine guarding the Prism castle door that Carl disables and pockets.
events.evidence: disabling traps at Prism castle door

events.effects: traps that raise an alert when triggered
events.description: Three alarm traps stolen from the Prism castle entrance.
events.evidence: steals three alarm traps at doorway

events.effects: would normally summon four guards to its location; currently inert due to the teleportation ban
events.description: A summoning trap taken from the Prism castle door.
events.evidence: final trap taken at castle door

events.cause: deployed inside the Prism castle to scout and attack occupants (quantity not stated)
events.evidence: automatons dropped and scattered inside castle
ring donned before calling out the Empress

events.text: Entering the Prism throne room: it can be captured (defeating the Prism) by occupying it for six hours, but the timer is paused while multiple factions occupy the room.
events.evidence: throne room entry system messages

events.cause: tossed into the throne room for cover before entering
events.evidence: smoke rolled in ahead of entry

events.cause: several metal spheres hurled one after another at D'Nadia to spark and drain her shields; recovery not stated
events.evidence: repeated sphere throws at the Empress

events.cause: cast on D'Nadia to bring her to full health so the Ring of Divine Suffering could mark her
events.evidence: heal cast after knife knocked away

events.text: Carl successfully marks Warlord Empress D'Nadia with the Ring of Divine Suffering; the system reports her highest stat is Intelligence, and the mark fully forms after 30 seconds.
events.evidence: mark messages after healing the Empress

events.cause: Ring of Divine Suffering mark payout on D'Nadia's death (her highest stat was Intelligence); resulting total not shown
events.evidence: five points flash into interface after her death

events.name: Gavrilo Princip!
events.description: Awarded for killing Empress D'Nadia, the last living heir of a powerful trading dynasty, an assassination with galaxy-wide financial and legal fallout; the announcer goads Carl to kill more system leaders for bigger chaos.
events.evidence: achievement popup after D'Nadia dies

events.description: Loot box awarded by the Gavrilo Princip achievement; not opened this chapter.
events.evidence: reward line of the achievement

events.text: System messages: kill credit for Warlord D'Nadia goes to pet Mongo; the Prism is defeated and all Prism assets (land, castle, mercenaries) are awarded to the Princess Posse, though the non-adjoining throne room is vulnerable to recapture.
events.evidence: system messages after the kill
Carl pulls the ring off before the mercenaries

events.description: Donut loots the dead Empress (specific items not listed) and then takes the corpse itself into her inventory.
events.evidence: Donut looting the body mid-scene

events.text: System notification: due to recent upgrades, Carl's total base stats now make him the strongest party member, so he is designated the new Party Leader of the Royal Court of Princess Donut, and a new interface tab unlocks. Donut is displeased but they keep the party name.
events.evidence: party leader notification re-read for Donut

events.text: Donut says her Constitution still has several more points to tick up before hitting the cap because of her cloak; Carl counters that the cloak's Constitution boost is an enhancement, not base stats (no numbers given).
events.evidence: argument about party leadership outside throne room

events.cause: rapidly placed around the captured Prism castle before abandoning it; a freeze trap set in front of the throne room door
events.evidence: trap placement while planning withdrawal

events.cause: left for Lockjaw, the Madness mercenary ordered to sit in the throne room for six hours
events.evidence: provisions dropped for Lockjaw in throne room

events.text: Warlord warning: the newly owned Prism throne room is occupied by Madness enemy forces; if not reclaimed within six hours the castle and its land will be ceded to the Dream. Carl deliberately leaves it, hoping to sow discord among enemy leaders.
events.evidence: warning and countdown as pets leave throne room

events.cause: planted in front of the saferoom shack door south of the former Prism castle before returning to base through it
events.evidence: trap left at bait-shack-style saferoom entrance

events.text: Donut says she can only stay in the saferoom for about half an hour under the reactivated saferoom restrictions.
events.evidence: Donut protests before showers at base

events.text: The blood bar saferoom restriction (from the end of the sixth floor) reactivates now that fighting has begun: it drains while inside a saferoom and refills only by killing. Carl's bar is full, giving him about eight hours.
events.evidence: blood bar activates on entering personal space

events.effects: spellbook Donut has not yet read
events.description: A spellbook from Donut's Legendary 1914 Box; the book itself bleeds and cries, which Donut finds disgusting.
events.evidence: Donut's message about her 1914 Box

events.text: Carl has accumulated several unreviewed achievements, and another unnamed achievement pops when he stomps the Valtay brain worm (Goldblum); he waves it away without reading it.
events.evidence: achievement flash after killing the worm

events.text: Zone-wide Extreme Focus buff active, boosting dexterity and multitasking for everyone in the castle zone; Imani confirms it is active on Carl despite him not feeling its chatty side effect.
events.evidence: Imani explains Extreme Focus buff during castle defense

events.effects: Dead mercenary body with a sparking tech-based invisibility cloak still attached.
events.description: The body of an invisible-cloaked Dreadnaught mercenary killed on the barracks roof, pulled into Carl's inventory.
events.evidence: Carl loots dead dreadnaught on barracks roof

events.effects: Strange faceless mask worn by the Madness Monkeywrencher disguised mercenaries.
events.description: A faceless disguise mask taken from the dead Dreadnaught mercenary on the roof.
events.evidence: Carl takes faceless mask from dreadnaught body

events.text: System message announces the Scavenger's Daughter has been fed after Carl beats a mercenary to death bare-handed; Carl later activates the Daughter's Kiss skill to leap onto a fleeing mage.
events.evidence: system message after punching J IronFjord to death
Donut examines orc king via new crown-added Targeting skill

events.text: Donut spends the party's pooled gold on base upgrades: a standalone hangar access point (5 million gold), a new anti-air gun (5 million gold), a third mobile anti-air gun, and banners, leaving them nearly out of money.
events.evidence: guildhall bopca base-upgrade purchases before siege

events.effects: Captured war-machine inventory; captured items do not count against the inventory limit.
events.description: Two enemy tanks called Weepers, formerly owned by the Operatic Collective, captured by Florin's forces and added to the warlord's inventory. High-tech tanks resembling WWI Mark V's with a top turret and anti-air tube.
events.evidence: warlord update after Florin captures Operatic armor

events.item: The Cloak of the Benevolent Champion
events.cause: The cloak raises her lowest base stat to the spoofed 193 over 66 hours of wear; at ch. 34 she says it has 'several more spots to tick up', so the ramp completes shortly after.
events.evidence: My Constitution still has several more spots to tick up before it hits the top because of my cloak

events.description: The severed head of King Rust of the Skull Empire, taken as battlefield loot after Rosetta used it for a retina scan.
events.evidence: Rosetta tosses head back, tells Carl to inventory it

events.description: The headless body of King Rust, still wearing his magical gear, pulled into Carl's inventory during the retreat.
events.evidence: Carl pulls the king's corpse into inventory amid grenades

events.text: System message: the team's non-connected territory 'Former Prism castle' has been ceded to the Dream; alliance update shows the Bone Clan and the Dream leaving the Bloc while the Princess Posse remains allied with Team Retribution.
events.evidence: territory-lost and alliance system messages after return

events.text: Donut says she read the disturbing spellbook (the one that was bleeding and crying), learning an undisclosed spell; Mordecai told her to keep its effect secret and she is nervous about casting it.
events.evidence: Donut mentions reading the bleeding, crying spellbook

events.effects: A second copy of the ring Carl already owns; noted as looted from King Rust's body.
events.description: King Rust's Ring of Divine Suffering, obtained from the corpse in Carl's inventory.
events.evidence: Carl reflects he now has a second Ring

events.description: A magical necklace taken from King Rust's body, along with several other magical items to be distributed later.
events.evidence: Carl notes he has the damn necklace too

events.item: The Tiara of a Thousand Lights

set.description: Sponsor tiara whose hundred absorption jewels soak up buffs from charge pillows; by the faction wars it has absorbed dozens, including +10 Constitution and a Dodge skill upgrade.

events.cause: Katia drains buff hats onto the tiara's absorption jewels during the faction wars.
events.evidence: The tiara had just gained another 10 to Constitution, a plus two enhancement to Donut's Dodge skill

events.item: Spellbook of Run, Little Günter, Run
events.effects: Special Edition spellbook: teaches the spell at level 5 and it trains up faster than usual. The book is animate, with centipede legs, and leaks black liquid while begging not to be read.
events.description: A living, whimpering spellbook from Carl's 1914 loot box that teaches the summoning spell Run, Little Günter, Run.
events.evidence: crying spellbook from the 1914 box on kitchen counter

events.item: Spellbook of Run, Little Günter, Run
events.cause: Carl applied the spellbook from his inventory to learn the spell.
events.evidence: Carl clicks the book and applies it

events.effects: Summons Little Günter (1 HP) in front of the caster; attacking him stores the attack, then he runs toward a designated target and the stored attack triggers on arrival or timeout. Cost variable, minimum 20 mana; duration 20 seconds plus 1 second per extra mana point; 10-hour cooldown; must be activated within 30 seconds. Higher levels reduce cooldown and mana cost and speed him up; at level 10 he can split into two, at 15 into five. Once activated his constitution matches the caster's, and killing him early triggers the stored attack immediately.
events.evidence: spell description shown after applying Special Edition spellbook

events.text: Katia charges Donut's tiara from drained buff hats: three more absorption jewels activate (23 of 99 total), adding +10 Constitution, a +2 enhancement to Donut's Dodge skill (stated to bring it to 15), and a +1% Intelligence buff.
events.evidence: Katia places tiara on charging pillows, gems sparkle

events.text: Katia tattoos Carl's right forearm (a chain shaped like a dragon) using ink made from Li Na's freely-given blood potion, granting Carl her level-13 skill (name not stated in this chapter) for roughly the rest of the floor. Li Na temporarily lost five levels in the skill but keeps it; the tattoo itself is permanent.
events.evidence: blood-ink tattoo session before warlord council teleport

events.text: The system rated Carl's new skill tattoo on his right forearm as expert quality, which Edgar says is the second-highest rating possible.
events.evidence: Carl tells Donut about the tattoo's system rating

events.text: Carl spends one of the Princess Posse's two remaining Emergency Action Items to close all communication loopholes circumventing Team Retribution's earlier feed-blackout action item; the request is accepted.
events.evidence: Emergency Action Item accepted at warlord council

events.effects: Potion balls prepared by Mordecai for the assault; Carl is warned not to use them on more than two members of the same religion.
events.description: A batch of black potion balls Mordecai laid out on the saferoom table; Carl gathers them into his inventory, bringing his total to hundreds.
events.evidence: saferoom scene, Carl collects Mordecai's potion balls

events.text: Carl receives a system notification that Crawler Imani has used his Tripper spell (lent to her via a blood tattoo), causing him a jolt of searing chest pain.
events.evidence: notification when Imani triggers borrowed Tripper spell

events.cause: The spell had raised on its own to level 7; Carl discovered this when removing his bandana, Drakea's Enchanted Kerchief of Disorder, which boosts the item-based version to level 15.
events.evidence: flashback explaining bandana and innate Tripper levels

events.cause: Lost six levels (from 7) when Katia drew his blood to transfer the spell to Imani as a tattoo; the spell will heal back over four days. The bandana still raises it to 15 while worn.
events.evidence: blood tattoo transfer of Tripper to Imani

events.text: Carl recruits Prepotente into his army at the rank of Colonel, allowing them to work together under Faction Wars rules; Carl stows the unused potion of Conscription back into his inventory.
events.evidence: Prepotente lands and demands to be recruited

events.text: Donut receives a notification that one of the four Blood Sultanate line-of-succession targets (the head of the castle guard) has been killed, leaving three targets remaining.
events.evidence: Donut messages that she got the kill notification

events.text: Warlord message: a member of Carl's army occupies the throne room of the Blood Sultanate stronghold; holding it for six hours will capture the castle and defeat the Blood Sultanate.
events.evidence: warlord message at chapter opening

events.cause: Carl hands one of Mordecai's yellow aura potions to Prepotente before entering the temple of the Dagda.
events.evidence: Carl gives Prepotente an aura potion outside temple

events.cause: Carl drinks the potion via menu and gains the Closed Off effect: immune to aura Blessings and Dreads for one hour (does not cancel existing effects or his own auras).
events.evidence: Carl drinks aura potion, one-hour timer appears

events.text: New quest from Emberus: Kill the Blasphemer. Carl must kill the Hellik-worshiping ram Mork for insulting Emberus; reward is an additional Emberus Boon, better the more entertaining the kill.
events.evidence: Emberus quest after Mork insults him
Carl compares his level to the mantaur guards

events.cause: Thrown at Mork the ram; the potion temporarily removes the target from his god's grace, blocking god-granted spells and temple entry.
events.evidence: black potion hurled at Mork fleeing the vestibule

events.text: Quest complete: Kill the Blasphemer. Mork dies from Li Na's amplified dread, and Emberus rewards Carl with a Celestial Boon; the tattoos on the backs of Carl's hands burn as it is granted.
events.evidence: quest completion after Mork's death

events.cause: Celestial Boon from Emberus; for the remainder of the floor, any punch attack adds Heathen Scald skill damage.
events.evidence: boon notification after quest completion

events.text: Carl confirms he holds a spell lent to him by Li Na via blood tattoo and prepares to cast it, warning her she will feel pain when he does (as he did when Imani used his Tripper).
events.evidence: Carl messages Li Na about casting her tattoo spell

events.text: On entering the Temple of Khepri, Carl is denied a seat at the feast, flagged as trespassing in a temple, and marked for death by Khepri, who is personally present.
events.evidence: warnings upon entering Temple of Khepri

events.name: Charge of the Light Brigade
events.description: Awarded for walking into an extraordinarily bad trap — the AI says this goes beyond ordinary traps and predicts a gloriously messy outcome.
events.reward: None granted; the AI says it will give a good reward only if Carl survives without escaping via a cop-out.
events.evidence: achievement popup after entering the temple

events.text: In the presence of the deity Khepri, Carl's Scavenger's Daughter opens her eyes and fills with power; a yellow glow covers Carl.
events.evidence: deity-presence notification, Scavenger's Daughter empowers

events.text: Temporary effect from Khepri: Carl is immortal for the next 90 seconds — a genuine standard effect of being in this deity's presence, per the AI.
events.evidence: temporary immortality effect notification

events.text: Carl has an active immortality effect; its countdown will expire 27 seconds after Vinata's, which is frozen at 63 seconds.
events.evidence: observing Vinata's frozen immortal countdown in temple

events.cause: Detonated the thermos-sized thermobaric bomb inside Khepri's temple to break the naga attack.
events.evidence: bomb dropped as naga guards charge

events.cause: Used from inventory to heal after being nearly obliterated by his own blast.
events.evidence: clicked potion while reforming after explosion

events.text: A flood of unspecified achievements and level notifications scrolls past after the blast; specifics are not shown.
events.evidence: notifications flood vision after temple explosion

events.text: Gods pouring into the realm trigger Carl's Scavenger's Daughter patch, giving him multiple buffs and debuffs: a Never-Ending Mana buff, suction cups on his fingertips, and debuffs including Shit-Faced (drunk) and Stiff Legs (cannot bend knees).
events.evidence: buff and debuff list after gods arrive

events.text: System message on Khepri's banishment: the Scavenger's Daughter closes her eyes to Khepri, but Carl's god-granted boon is timed and will expire on its own.
events.evidence: notification when Khepri leaves the realm

events.cause: Used a second healing potion while Vinata crushed and pounded him.
events.evidence: clicked another potion during Vinata's assault

events.text: Carl is marked as a friend of the church by Hellik; Hellik's worshipers will no longer be automatically hostile toward him.
events.evidence: system message after Hellik's speech

events.text: System message: the Blood Sultanate is defeated. Its assets are split evenly between Warlord Princess Donut and General Katia Grim as surviving blood relatives of the Sepsis Whore, and per prior arrangement all assets are forfeited to the Princess Posse.
events.evidence: system messages after Vinata's death

events.description: The body of the slain naga princess Vinata, the Sepsis Whore, taken into inventory after the fight.
events.evidence: Carl loots Vinata's body in wrecked lobby

events.cause: Hurled at the ogre Basilica Guard to strip his Untouchable status.
events.evidence: potion splatters on ogre's chest

events.cause: Dropped two alarm traps that play Megadeth's 'Holy Wars... The Punishment Due' to force mantaurs into berserk status.

events.cause: Two more potions delivered by Profane Iron chains, smashing into two mantaurs.
events.evidence: chains pluck potions and strike mantaurs

events.text: Carl uses Profane Iron for the first time, the tattoo skill previously granted by Li Na: a once-a-day defensive skill that erupts dozens of chains from his flesh for about thirty seconds, of which he can truly control only two. Activating it costs him a small amount of health.
events.evidence: activating Li Na's tattoo skill against mantaurs

events.cause: Thrown at a second ogre Basilica Guard, who then fled.
events.evidence: second ogre hit with same potion type

events.text: System message: discovered two hidden guilds, the Map-Making Guild and the Calligrapher's Guild.
events.evidence: hidden door found near line of guilds

events.cause: Skill added by draining a hat into her Tiara of a Thousand Lights.
events.evidence: Donut glares up at tower builders in fading light

events.text: Donut's Tiara of a Thousand Lights currently holds 35 of 99 possible skills and upgrades, with two more to be added before the Phase Three timer expires. The source hat for Perfect Night Vision was drained (destroyed) in the process.
events.evidence: tiara upgrade count during tower construction

events.text: Interview reveals Carl holds the number one spot on the top ten crawler list; Li Na, newly level 84, is now number two just behind him. Five Celestial Who-Let-The-Gods-Out Boxes were awarded to Li Jun, Zhang, Carl, Prepotente, and Katia for the Club Vanquisher attack.
events.evidence: Kevin's interview recap of celestial box awards

events.item: The Enchanted Spiked Knee Pads the Munificent Goddess Kina
events.effects: Divine item: +25% melee damage reflect, Breathe Underwater benefit, Level 15 Swim, Level 10 Iron Stomach (eat magic items to raise intelligence), Level 10 Eater of the Dead (eat corpses to randomly raise constitution/strength/dexterity); grants extra varying benefits near divine entities.
events.description: Knee pads made from the eyelashes of the sea goddess Kina, awarded to Katia from her celestial box. Narration says Katia has already unequipped and given them away, and the commentary makes clear they were intended for Carl to replace his similar existing knee pads, so Carl is the likely recipient.
events.evidence: interview segment on Katia's celestial award

events.item: The Enchanted Hairbrush of the Beefmaster
events.effects: Usable once per day on one male with hair 25 cm or longer; brushing for five minutes grants 30 hours of the Shining Charisma benefit (party members gain 20% of Carl's charisma), the Speedster benefit (very fast short-distance movement, combinable with his Gloom Wraith Phase), +25% constitution, and Level 10 Entourage (illusion that mirrors party members). The brush screams like a cheering crowd when used.
events.description: A gold, jewel-handled magical hairbrush from Carl's celestial box earned in the Club Vanquisher fight — his third celestial item after the Scavenger's Daughter patch and the potion that gave him Gloom Wraith Phase. Its constitution boost makes it too valuable to give up, forcing Carl to keep his hair long.
events.evidence: hairbrush description shown during interview

events.cause: Implied current value: Shining Charisma grants party members 20% of Carl's charisma, and the text states Donut would be boosted by six points (20% of 30).
events.evidence: Shining Charisma benefit math for Donut

events.name: The Shining Charisma Benefit
events.description: Everyone in his party gains an extra 20% of his charisma.
events.evidence: Shining Charisma gives the party 20% of his charisma

events.name: The Speedster Benefit
events.description: Blink-fast movement over short distances; combines with his Gloom Wraith Phase.
events.evidence: Speedster allows very fast short-distance movement

events.text: Carl received 32 loot boxes and over fifty achievements after the Club Vanquisher raid.

events.reward: Permanently banned from Club Vanquisher and from all temples except his own religion (Emberus) and temples where he is named friend of the church (Apito, Eileithyia, Eris, Grull, Hellik, Issitoq, Nekhebit, Theia, Yemaya)
events.evidence: first achievement in scratchpad recap

skill potion from platinum box

events.cause: Gave his extra ring to Li Na at her request, urged by Donut

events.name: Holistic!

events.text: Full system description of Donut's War Crime spell is shown: Atrocity-type, line-of-sight eye beam in a narrow cone, cost 1 mana. It cannot be trained; it starts at level 5 and rises by 5 each cast. It can only be cast once per floor, only on Scolopendra levels (3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18), a maximum of 3 times total, and has been cast 0 times so far. At level 5 it creatively combines two chosen spells (three at level 10, four at 15); chosen spells must be at least level 8. Casting drops the caster's health to 1% and locks the chosen spells for the rest of the floor, but afterward they can be chained together for the cost of one. Victims cannot die directly from the spell no matter what happens to them.
events.evidence: Carl reviews the spell description from his scratch pad

events.text: Carl mentions he has dozens of unopened loot boxes, achievements to review, and a fan box waiting from the end of the ninth floor.
events.evidence: Carl's inner monologue while driving heat one

events.cause: Donut states her current level in dialogue: 'As the number one crawler at level 135'.
events.evidence: Donut boasts before killing the manatee

events.text: Donut one-shots Miss Talulah, a level 90 Screeching Death Manatee (special guest creature), with a pair of magic missiles. A notification lets her choose the fate of her kill: keep it dead for regular experience, or let the audience vote. She picks the audience vote; results pending.
events.evidence: special guest creature kill-fate notification

events.cause: Cast on a bucket and a kitchen bowl to make water sources for putting out the bugbears' burning minivan.
events.evidence: Carl casts scrolls at burning minivan

events.cause: The water-gushing bowl was dropped by a bugbear, rocketed away, and fell into the dark cave water.
events.evidence: bowl spins off into cave water

events.description: A tie-dyed headband discarded in disgust by Jasha the bugbear; Donut quietly takes it into her inventory.
events.evidence: headband disappears into Donut's inventory

events.effects: Each thorn can grow into a full-sized Avernus Creeper plant, one of the most invasive species in the universe, though they will not sprout on this floor.
events.description: Multiple thorns plucked from Rend's skin after Team Sparkles' plant mount ran him over; Mordecai asks Carl to save them all and keep them in inventory until reaching the safe room. Exact quantity not stated.
events.evidence: Carl plucks thorns from Rend after tumbleweed passes

events.text: Follow-up notification to Donut's manatee kill-fate vote: the audience voted for Otto the slime (of Otto Banking Unit 3) to be the manatee 'stand-in' in the arena. Meaning unknown to the party.
events.evidence: strange notification while driving through cave

events.text: Heat One results: The Royal Court of Princess Donut finishes seventh of eight surviving teams (they towed Team Free Love's wrecked minivan across the line for eighth; The Trauco Triplets were eliminated). Placement determines upgrade choices between heats.
events.evidence: Heat One results screen at finish line

events.text: Using their post-heat-one upgrade pick, the party selects the Retractable Legs (spider legs) upgrade for the food truck on Hedy's recommendation: on-demand deployable legs that can climb walls and cross obstacles, faster than the tire option but not meant to run constantly.
events.evidence: Carl tells Hedy to pick the spider legs

events.text: Safe room warning: a Blood Bar is now in effect. It starts full, allows a maximum of 10 hours in the safe room, and can be refilled by killing things.
events.evidence: system warning on entering safe room

events.description: Mailbox delivery includes another hat for Donut plus hats for the pets Mongo and Rend, apparently the accessories from Donut's Assassin of Sekhmet class. Names and stats not shown.
events.evidence: Donut spots hats in safe room mailbox

events.effects: Special edition spell book that trains its spell faster than normal.
events.description: A living, slime-oozing spell book that arrives via the safe room mailbox and scurries around the room; Donut declines it, so Carl takes it.
events.evidence: spell book escapes mailbox, cleaner bot catches it
Carl applies the spell book at chapter end

events.cause: Spell book consumed when Carl applied it to learn the spell.
events.evidence: Carl applies the spell book at chapter end
Donut describes spells from her new class

events.text: Donut's Assassin of Sekhmet class also buffs her create-tree and summon-vermin spells from the Oak Fell titles, and grants her first aura, Healer's Grasp, which heals anyone near her.
events.evidence: Donut lists her new class goodies

events.text: Donut states she has two unopened celestial boxes plus new achievements still to review.
events.evidence: Donut insists on getting things moving

events.effects: Precognition Benefit: warns wearer if anyone nearby is preparing an offensive or binding spell or readying a skill. Only wearable by a female of a Feline-class species; enemies of Sekhmet become automatically hostile to the wearer.
events.description: Egyptian-style beaded headdress worn by the court of the goddess Sekhmet, meant to warn off those who would harm the bearer. One of Donut's class-accessory hats.
events.evidence: Donut shows off her class accessory headdress
headdress appears atop her head
tiara disappears when veil is donned

events.effects: Pet-only accessory. Eternal Protector of Rage: pet becomes invulnerable for 60 seconds (once per day) and gains 2x strength when within three meters of an owner whose health is below 10%. Bringer of Death: pet bites have a 75% chance to inflict the Cruel Sepsis debuff.
events.description: A pharaoh-style striped fabric nemes hat intended for Donut's pet Mongo; one of the class-accessory hats.
events.evidence: pet hat examined on the table

events.effects: Pet-only accessory. Eternal Protector of Health: pet becomes invulnerable for 60 seconds (once per day) and gains the Heal Party Member skill when within three meters of an owner whose health is below 10%. Bringer of Resurrection: fatal bites have a 75% chance to cast a level 10 Raise Bootlicker on the corpse.
events.description: A red patterned headband intended for the pet Rend; one of the class-accessory hats.
events.evidence: second pet accessory examined

events.cause: Donut mentions receiving leveling achievements, including one for reaching level 100 (no box, accomplishment 'reward enough').
events.evidence: Donut recaps her achievements

events.text: Donut's box haul: two celestial boxes earned from her War Crime spell (a celestial slap-chop box and a celestial godslayer box), a legendary Lorena Bobbitt box involving Meatus, a legendary 'default winner' box for winning Faction Wars, and about forty boxes total, mostly Bronze Adventurer Boxes. The bulk contents were cheap clothes and assorted healing scrolls.
events.evidence: Donut recaps boxes before opening them

events.effects: Summoning scroll: the summoned gremlin can repair a vehicle that breaks down mid-race; this variety can also fix biological mounts.
events.description: Heavy green metal-like summoning scrolls; Donut receives roughly twenty of them (exact count not stated) across many boxes.
events.evidence: green scrolls stack up from bronze boxes

events.description: The legendary default winner box contains a spell book that quickly disappears into Donut's inventory; it is identified in the next chapter.
events.evidence: default winner box yields a spell book

events.effects: Enchanted crossbow bolt that does no physical damage but anchors the struck body part in place for (2 seconds x the shooter's Crossbow skill level), with messy physics if the target is moving.
events.description: A quiver holding about ten glittering crossbow bolts (count approximate) from the legendary Lorena Bobbitt box.
events.evidence: 7-Eleven-wrapper box reveals sparkly bolts

events.effects: Anchoring crossbow bolt (see Donut's entry).
events.description: Carl snatches one of Donut's Danforth bolts to examine before the rest disappear into her inventory.
events.evidence: Carl grabs one bolt to examine

events.effects: Matching item to Donut's champion cloak (abilities revealed next chapter).
events.description: A plain silver fine chain mail crupper from the celestial slap-chop box; Mordecai identifies it as a match to the Cloak of the Benevolent Champion.
events.evidence: celestial slap-chop box opens as origami swan

events.text: The celestial godslayer box leaves no physical reward: smoke reenacts Meatus's death, then the paw Donut used for the War Crime spell begins to glow painfully and her extended claws slice the kitchen table in half. Mordecai says it will be a good thing (boon detailed next chapter).
events.evidence: godslayer box smoke, glowing paw, split table

events.text: Celestial boon (permanent, not a spell or skill) from the godslayer box: the four main claws of Donut's right front paw are dipped in radiant light. They can cut through anything in the mortal realm, can slice ghosts, and glow brilliant blue; the paw faintly glows even when claws are retracted.
events.evidence: Mordecai explains the glowing-claw boon
Donut checks Claw skill after boon; past normal 15 cap

events.effects: Translucent red caps that mute the claws' glow (appearing purple); can be removed and re-armed at will via inventory, protecting against accidental cuts.
events.description: Rosetta buys four claw caps from the safe room shop interface so Donut's boon claws don't destroy things by accident.
events.evidence: Rosetta buys caps at shop interface
caps go to inventory then appear on her claws

events.text: The default winner box's spell book is identified as Ice Slick, a special edition that starts at level 5 and trains faster. The spell creates a one-inch-thick ice sheet over a wide area that scales with intelligence and spell level. (Not shown being learned in this chapter.)
events.evidence: Carl examines the freezing-cold spell book

events.item: Enchanted Fae-Scale Quadruped Crupper of the Fleet
events.evidence: Donut removes old scale-mail crupper (+2 dexterity, worn since floor one)

events.item: The Crupper of the Benevolent Champion
events.evidence: new chain mail crupper appears on her backside

events.description: Awarded for wearing a matched set. If Donut finds the third piece of the set, a fourth piece will be given as a reward.
events.evidence: Donut announces matched-set achievement

events.text: The Crupper of the Benevolent Champion is a unique item, part of a matched set with the Cloak of the Benevolent Champion (two more pieces exist; finding the third awards the fourth in a box). Its abilities are identical to the Cloak's, plus set modifiers: the stat raised to match her highest base stat (Constitution) is buffed an additional 20%, Find Weakened rises to level 10, and one spell the Cloak raised to 15 may be raised to 16. More boons unlock with all four pieces. The description includes a long AI rant about Donut's brutal Faction Wars kill and heroism.
events.evidence: full crupper item description
crupper matched-set bonus

events.text: Donut states the spells previously raised to level 15 by her Cloak are Wall of Fire, Heal Critter, Bad Attitude, and Puddle Jumper; her Brain Trust skill already makes them all activate as if level 16.
events.evidence: Donut lists cloak-raised spells while choosing
Donut picks Puddle Jumper for the set bonus; now officially 16

events.name: Bombs Away!
events.description: Earned for dumping a huge quantity of bombs from his own inventory while airborne, done unaimed while fleeing Larracos to protect Donut from Lucia's attack; the AI gloats that his weakness is now confirmed.
events.evidence: first notable achievement reviewed

events.text: Carl received numerous additional achievements yielding multiple gold and silver boxes for helping snipe automated towers from Donut's Nest during Faction Wars.
events.evidence: recap of tower-sniping achievements

events.name: Default Winner!
events.description: Earned for winning Faction Wars only because the rival warlords fled the battlefield and were disqualified; the AI compares him to athletes who won via others' disqualification and hints something bigger is being set up.
events.evidence: second notable achievement reviewed

events.name: Wastrel
events.description: The AI berates Carl for wasting one of the game's most powerful items by feeding the Ring of Divine Suffering to Donut.
events.reward: None (the reward line is an insult)
events.evidence: achievement about the eaten ring

events.name: Come to Daddy
events.description: Earned for rejecting an offered exit deal; the AI reacts with unsettling gratitude that Carl chose to stay.
events.evidence: achievement for refusing the exit deal

events.effects: Summoning scroll for a gremlin that repairs vehicles (and biological mounts) mid-race.
events.description: Several Emergency Gremlin scrolls among Carl's routine box contents (exact count not stated).
events.evidence: usual stuff from opened boxes

events.description: An unenchanted can of white spray paint from the Bronze Vanessa Williams Box.
events.evidence: Vanessa Williams box contents

events.effects: Throwable potion that prevents smaller bombs from detonating if tossed onto them in time.
events.description: Five Bomb Blanket potions from the Bronze Daddy's Little Secret Box; Mordecai had mentioned them before.
events.evidence: Daddy's Little Secret box contents

events.description: Restock of dynamite from the boxes (quantity not stated).
events.evidence: bomb restock from boxes

events.description: Restock of hob-lobbers from the boxes (quantity not stated).
events.evidence: bomb restock from boxes

events.description: Restock of surefires from the boxes (quantity not stated).
events.evidence: bomb restock from boxes

events.effects: Upgraded version of the hob-lobber; a thrown explosive shaped like a shoebox on a stick.
events.description: New explosive type received from the boxes; Carl plans to test them in his bomber's studio first.
events.evidence: new bomb type appears

events.effects: Skill potion: drinking it expands the 10-space hotlist into 20 spaces.
events.description: Skill potion from the Gold Payload Box (WWII gravity-bomb shaped); not drunk this chapter.
events.evidence: Gold Payload Box opens with fanfare

events.effects: Carries a quadruped buckled to Carl's chest; while worn, the passenger transforms along with Carl's Phase or Oozy spells.
events.description: A pink chest-mounted carrier from a Gold Adventurer Box, intended for carrying Donut; she refuses to use it.
events.evidence: pink BabyBjorn-style item from gold box

events.description: The legendary Naga fan box (cobra-head shaped, earned before executing Rishi) dumps a ceiling-high mountain of fire-damaged garbage from Rishi's home into the safe room: wrecked furniture, clothes, electronics, a GreaserBall participation trophy, and bootleg Princess Donut makeup palettes. The AI says most is junk but a useful thing or two may be hidden inside.
events.evidence: fan box floods safe room with junk

events.text: Carl pulls the white cleaner bot (salvaged from Architect Houston's operating theater) out of his inventory and hands it to Mordecai, who resets the old-model unit so it will work for their team.
events.evidence: Carl shoves white cleaner bot at Mordecai

events.text: Donut's pet accessories are fitted in the garage: Dong helps Mongo put on the Nemes of Pestilence, and Donut produces the headband (Wrap of Sekhmet's Compassion) for an excited Rend.
events.evidence: hat and headband put on pets in garage

events.text: System messages announce entry into Hungry Eyes Village and their Team Roundabout, where safe room rules apply. Their garage door is painted with a giant '7', which Carl takes to mean they placed seventh in their race heat.
events.evidence: entering Hungry Eyes Village system messages

events.description: Radoslav tosses Carl a peach-flavored beer from the bugbears' magically refilling cooler; Carl declines to drink it but keeps the can in his inventory.
events.evidence: Carl pockets unwanted peach beer

events.description: Radoslav hands Carl a regular Busch Light from the never-ending cooler.
events.evidence: second beer tossed to Carl

events.cause: Carl cracks open and drinks the regular beer.
events.evidence: Carl drinks the beer

events.text: The rival racing team The Wild Hunt (three razor fox ninjas) performs a form-magic team casting of the spell Size Up on everyone present, so they now know Carl's and Donut's capabilities.
events.evidence: Chiyome explains the somersault routine

events.text: Mordecai advises that the womantaurs' domination magic saps energy or mana like a charm; Carl's Mind Balance keeps him immune, and Donut's high charm should keep her safe.
events.evidence: chat with Mordecai about domination magic

events.text: New quest received: 'I have dreamed thee too long' (part one of two). Part one: reunite NPC Dong Quixote with his best friend, Corcunda (Corky), located on this floor. Part two will require finding Corcunda's second half, nicknamed Porky, plus one of several wandering fleshmancers to reglue them. Reward for completion: two stand-ins of the crawlers' choosing will be sent to the Arena on the 11th floor.
events.evidence: quest popup after spotting the half-mantaur

events.item: trucker hat ('I'd Hang a Little with Chad')
events.description: A cheap green-and-yellow trucker hat bought from a Hungry Eyes hat vendor; Donut wants it for her mood wall.
events.evidence: Donut returns from hat vendor with trucker hat

events.description: A bottle of beer Imani slides over to Carl at the Lollipop bar table.
events.evidence: Imani slides a beer to Carl at table

events.description: A bowl containing a Shirley Temple with extra cherries that Imani pushes toward Donut.
events.evidence: Imani pushes bowl toward Donut

events.cause: Donut drinks it and chews the cherries during the bar conversation
events.evidence: Donut chews a cherry while talking

events.text: The Enriched Pet Biscuit's system description confirms Donut's origin: she is the result of an enhanced pet biscuit that granted the 'Enhanced Growth' benefit, under the Party Companion subtree, tree path one, which kept her fully a cat; her benefit also came with an enhanced memory.
events.evidence: Paramour biscuit description recaps Donut's biscuit result

events.text: Carl states in conversation that Donut has the Love Vampire skill, which makes mobs fall in love with her and die for her, and that her build is charisma- and charm-based.
events.evidence: Carl argues with Donut about Love Vampire

events.description: An unspecified device Donut confirms she picked up from Hedy before the race; its function is not described in this chapter.
events.evidence: Carl asks if Donut got Hedy's device

events.effects: Lever rig attached to the steering wheel letting Donut steer by rocking back and forth, with sticks that press the pedals.
events.description: A janky cat-driving apparatus built by the team's mechanic over a few hours; it now sits in Donut's inventory awaiting refinement by Carl.
events.evidence: mechanic finishes drive system, stored in Donut's inventory

events.text: The chicken truck's freezer has a never-ending supply of frozen chicken patties, French fries, and tater tots; Donut stores removed frozen food in her inventory, and the freezer restocks itself about ten seconds after each removal.
events.evidence: Donut empties self-restocking freezer into inventory

events.text: Donut's Scorpion's Bite weapon mount forms on her back holding Katia's crossbow, auto-loading a glowing Danforth bolt that roots its target in place; the party has only ten Danforth bolts.
events.evidence: crossbow readied on start grid, ten bolts remark

events.cause: Carl hurls it to shatter the chicken truck's entire windshield at the race start
events.evidence: Carl throws banger sphere through windshield at green light

events.text: System notification: Carl discovered an Upgrade Engineer (man in kangaroo suit inside boss pouch); at least one engineer appears each race and engineers can be added to the garage.
events.evidence: examining man in boss pouch, engineer discovery popup

events.cause: Read four scrolls to summon gremlin decoys for the Prison Pocket boss fight
events.evidence: reading gremlin scrolls before boss assault

events.cause: Read scrolls alongside Carl; eight gremlins summoned in total
events.evidence: reading gremlin scrolls before boss assault

events.cause: Summoned three more gremlins mid-fight at Carl's request
events.evidence: Donut summons more gremlins during fight

events.effects: Fills a costume slot as an outer garment; grants Protection from Seeping Acid and a level 10 Jump skill; 12-hour timed item
events.description: The skin of a dead joey from the boss's pouch, wearable as a full-body kangaroo costume, pulled off the engineer's corpse that Genesis retrieved.
events.evidence: stripping joey skin from dead engineer Niels
Carl pulls on kangaroo skin, head clicks

events.text: An unnamed achievement flashed when Carl locked the kangaroo costume on; details not shown in this chapter.
events.evidence: achievement flash as costume head clicks

events.text: System warnings: Carl's boxer effects and pedicure effects are negated while wearing the costume.
events.evidence: warnings after donning kangaroo costume

events.cause: Pulled from inventory into the costume paw but immediately rolled away
events.evidence: sphere rolls out of kangaroo paw

events.cause: Dropped inside Prison Pocket's pouch and detonated, killing the province boss
events.evidence: satchels released in pouch, detonator hit

events.text: Multiple achievements and a level-up notification appeared after the boss kill, but Carl dismissed them unread; details follow in the next chapter.
events.evidence: notifications waved away after explosion

events.text: System warning confirmed the kangaroo costume is a timed item and cannot be removed early.
events.evidence: warning when trying to remove costume
Carl tells Mordecai his costume skill level

events.cause: Leveled up three times after being credited with 95% of the damage to province boss Prison Pocket
events.evidence: post-race recap of boss kill credit

events.description: Boss-kill loot box awarded to each of Carl and Donut for killing Prison Pocket.
events.evidence: boss box reward after Prison Pocket kill

events.text: Carl received a half dozen achievements from the race and boss kill; names not shown in this chapter.
events.evidence: post-race summary of achievements

events.text: Team finished the heat in last (seventh) place, earning a mandatory audience-vote upgrade for their truck; the vote ended with a result said to better protect Donut.
events.evidence: Hedy announces upgrade vote ended

events.text: The audience-vote upgrade from the last race is revealed as a 'princess throne seat' being installed in the truck for Donut.
events.evidence: Hedy mentions installing princess throne seat

events.effects: Carries a hidden message readable only via Carl's Escape Plan skill
events.description: A blank shop invoice handed over by a generated gremlin, stating the C&W&U shop opens after the third heat; its hidden message from Akuma demands Carl kill two teams next heat and offers smuggling friends to the Pineapple Cabaret.
events.evidence: gremlin hands Carl warm folded note

events.text: Carl's Escape Plan skill activated on the gremlin note, revealing a secret message from Akuma, the War Mage Rebellion leader.
events.evidence: hidden words form on invoice note

events.text: Quest Complete: 'I have dreamed thee too long' (part one of two) — Dong and Corcunda learning the other lives counted as reuniting them. Reward withheld until part two.
events.evidence: quest complete popup after Dong hears Corcunda

events.text: New Quest: 'Half a prayer, half a song' (part two of two) — reunite Corcunda's two halves (Corky and Porky) using a fleshmancer, freeing Corcunda from the Lady Dominators team. Fails if Dong dies first. Reward: two stand-ins of their choosing summoned to the arena on the 11th floor.
events.evidence: new quest popup following quest completion
new suit-granted skill hits level 5
system message, Fursona level 8, yiffing unlocked

events.name: Last Place
events.description: Awarded for the team finishing the heat in last place; Donut grumbled about it.
events.evidence: Donut opening loot boxes at bar counter

events.text: Loot boxes yielded multiple additional Emergency Gremlin scrolls and little else of value; exact quantities not stated.
events.evidence: box-opening session in safe room

events.name: Weaseler!
events.description: Earned for wearing an enemy's corpse to fool another creature and then entering that creature.
events.reward: Temporary classes while wearing corpse suits unlocked, granting the suit's associated benefits and buffs until removal; current temporary class: Fursuiter
events.evidence: first of Carl's boss-fight achievements

events.name: Slip-'n'-slide
events.description: Earned for sliding into the boss's pouch; the AI gave only innuendo instead of a description.
events.evidence: second achievement, for entering pouch

events.name: Brotein!
events.description: Earned for touching a male boss's nipple mid-battle; the AI declined to reward it.
events.evidence: third unhinged achievement in the batch

events.name: '76 Buccaneers
events.description: Earned for surviving a heat while finishing in last place.
events.reward: A job application for a Big Shot Chicken location in Tucson, Arizona
events.evidence: achievement mocking last-place finish

events.description: A non-magical, bent, half-shredded, stained fast-food job application received as the '76 Buccaneers achievement reward.
events.evidence: reward from last-place achievement

events.effects: Expands the 20-space hotlist to 25 spaces and unlocks the Contingency skill for programmable hotlist automations
events.description: An upgrade potion from Carl's Platinum Boss Box that enlarges his hotlist and adds automation capability.
events.evidence: contents of Platinum Boss Box

events.cause: Drunk manually right after opening the boss box
events.evidence: Carl drinks potion after messaging Mordecai
new Hotlist contingencies tab appears

events.text: New interface tab 'Hotlist contingencies' allows up to ten automations, each able to use multiple hotlist items based on preset conditions.
events.evidence: Carl explores new interface tab

events.text: Carl's pet Rend (the Tummy Acher) was killed by Mordecai's Reaper Gaze in the safe room; system message: pet has died.
events.evidence: Mordecai's reaper form kills Rend

events.text: Carl's pet Rend resurrected after his sixth death, retaining memories and identity, becoming 25% stronger, and gaining a new random weakness: potatoes. Full weakness list shown: sylvan crystal, consuming hobgoblin sweat, the bite of a rivenwing, consuming undead flesh, Reaper Gaze, potatoes.
events.evidence: pet resurrection system message after Rend rises

events.text: Rosetta hired two level 25 Jikininki ghoul mercenaries for the team, the only mercenaries left available.
events.evidence: ghouls arrive at safe room door

events.text: Donut states her War Crime spell cannot be cast again until the twelfth floor.
events.evidence: Donut answers goat's War Crime question

events.item: Smart Companion Royal Pet Throne, Ultimate Diamond Pampered Pet Princess edition
events.effects: Gyro-stabilizing pet seat that protects its assigned princess in crashes, monitors vitals, offers stress relief, feeds snacks on demand, and talks (Donut named it Dorota).
events.description: A talking pet car seat, a real commercial product in the wider universe, installed in the food truck's passenger seat for Donut; it manifests a fluffy bed when its assigned princess sits in it.
events.evidence: new passenger chair Dorota described before heat three
cookbook entry from Luca Di, associated spell

events.cause: Fired all eight from the eight-pack launcher at One Fine Pig and team Sparkles during the race; the missiles coat targets in blinding mucous goo rather than exploding.
events.evidence: fires eight-pack launcher out the back of truck

events.text: Donut casts her Wall of Fire spell, stated to be level 15 but powered up to level 16; it creates a mile-long, hundred-foot-tall wall of flames beside the truck.
events.evidence: wall of fire cast during sugar hermit wave

events.text: Donut casts Temporary Macaroni, newly received from her Banquet Fit for a Princess book; it casts at level 14 but is enhanced to 15, costs only ten mana, can be chained, and at that level she can choose the size, shape, and direction of the giant pasta tube.
events.evidence: giant cannelloni tunnel conjured during race

events.text: System announces the Scavenger's Daughter has been fed and her wrath can be unleashed; Carl notes he must activate it before the race ends or be stuck with it until the next race.
events.evidence: Scavenger's Daughter fed notification mid-race

events.text: Carl activates Daughter's Kiss while riding the truck hood; his empowered kangaroo feet explode the kaiju beetle skull blocking the finish line.
events.evidence: activates Daughter's Kiss before hitting kaiju skull

events.cause: Carl's health scroll contingency automatically activated when he was flayed and broken in the finish-line crash.
events.evidence: health scroll contingency activates during crash

events.cause: Carl drank two Fine Healing potions to heal broken arms, legs, and crushed organs after the crash.
events.evidence: takes two Fine Healing potions after wreck

events.text: The Royal Court of Princess Donut takes first place in Heat Three of the vehicle races; the Wild Hunt and the Jugglers are eliminated.
events.evidence: Heat Three results system message

events.text: System message: Eris enters the realm; Carl's Scavenger's Daughter patch opens her eyes and fills with power. A random-effect notice states a temporary magical effect, item, or aura from someone in Carl's presence (other than himself) will become permanent.
events.evidence: Eris entry system message freezes the world

events.text: Carl grabs Eris's five-sided fate coin mid-flip, selecting the random fifth outcome; the coin dissolves and leaves a slowly spinning swirl-pattern tattoo on his right palm that even the AI cannot identify.
events.evidence: grabs flipping coin, swirl tattoo appears on palm

events.text: Eris leaves the realm and the Scavenger's Daughter closes her eyes. Eris's random permanence effect resolves on Imani, not Carl or Donut: her Legendary card summon Jacobus the Reverse Tooth Fairy becomes permanently summoned due to the effect of Carl's Scavenger's Daughter patch.
events.evidence: Eris departs; Imani's fairy card made permanent

events.description: A message from Akuma scrawled in black crayon on a kiddie menu, looted from the dead upgrade imp's counter, telling Carl he was supposed to come alone.
events.evidence: loots note from dead upgrade imp

events.effects: A trap module Carl deactivated and collected from the hidden door behind the poster.
events.description: The hidden disintegration trap that had been rigged to the secret door in the upgrade room; Carl disarmed and looted it.
events.evidence: deactivates and loots trap behind yak poster

events.text: Carl drinks a beer at the Lollipop bar and receives a notification granting a temporary 2% constitution buff for the next race, as Tipid had vouched.
events.evidence: beer at Lollipop bar, constitution buff notification

events.text: Team truck gained three new upgrades: an anti-flip gyro, a Bubble Buddy knockoff shield (electrifiable, upgradeable to a floating golden edition), and resizable all-terrain tires.
events.evidence: three new dash lights before race start

events.text: Survival containment bubble applied to the team vehicle for this heat: indestructible, protects against toxic air, but is lost if the vehicle is destroyed; winning team of each race now receives a key to a defeated team's garage.
events.evidence: Zev's loudspeaker announcement before race

events.text: Carl's Mind Balance skill automatically negated the Soul Suck cast by the cannibal chieftain Great Rusty; Donut is stated to be immune via her high constitution-and-charisma combination.
events.evidence: chieftain chants Soul Suck, system negation message

events.text: Donut's Astral Paw spell is much stronger than before but with shorter duration and an 8-hour cooldown; she can combine it with Bijanbi and cast both at once; her War Crime spell cannot be cast again until the twelfth floor.
events.evidence: after sweeping cannibals off the arch bridge

events.text: Received mandatory quest 'Pasty Inferno' after killing Great Rusty: find and kill Maurice, chieftain of the northern cannibal tribe. Reward is the contents of Maurice's cook pot; the race cannot be finished until Maurice is dead.
events.evidence: quest popup after running over Great Rusty

events.text: Donut cast Fresh into Salt on the piranha river; the text states she received this spell from her Champion of Nekhebit title.
events.evidence: salting river to kill piranhas

events.text: All three of the team's hired mercenaries — Finley, Olga, and Quemada — were killed when run over by One Fine Pig's APV, confirmed by system notifications.
events.evidence: mercenaries run down outside garage, kill notifications

events.text: At Carl's request, Donut collected the corpses of Finley, Olga, and the dead dromedarian camel into her inventory.
events.evidence: Donut takes corpses from street after rampage

events.text: Donut can now cast All Eyes on Me with spoken words instead of a song because her charisma is so high; she charmed both Tigrans this way.

events.cause: Carl deployed two hidden disintegration traps from inventory beside the Tigrans' truck, programmed to trigger only on Tigrans
events.evidence: trap-laying while Donut distracts the Tigrans

events.effects: Drinking about half a potion bottle's worth grants roughly 30 minutes of protection against hostile environments/toxic air.

events.evidence: Louis brings bucket he purchased for Carl

events.text: Both of Carl's disintegration traps activated, dissolving the two Tigran racers; Team One Fine Pig was eliminated from the race (five teams remain). No notification appeared for Penelope the pig.
events.evidence: trap activation and elimination notifications

events.text: Random track drawing moved the team from the jungle track to Satan's Water Park for the race's second half; unfinished quests transfer to a mirrored version on the new track.
events.evidence: Zev's twist announcement and heat roll

events.text: Donut's Second Chance spell is at level 11, raised to an effective level 12 by her Brain Trust skill; it animates a corpse for a maximum of fifteen minutes.
events.evidence: resurrected Olga corpse driving the truck

events.text: The 'Pasty Inferno' quest carried over to the water park track: Maurice must still be found and killed; the gingers there are called 'season pass holders'.
events.evidence: map update after entering water park track

events.text: Carl states his spare corpse inventory: most good corpses used up on the last floor, but he still has a camel, a couple of NPCs from Faction Wars, and a large number of dead orcs (mostly headless) lent by Elle.

Book of Boom entry from crawler Mehmet Or

events.cause: dropped out the window and detonated on axe-wielding gingers on inner tubes

events.item: Mehmet's page from Carl's Book of Boom
events.cause: eaten to acquire the Gutter Bumper spell, which Carl then cast to guide Donut's Ice Slick along the river (extending it by an additional 50%)

events.cause: fashioned into a dragged 'brush hog' behind the truck to shred frozen gingers, then cut loose and abandoned after the river
events.evidence: weapon pile kicked out the back doors

events.text: Quest 'Pasty Inferno' completed when zipper shrews (drawn by Donut's Summon Ye, Vermin) killed Maurice; the quest reward (an engineer) was destroyed in the process, so no reward was received.

events.effects: Cursed with 'Michale Graves's disease'; Donut declares it worthless.
events.description: The small, cracked racing helmet of Lucienne of team Sparkles, fished out of the gore in the truck after she was killed at the finish line.
events.evidence: Donut loots helmet from truck floor

events.text: Heat Four results posted: The Royal Court of Princess Donut finished in first place; One Fine Pig was eliminated. Per the earlier rule, winning the race entitles the team to a key to a defeated team's garage.
events.evidence: Heat Four results notification at chapter end

events.text: After winning the race heat, the team holds two Golden vehicle upgrades plus a key that opens any of the abandoned houses in their cul-de-sac.
events.evidence: Hedy reviews prizes after the race win

events.text: Vehicle upgrades chosen: full Golden-version Bubble Buddy shield (protects against molten steel, floats), a heat sink, regular booster rockets, and the Golden upgrade Roller Limbo, a trans-dimensional ability to race upside down and slip through gaps of at least ten centimeters.
events.evidence: upgrade selection discussion with Hedy

events.text: Carl uses the race-prize key to open the abandoned One Fine Pig garage and takes in Penelope the pig, who follows them home to live with the team.
events.evidence: opening abandoned garage, pig follows them out

events.text: Ownership of the hired mercenary fleshmancer Grigori the Placid is transferred to Carl and Donut's guild.
events.evidence: mercenary ownership transferred at the Lollipop

events.cause: Drunk before the karaoke guild attempt; grants the Honeyed (singing control), Yo-Yo Ma'd (perfect pitch), and Dio'd (extended vocal hold) buffs. Made by Mordecai using a new potion-compounding technique to bypass cooldowns.
events.evidence: Carl drinks singing potion before duet

events.cause: Activated onstage to let Carl hit notes above his natural register; grants the Choired buff and makes his voice squeaky.
events.evidence: scroll activated just before singing Take on Me

events.text: Carl is granted permanent access to the Stage Performance Guild after passing the karaoke entrance exam.
events.evidence: system message after finishing the song

events.name: Sing for your supper!
events.description: Awarded for successfully performing a song and completing a task-based guild's entrance exam.
events.reward: Hidden guilds with entrance requirements now appear on Carl's map when he is in close proximity.
events.evidence: achievement popup after karaoke performance

events.text: Donut fails her performance gauge (a FAILED sign appears over her head after the kappa crashes into her on her final line) and does not gain entrance to the Stage Performance Guild.
events.evidence: FAILED sign over Donut after song ends
Porky rewards Carl for freeing stuck zipper

events.text: System warning: hired mercenaries Splash Zone, Gluteus, and Doctor Bones are no longer on the floor (missing in the Desperado Club) and cannot be contacted, but their daily fees continue to be charged until located.
events.evidence: warning message when Carl messages Splash Zone

events.text: Li Na casts a Deadly Bloom dread trap on Carl's small Home Depot cactus (an item from his Earth-class personal space). The enchanted cactus now serves as a remote status indicator for Li Na: glowing means alive in dungeon, self-activation means she escaped the game, turning to dust means she died, one bloomed flower signals the Pineapple Cabaret route is safe, three flowers signal it is not.
events.evidence: Rosetta presents the trapped cactus indicator

events.cause: With Li Na (inside an XL pet carrier) stored in its utility inventory, the white cleaner bot is listed for sale at the guild shop terminal by Donut so Herot can purchase it and release Li Na inside the Pineapple Cabaret. The interface confirms the item sold.

cookbook entry drawing from crawler Mees Koning 2

events.text: Heat Five race results: The Royal Court of Princess Donut finishes in fourth place, crossing the line with fifteen seconds to spare.

events.name: Quest Complete! Half a prayer, half a song.
events.description: Completed the quest by reuniting the two half-mantaur halves (Corky and Porky) into one being before Dong died, despite the AI making the quest extremely difficult.
events.reward: May choose two creatures/people within the AI's sphere of influence to bring to the 11th-floor arena that appears at the end of the Parade of Horribles; the choice must be made once inside the arena.
events.evidence: quest complete notification read in garage after race

events.effects: Grants the drinker's vehicle or mount a 25% constitution buff; the system classifies it as a potion, so it counts against potion cooldowns.

events.effects: Grants the drinker's vehicle or mount a 25% constitution buff; system-labeled as a potion.

events.evidence: before race start, hologram appears on dash

events.text: Mandatory quest 'Ad Infinitum' issued to all tenth-floor crawlers: finish the race. The AI explains the apartments are predictive what-if models; reward for finishing the 6th heat is a participation trophy.

events.evidence: flashback after detonation, cooler sabotage explained

events.cause: Drunk from inventory to gain environmental-hazard protection; system message grants 'Splooged' buff for one hour

events.text: Quest 'Ad Infinitum' completed. The Royal Court of Princess Donut finishes Heat Six in second place; Team Free Love is eliminated.

events.cause: Tossed into the back of Dwight's vine vehicle, but it popped back out and was not recovered

events.text: For the final heat's vehicle upgrade, the team chooses the Golden upgrade version of the truck's existing rockets, giving always-on rockets with better maneuverability and enough power to launch the truck to the roof.

events.text: Carl and Donut collect last-minute food-based buff items in Hungry Eyes before the final heat (specific items not named).

events.cause: Downed before the final heat while watching Osvaldo
events.evidence: in garage before final race start

events.text: Truck outfitted for the final heat with weapons borrowed from other crawlers' vehicles (a potion-delivery shotgun and a hole-punch gun that opens a hole in any shield for three seconds, three uses per race) plus three GPS upgrades: standard heat-position tracking, integrated weapons control, and a Golden-tier cloaking upgrade that boosts processing and hides data from other GPS units.
events.evidence: at starting block, describing new truck systems

events.text: New nine-part quest issued: 'The Nine-Tier Attack. Part one.' Scolopendra has released the first of nine attacks; the first attacks are temporary, each unprotected person has a 20% chance of being affected, at least 30 hours between attacks, and the quest reward is only revealed at the ninth attack.
events.evidence: system message before final heat, three minutes to start

events.text: Donut's personal attack wheel lands on green: she is unaffected by the first Scolopendra attack.
events.evidence: second wheel spins, lands green for Donut

events.text: Carl's wheel lands on red. The first attack ('Beast of Burden Sin') hits him with a level 20 Transformation spell, turning him into a small quadruped (dog-like: he barks and gains keen smell). The effect is temporary, lasting until he passes through a stairwell. Some stats are temporarily altered (values not shown), a 'Quadruped actions' menu is added, and a system warning reveals he is still afflicted with Slugpox.
events.evidence: wheel lands red, transformation system messages
quadruped warning, items moved to inventory

events.text: Carl's transformation form is confirmed: a long-haired dachshund with stubby legs, no thumbs, a high-pitched voice with a German accent, and heightened smell. The system now renders his chat name as 'Karl'. His boxers, kneepads, jacket, cloak, and bandanna remain equipped but resized to the dog form.
events.evidence: Donut announces dachshund in party chat
listing what stayed equipped in dog form
xistera unequipped itself in dog form
gauntlet unequipped along with xistera

item_stats.duration: (5 minutes + 1 minute per level of spell) × [Your Intelligence / (Target's Constitution + Charisma)]

events.effects: Removes the target's ability to use any class-associated skills or abilities for a short time; against a deity it temporarily strips their Worshipper Base Associated skill and spell set. Valid targets: any opponent, NPC, deity, or OI entity.
events.description: Donut's new spell book, an unusual paperback marked as an Advance Reader Copy: it can be installed now but will not function until the 12th floor. She has not yet read or installed it.
events.evidence: Carl examines Donut's spell book at the table

events.text: Both have unopened loot boxes from the previous floor: Carl has several boxes from achievements he considers unremarkable, and Donut has many boxes, mostly Bronze and Silver Adventurer Boxes. Donut also complains she did not gain a single level on the past floor.
events.evidence: box-opening session at safe room table

events.description: A legendary loot box awarded for spotting the shadow mimic before it revealed itself.

events.text: The four-leaf-clover patch grants the Lucky Shot benefit: a fatal blow against the wearer is deflected into a party member's body instead, usable only once per floor. Carl plans to remove the patch as soon as it triggers.

events.effects: Upgrade for Mongo's saddle allowing a cart to be attached to him; the cart adds no weight and Mongo can still jump.

events.item: Gurkha class outfits for mercenaries and pets

events.description: A set of wild clothing Donut received via her new Gurkha class for outfitting her mercenaries, pets, and mounts; the hats resize themselves to fit each wearer.
events.evidence: Gurkha class gear distributed before the parade

events.effects: Foot caps for Mongo that greatly increase his slicing ability.
events.description: Special claw caps fitted to Donut's pet Mongo's feet for the coming fight; part of the Gurkha class gear.
events.evidence: Mongo gets kukri claw caps for his feet

events.text: Donut's own version of the Gurkha hat increases multiple skills by five levels; she keeps it on a pillow unworn because there was no time to drain it into her tiara.
events.evidence: Donut's hat boosts skills by five levels

events.text: Carl formally leaves the Royal Court of Princess Donut: system messages confirm he has left the party, is no longer in a party, and his personal space has been segregated into a single room. He immediately accepts an invitation and joins the party Safehome Yolanda.

events.text: After Carl leaves the party, his Royal Bodyguard title disappears from his tag, and Donut's royal title vanishes as well; Donut finds she cannot add a new title.
